IN THE CHAIR: ROBERTA METSOLA
President

Â 
1. Opening of the sitting
Video of the speeches
Â Â 

(The sitting opened at 09:02)

Â 

2. Negotiations ahead of Parliament's first reading (Rule 72) (action taken)
Video of the speeches
Â 

Â Â President. â In relation to the decision by several committees to enter into interinstitutional negotiations pursuant to RuleÂ 72(1), announced at the opening of the part-session on MondayÂ 6Â October, I have received no request for a vote in Parliament by Members or political groups reaching at least the medium threshold. The committees may therefore start negotiations.

Â 

3. United response to recent Russian violations of the EU Member Statesâ airspace and critical infrastructure (debate)
Video of the speeches
Â 

Â Â President. â The first item is the debate on the Council and Commission statements on a united response to recent Russian violations of the EU Member States' airspace and critical infrastructure (2025/2901(RSP)).

I would like to remind you that we continue with the test of the new format for this debate, so there is no fixed speakers list. After the first round of speakers, on behalf of the groups, Members who receive speaking time or non-attached secretariat from the groups as well will be called to speak and will be reminded of their allotted speaking time. So keep an eye on the screens and then make your way to the lectern.

I'll first give the floor, on behalf of the Council, to MarieÂ Bjerre, Minister for European Affairs, to start the debate.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Marie Bjerre, President-in-Office of the Council. â MadamÂ President, honourable Members, Commissioner, the recent violations of Polish, Romanian and Estonian airspace, as well as hybrid actions, are unacceptable. They show that Europe's defence readiness is urgent. The reckless behaviour by Russia threatens EU citizens, regional stability and international peace.

They will not stop on their own. Our response must be clear, united and resolute. We must send a strong message to Russia: Europe stands together. We defend our security and we continue our support to Ukraine.

We need a truly pan-European mindset because the threats go far beyond the eastern border. In recent months, the EU has already taken strong action to improve its defence readiness. The SAFE instrument is one example. Last week, in Copenhagen, EU leaders were clear in their commitments to work together. They discussed progress on agreed priority areas that include, among others, air and missile defence, artillery, drones and anti-drone systems, military mobility and cyber-resilience.

As stated by President Costa, leaders in Copenhagen broadly supported priority flagships presented by the Commission and the High Representative. This includes the European drone initiative and the Eastern Flank Watch. The leaders provided guidance for the next step. The European Council in October will revert to the Defence Readiness Roadmap. Drones and counter-drone systems are a top priority.

Regarding the Eastern Flank Watch: there was also a meeting of defence ministers from the eastern flank countries on 26Â September that was hosted by Commissioner Kubilius and High Representative Kallas. Following the informal European Council last week, President Costa highlighted the need for increased coordination to keep track of progress and a greater role for defence ministers. The Eastern Flank Watch will be complementary to NATO's initiatives and actions at the eastern border, including the Eastern Sentry, the Baltic Air Policing and the Enhanced Forward Presence forces. EU-NATO cooperation remains essential to close capability gaps and to get European defence to the next level.

Drone incidents around Europe remind us that we are facing threats to our critical infrastructure. Such actions are testing our resilience and unity. EU and NATO must remain firmly and closely coordinated, both in our operational response and in our strategic communication. A coherent, clear, united stance is the answer to assuring our citizens that their security is being protected.

Russia is the most significant and direct threat to European and transatlantic security, not only because of its war of aggression against Ukraine, but also due to its systematic use of hybrid threats. Russia's hybrid activity activities continue to increase; our response needs to be adapted accordingly.

Already in October 2024, the EU condemned Russia's intensifying hybrid activity and adopted a new sanctions regime against Russia's actions. We will continue to make use of all available instruments to prevent and respond to Russia's hybrid activities. The EU is getting better prepared and getting more resilient, but we must not stop the course.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Ursula von der Leyen, President of the Commission. â MadamÂ President, MadamÂ Minister, honourable Members, something new and dangerous is happening in our skies. In just the past two weeks, MiG fighters have violated Estonia's airspace and drones have flown over critical sites in Belgium, Poland, Romania, Denmark and Germany. Flights have been grounded, jets scrambled and countermeasures deployed to ensure the safety of our citizens.

Make no mistake, this is part of a worrisome pattern of growing threats. Across our Union, undersea cables have been cut, airports and logistic hubs paralysed by cyberattacks, and elections targeted by malign influence campaigns.

These incidents are calculated to linger in the twilight of deniability. This is not random harassment. It is a coherent and escalating campaign to unsettle our citizens, test our resolve, divide our Union, and weaken our support for Ukraine. It is time to call it by its name: this is hybrid warfare, and we have to take it very seriously.

To those who still doubt, look at what is happening. One incident may be a mistake; two incidents, a coincidence. But three, five, ten â this is a deliberate and targeted grey-zone campaign against Europe. And Europe must respond. We must investigate every incident, and we must not shy away from attributing responsibility, because every square centimetre of our territory must be protected and safe.

Honourable Members, Russia wants to sow division. We have to answer with unity. It was Italian pilots under NATO's Air Policing mission that escorted Russian jets from Estonian skies. Ukrainian experts are sharing frontline expertise to help Member States counter drone incursions. This is solidarity in action. But we must not only react; we must deter, because if we hesitate to act, the grey zone will only expand.

The founding mission of the European Union is to preserve peace. Today, that means having the capacity to deter aggression and provocation. Europe must urgently equip itself with the strategic capacity to respond: one that is ready to address the changing nature of warfare. We must develop and maintain it together, and we must be capable of responding in real time.

The work has already begun. We are seeing the biggest surge of defence spending in the history of the Union. The Readiness 2030 agenda will mobilise up to EURÂ 800Â billion until 2030. Now we need a precise, pan-European plan, coordinated very closely with NATO, on how to move forward. This is the core of the scoping paper we presented to leaders in Copenhagen last week. In two weeks, we will present our Readiness 2030 roadmap that we call 'preserving peace'. This will not only set common objectives, but also very concrete milestones and timelines on the way to 2030, because we all know only what gets measured really gets done.

I want to focus on three points that are central to this work. The first point deals specifically with violations of our airspace. Here, I am talking about a new pan-European flagship: the Eastern Flank Watch and the 'drone wall'. The drone wall is our response to the realities of modern warfare. Just think about what happened in Poland. We had to deploy very expensive systems â actually, latest-generation fighter jets â to take down relatively cheap, mass-produced drones. This is absolutely not sustainable. We need an anti-drone system that is affordable and fit for purpose for swift detection, swift interception and, when needed, swift neutralisation. We have a lot to learn from Ukraine in this field. We have to learn a lot from Ukraine on capabilities but, even more importantly, I think, on their ecosystem of rapid innovation. Ukraine is ready to support our efforts.

The drone wall will contribute to the Eastern Flank Watch. This will monitor and protect the skies, seas and ground of our eastern Members. But, get me right, it is not only about our eastern border. At the moment it is our priority, but what we need is a 360Âº approach. This anti-drone system will be a shield for our entire Union, including of course, the southern flank. It should be designed to address a wide spectrum of challenges, from responding to natural disasters to tackling international organised crime, from monitoring weaponised migration to controlling the Russian shadow fleet. The list is long. Autonomous systems are at the centre of 21st-century security and Europe must be at the cutting edge.

Honourable Members, the protection of our people, our skies and our infrastructure requires more than drones. It requires drones, but more than that. This brings me to my second point: critical capabilities. We have already identified nine critical capabilities, from air defence to cyber to electronic warfare. For each of these, we will form collective capability coalitions that are groups of Member States committed to deliver together and fast.

We have seen this lead-nation model deliver before. Just think of the Czech initiative or the Danish-led initiative to provide weapons and ammunition to Ukraine. It was very successful. There also one nation took the lead. Others joined in to place larger orders; industry scaled up and prices went down. We will now expand this across the board. This is fast; this is efficient; and, most importantly of all, it is made in Europe.

My third point: none of this is possible without a strong European defence industry. We need to be able to rely on our own industrial base, specifically in critical times. Today, as we all know, most investment goes outside the European Union. In other words, these are jobs outside Europe. This is absolutely not sustainable.

Our surge in defence spending must also be an engine of growth here in the European Union. I know that partially this is already happening. We see new factories and production lines that are springing up across Europe. That's good. Our defence industry is hiring thousands of skilled workers. That is also very good. We see that the spill-overs are being felt in other industries too. We've seen, time and again, new technologies born in the defence sectors bringing new applications for civilian industries, whether it is from cybersecurity tools to, for example, advanced materials for shipbuilding and aerospace.

Our new defence investment can and must contribute to Europe's competitiveness. It must be based in our Union. That's why at least 65Â % of any project funded under SAFE must be from here, from the European Union. This is European money, and we want to see a return on investment of this European money in jobs here in Europe.

Honourable Members, tackling Russia's hybrid war is not only about traditional defence. It is about software for drones. It is about spare parts for pipelines. It is about rapid cyber-response teams. And it is about public information campaigns to spread awareness. This requires a completely new mindset for all of us. We must be ready to leave our comfort zone. We need to explore new ways of doing things. And, most importantly, we must deter everyone who seeks to harm our territory and our people.

The choice before us is very simple. We can either shy away and watch Russian threats escalate, or we meet them with unity, deterrence and resolve. I know which side we will choose. Thank you very much. Long live Europe.

Â 
Â Â 
Â

Â Â Pierre-Romain Thionnet, au nom du groupe PfE. â Madame la PrÃ©sidente, Monsieur le Commissaire, vous le savez, nous sommes fermement attachÃ©s Ã  la souverainetÃ© des Ãtats, au respect de l'intÃ©gritÃ© territoriale et au caractÃ¨re inviolable des frontiÃ¨res. Nous ne sommes pas en guerre avec la Russie, et c'est tant mieux. Mais nous ne sommes pas en paix non plus. C'est dans cette zone grise, cette Â«paix-guerreÂ», oÃ¹ nous nous trouvons avec la Russie, oÃ¹ toute incitation inutile Ã  commettre l'irrÃ©parable est Ã  proscrire, mais oÃ¹ il faut se garder d'Ãªtre tÃ©tanisÃ© par les menaces de guerre totale brandies par Moscou pour nous empÃªcher d'agir.

UkraiÅcy majÄ peÅne prawo atakowaÄ zarÃ³wno rosyjskie rafinerie, jak i sieci przemysÅowe i przesyÅowe. A nam pozostaje ich wspieraÄ, a nie za to karaÄ.

Â 

Â Â ValÃ©rie Hayer, au nom du groupe Renew. â Madame la PrÃ©sidente, Madame la ministre, chers collÃ¨gues, nous sommes Ã  un moment de vÃ©ritÃ© pour l'Union europÃ©enne. Nous ne sommes plus en paix. Nous ne sommes pas en guerre non plus. Nous sommes dans cet entre-deux qui s'appelle la guerre hybride.

Chaque jour, la Russie mÃ¨ne contre nous des ingÃ©rences, des cyberattaques, des sabotages. Elle nous survole avec des drones et des avions de chasse. Ces menaces, nous les connaissons. Je ne vous apprends rien. Mais nos rÃ©actions ne sont toujours pas Ã  la hauteur.

Chers collÃ¨gues, nous devons assumer la confrontation avec la Russie. Nous devons monter d'un cran dans notre rÃ©ponse âÂ je dirais mÃªme, dans notre riposte. Car, chers collÃ¨gues, si nous voulons la paix, Poutine ne comprend qu'une chose: la force.Â Si nous voulons Ãªtre libres, il faut Ãªtre craints. Et pour Ãªtre craints, il faut Ãªtre puissants.

Ãtre puissants, c'est augmenter les budgets consacrÃ©s Ã  la dÃ©fense de nos Ãtats membres. Ils se sont engagÃ©s Ã  le faire dans un temps record d'iciÂ 2030. Mais je le demande au Conseil: Madame la ministre, quand sortirons-nous pleinement de nos dÃ©pendances? Nous devons avoir la maÃ®trise de la fabrication et de l'usage de nos armes pour enfin poser les fondations d'une souverainetÃ© europÃ©enne sur la dÃ©fense. Ãtre puissants, c'est accroÃ®tre nos investissements dans les infrastructures pour le dÃ©ploiement de matÃ©riel militaire. Ãtre puissants, c'est Ã©riger un bouclier aÃ©rien au dessus de l'Union europÃ©enne et de l'Ukraine. C'est renforcer nos capacitÃ©s de dÃ©fense aÃ©rienne et la surveillance du flanc oriental. Ãtre puissants, c'est Ãªtre capable d'abattre tout drone qui porterait un danger immÃ©diat contre nous. C'est aussi sanctionner ViktorÂ OrbÃ¡n qui envoie des drones hongrois dans le ciel ukrainien. C'est scandaleux!

Ãtre puissants, c'est utiliser la valeur totale des avoirs russes gelÃ©s pour soutenir l'Ukraine. Je m'adresse aux Ukrainiens, aux Moldaves, aux Estoniens, aux Polonais, ou encore aux Roumains. Que vous apparteniez ou non Ã  l'Union europÃ©enne, vous n'Ãªtes pas seuls, parce que votre sÃ©curitÃ© est la sÃ©curitÃ© de tous les EuropÃ©ens. Je m'adresse aussi Ã  ceux qui prÃ©tendent que nous exagÃ©rons et que nous sommes des va-t-en-guerre. Les menaces nous concernent tous, tous nos pays membres. De Madrid Ã  Narva, de Dublin Ã  GoÅdap.

C'est pour cette raison que la Russie de Poutine doit avoir une certitude: qu'elle cesse les provocations et sa guerre hybride contre nous. Car nous sommes plus forts qu'elle. Bien plus forts.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Terry Reintke, on behalf of the Verts/ALE Group. â Madam President, dear Commission President, dear Minister, dear colleagues, the European Union is built on solidarity, so first and foremost, I want to say that we stand with all the people who are at the moment feeling the Russian threat the most. These are the people living in the frontline states, in the Baltics, in Poland and Finland. It has to be clear â when one of us gets attacked, we all get attacked, and we will be at your side in these difficult times.

For that, we need European strength right now, because Putin will not succeed in weakening Europe by spreading fear. That's exactly the aim of all the things that are happening right now. This will only forge a stronger solidarity between European citizens. We will resist this threat together. This means that rightfully, at the eastern flank, we need a drone defence network, and across all of the European Union, we need better protection against drones, whether it's at our airports, power plants or other critical infrastructure.

This means we finally have to phase out dirty Russian energy imports. In AugustÂ 2025 alone, EU Member States still spent over EURÂ 1Â billion on Russian fossil fuels. Colleagues, this has to stop â they are using this money to wage war against Ukraine and threaten the rest of the European Union. This means it is not European citizens that should pay for Putin's war, but Putin himself. It is high time that we make full use of the frozen Russian assets â make Putin pay for the damage he has done in Ukraine and all of Europe.

Finally, colleagues, Ukraine belongs in the European Union. In the past years, it has become more than crystal clear that there is actually no place in Europe where people long for our vision of freedom and democracy more than in Ukraine. So let's make it happen as soon as possible. I want Ukrainian colleagues to sit here in this chamber and to discuss and fight with us for a stronger Europe together.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Danilo Della Valle, a nome del gruppo The Left. â Signora Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, qualche giorno fa abbiamo scoperto che anche l'ex cancelliera Merkel Ã¨ una pericolosa putiniana, perchÃ© pubblicamente ha sostenuto che Polonia ed Estonia hanno impedito il dialogo con la Russia prima dell'invasione del 2022, confermando quello che sosteniamo di fatto da sempre, vale a dire che l'Unione europea Ã¨ ostaggio di una leadership pericolante e morente, che ci sta spingendo tutti alla terza guerra mondiale solo per coprire la propria inadeguatezza.

Classe dirigente - la Sua Presidente - amplificata dai media mainstream che diffondono spesso fake news, come la Russia, con il solo intento di generare paura e panico tra la popolazione, per far passare proposte politiche che non hanno nessun tipo di consenso tra gli elettori, come ad esempio il ReArmEU.

Siamo passati dall'invasione russa di Lisbona, programmata per il 2029, fino ai droni che ormai volano liberi per i cieli di mezza Europa. Eppure, l'attentato russo al Nord Stream era in realtÃ  ucraino. L'attacco di droni russi alla Polonia del 13 settembre Ã¨ stato smentito dalle stesse autoritÃ  polacche e NATO, e anche il sabotaggio del Suo aereo in realtÃ  era una bufala.

Come si dice in Italia, a pensar male spesso ci si indovina, e qui sembra davvero che qualcuno non veda l'ora di far nascere l'incidente, di portarci tutti in guerra e mandare al massacro migliaia di giovani in nome degli interessi di banchieri e lobbisti. Ma mandi i suoi figli in guerra, se ci tiene tanto.

I cittadini europei non credono piÃ¹ alle politiche guerrafondaie, e non certo perchÃ© lo dice Putin, ma perchÃ© sono stanchi delle bugie, dell'austeritÃ , di dover rinunciare a sanitÃ , trasporti e salari degni in cambio di missili e jet.

Io lo so che per voi che avete un'impostazione colonialista e suprematista Ã¨ difficile, ma i popoli europei hanno bisogno di pace e di costruire un mondo multipolare e non certo della guerra.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Milan UhrÃ­k, za skupinu ESN. â Zase tu mÃ¡me ÄalÅ¡iu diskusiu o vojne, o strachu, o zbraniach a o vojenskÃ½ch vÃ½davkoch. Pani LeyenovÃ¡, uvedomujete si vÅ¡ak, prosÃ­m vÃ¡s, akÃ© Å¥aÅ¾kÃ© je pre Ä¾udÃ­ zabezpeÄiÅ¥ si dnes bÃ½vanie, zaplatiÅ¥ vÅ¡etky tie ÃºÄty? AkÃ© Å¥aÅ¾kÃ© je pre mladÃ½ch Ä¾udÃ­ zÃ­skaÅ¥ hypotÃ©ku, aby si mohli zabezpeÄiÅ¥ Å¡tyri steny, ktorÃ© musia splÃ¡caÅ¥ potom celÃ½ Å¾ivot? A EurÃ³pska Ãºnia tieto problÃ©my prakticky vÃ´bec nerieÅ¡i. A ak Ã¡no, tak len pre nejakÃ© marginalizovanÃ© skupiny. A namiesto toho tu neustÃ¡le dookola poÄÃºvame o tom, ako treba vyÄleniÅ¥ stovky miliÃ¡rd eur na vojenskÃ© vÃ½davky na zbrane. PoÄÃºvame tu diskusie o vyvolÃ¡vanÃ­ strachu, ktorÃ© majÃº podobne ako za covidu obhÃ¡jiÅ¥ tie stÃ¡ miliardovÃ© vÃ½davky na bruselskÃ© kÅ¡efty. Pani LeyenovÃ¡, ja vÃ¡m poviem, moÅ¾no pre vÃ¡s novinku, ale EurÃ³pania neoÄakÃ¡vajÃº a nechcÃº od Bruselu rakety a nÃ¡boje, ale mier, prosperitu a hospodÃ¡rsku spoluprÃ¡cu.

Â Â Esteban GonzÃ¡lez Pons (PPE). â SeÃ±ora presidenta, pasÃ© el 27 de septiembre en Kiev, bajo uno de los mayores bombardeos rusos de este aÃ±o âseiscientosÂ drones y cincuentaÂ misilesâ, y entendÃ­ que la guerra de Ucrania para Putin es solo el ensayo para su guerra en Europa. El escudo antidrones enviÃ³ un mensaje de unidad muy claro, pero seamos conscientes de que un dron se construye y gobierna desde cualquier parte, tambiÃ©n desde detrÃ¡s del muro, desde el interior de nuestras propias ciudades.

Mientras nosotros hablamos de los millones que invertiremos en proyectos plurianuales de defensa, en los campos de batalla de Ucrania, los pequeÃ±os drones dominan el frente. Ni siquiera los carros de combate se les resisten. En Ucrania, los ratones hacen huir a los elefantes, pero nosotros seguimos pensando en elefantes. Si Putin nos ataca, no lo harÃ¡ con un misil, sino con un dron sin bandera, casi indetectable, con autonomÃ­a suficiente para salvar largas distancias y explotar en cualquier punto estratÃ©gico, y estarÃ¡ diseÃ±ado no por una gran compaÃ±Ã­a, sino por un grupo pequeÃ±o de estudiantes.

Nosotros lo llamamos guerra hÃ­brida, pero, para Putin, es pura y simplemente guerra. No busca ganar territorio, sino influencia. No quiere reconstruir la URSS, sino el Pacto de Varsovia. En el mundo de Putin, la guerra abierta sustituye a la disuasiÃ³n, y, si nosotros no le mostramos que estamos dispuestos a responder, si no le demostramos que estamos preparados para darle una respuesta, no seremos capaces de disuadirle.

Gracias por su compromiso en este sentido, seÃ±ora presidenta.

Â Â Anders Vistisen (PfE). â Fru formand! I gÃ¥r mÃ¥tte Danmarks statsminister, Mette Frederiksen, pÃ¥ dansk tv erkende, at hun ikke havde gjort nok for at forberede Danmark pÃ¥ hybridtruslen. Det kan ellers vÃ¦re underligt, for den danske statsminister har vÃ¦ret blandt de mest aggressive fortalere for konflikt med Rusland i EU. SÃ¥ hvordan man tre et halv Ã¥r inde i krigen ikke er i stand til at sikre kritisk dansk infrastruktur er mig en gÃ¥de. Vi har alle i Danmark stÃ¥et bag regeringen, nÃ¥r det kom til at donere halvfjerds milliarder kroner til Ukraine. Vi har stÃ¥et bag et bredt flertal i Folketinget, hvor I har fÃ¥et pengene til oprustning. Men sandheden er, at da truslen kom, stod Mette Frederiksen en uge fÃ¸r og talte om at kÃ¸be lang- og mellemdistanceraketter, der eksplicit skulle ramme mÃ¥l i Rusland, mens en drone i KÃ¸benhavns Lufthavn kunne lukke vores kritiske infrastruktur ned. Jeg forstÃ¥r godt, at danskerne er blevet bange, nÃ¥r man ser pÃ¥ den danske regerings krisehÃ¥ndtering. Og den danske regering skylder nu danskerne at fÃ¥ styr pÃ¥ situationen. Stop med at holde bombastiske pressemÃ¸der og sÃ¸rg for vores sikkerhed, sÃ¦rligt pÃ¥ hybridfronten, hvor et enkelt topmÃ¸de i KÃ¸benhavn nu krÃ¦ver hjÃ¦lp fra 10 europÃ¦iske NATO-allierede for at kunne gennemfÃ¸res i sikkerhed.

And to you, Ursula von der Leyen, you are very happy to say that everyone who disagrees with you is a Putinist. But sometimes action speaks louder than words. So let's look at your historical actions: you were part of Angela Merkel's government. Not only part; you were Minister of Defence. You left Germany, NATO, defenceless in that period. More than that, you were an active part of moving away from nuclear power and into buying Russian gas and oil in the biggest energy consumer in Europe, Germany.

So to stand here and lecture those of us who believed in a strong defence, who tried to avoid us taking the peace dividend when you didn't see Russia as a threat is simply historical false narrative.

In the Danish People's Party, we fought against leaving submarines in Denmark. We fought against being defenceless when it came to our territorial defence. We fought in favour of the National Guard, in favour of conscription, when your allies in Denmark wanted to give it all up, and therefore Denmark is marginally better placed now to defend ourselves, even though we are falling hopelessly behind.

But please face up to your action, instead of blaming everyone else.

Â 
Â Â 
Â Â 

VORSITZ: KATARINA BARLEY
VizeprÃ¤sidentin

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Alberico Gambino (ECR). â Signora Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, ogni violazione dei cieli dei nostri alleati, ogni sabotaggio a gasdotti e ai porti rappresenta una minaccia alla nostra sicurezza comune. Serve sangue freddo per evitare le provocazioni, ma servono anche regole chiare per difendere i nostri confini e le nostre infrastrutture critiche.

L'Italia sostiene la creazione di uno scudo anti-drone europeo, integrato con la NATO e promosso dal ministro Crosetto, per proteggere le nostre reti strategiche. Chiediamo inoltre la piena attuazione delle direttive su resilienza e cybersicurezza e risorse comuni per difendere porti, gasdotti e reti digitali, cosÃ¬ come un'azione congiunta di giustizia e polizia europea contro il sabotaggio e la disinformazione.

Poi condivido in pieno quello che ha detto prima la Presidente von der Leyen, soprattutto rispetto alla problematica che gli investimenti europei per la difesa vengano appunto spesi in Europa per rendere quanto piÃ¹ competitivo e forte il nostro continente. Solo un'Europa unita e rapida nelle decisioni potrÃ  garantire sicurezza e libertÃ  ai suoi concittadini.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Marie-Agnes Strack-Zimmermann (Renew). â Frau PrÃ¤sidentin, meine Damen und Herren! Vor Ã¼ber einem Jahr hat Herr Stoltenberg bereits gesagt: Wir leben nicht im Krieg, aber auch nicht mehr im Frieden. Endlich verstehen die Regierungen in Europa, dass dem so ist. Auch Herr Merz hat das jetzt Ã¶ffentlich verkÃ¼ndet, denn wir mÃ¼ssen den Menschen erklÃ¤ren, wie die Lage ist. Putins Kriegslogik ist offensichtlich. Sein Ziel ist weit Ã¼ber die Ukraine hinaus. Er sagt auch laut, dass er das Baltikum einnehmen will.

Meine Damen und Herren, militÃ¤risch ist Putin nicht erfolgreich, so wie er dachte. Seit Jahren Ã¼berschÃ¤tzen wir ihn militÃ¤risch, aber wir unterschÃ¤tzen seine BrutalitÃ¤t. Putin will keinen Frieden. Er erÃ¶ffnet viele Fronten, und das schon seit Langem: cyber, hybrid und jetzt auch dieses Drohnenspektakel Ã¼ber Europa mit ungeahnten Folgen.

Meine Damen und Herren, die Folge letztendlich ist ja fÃ¼r uns, nicht nur technisch darauf zu reagieren, sondern dass in den Nationen â und vor allen Dingen in Europa â zusammengearbeitet wird, und eben nicht dieses unendliche Kleinklein: Wer ist fÃ¼r was verantwortlich? Vielleicht kÃ¶nnen wir mal einer Meinung sein Ã¼ber Staaten, Ã¼ber Parteien hinweg, Ã¼ber Rat, Kommission und auch Parlament hinweg, dass wir nur gemeinsam Erfolg haben. Ich weiÃ, dass das nicht einfach ist. Ich weiÃ, dass wir unseren Regierungen erklÃ¤ren mÃ¼ssen, dass dieses kleinkarierte nationale Denken in Zukunft nicht mehr funktioniert. Denn letztendlich werden wir nur gemeinsam erfolgreich sein. Es gibt kein Land in Europa, das in der Lage ist, sich selbst zu verteidigen â kein einziges Land. Es ist eine StÃ¤rke, gemeinsam diesen Schritt nach vorne zu machen.

Meine Damen und Herren, Putin hÃ¶rt uns genau zu. Putin versteht nur die Sprache der StÃ¤rke. Alles andere â und so schÃ¶n es ist, Friedenstauben in den Himmel zu schicken â, all das ist Unsinn. Das Einzige, was er versteht, ist Kraft und vor allen Dingen Einigkeit. Lassen Sie uns diesen Weg gehen. Es lebe Europa!

(Die Rednerin ist damit einverstanden, auf eine Frage nach dem Verfahren der âblauen Karteâ zu antworten.)

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Siegbert Frank Droese (ESN), Frage nach dem Verfahren der âblauen Karteâ. â Sie sind ja Vorsitzende des BarbarossaâAusschusses hier im Haus. Sie werden nicht mÃ¼de, uns sozusagen vorzubereiten auf den Angriff auf Russland. Andererseits stehen Sie der RÃ¼stungsindustrie nahe. Meine Frage ist ganz einfach: KÃ¶nnen Sie uns, kÃ¶nnen Sie dem Parlament, kÃ¶nnen Sie der Ãffentlichkeit mal mitteilen, wie viel Sie von der RÃ¼stungsindustrie jÃ¤hrlich an Provisionen einnehmen, oder kann ich das in irgendeinem Register nachlesen?

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Marie-Agnes Strack-Zimmermann (Renew), Antwort auf eine Frage nach dem Verfahren der âblauen Karteâ. â Das SchÃ¶ne wÃ¤re, wenn Sie lesen kÃ¶nnten. Das ist offensichtlich nicht der Fall. Sie wissen genau, dass Sie ja nur mit Dreck werfen, da ist sonst nichts anderes. Nein, Sie! Sie haben mich gefragt, und ich sage Ihnen etwas: Mit der billigen Tour kommen Sie nicht rum. Sie sind derjenige, der sich hier in dieses Parlament wÃ¤hlen lÃ¤sst, um den Laden hier auseinanderzunehmen. Und ich sage Ihnen was: Das wird Ihnen nicht gelingen!

Â Â

Â Â Villy SÃ¸vndal (Verts/ALE). â Fru formand! I de seneste uger har Ruslands aggressioner overskredet alle hidtidige grÃ¦nser. Vi har vÃ¦ret under et massivt pres fra hybride trusler, men selvfÃ¸lgelig ikke i et omfang, der bare minder om det, ukrainerne og den ukrainske befolkning udsÃ¦ttes for. OgsÃ¥ i Danmark har vi set intimidering og provokationer af et hidtil uset omfang. Blandt andet har droner invaderet vores luftrum.

De her episoder gÃ¸r det ogsÃ¥ klart for mig, at vi alle har et ansvar for vores fÃ¦lles europÃ¦iske sikkerhed. Det, der sker i Danmark, det, der sker i Polen, det, der sker i de baltiske lande og senest i Tyskland er fÃ¦lles europÃ¦iske problemer, som krÃ¦ver fÃ¦lles europÃ¦iske lÃ¸sninger. Jeg er en stor beundrer af det ukrainske folk, som pÃ¥ fjerde Ã¥r stÃ¥r imod den russiske aggression og nu tilmed kommer Europa til undsÃ¦tning. Vi kan lÃ¦re sÃ¥ meget af ukrainerne, og derfor er et tÃ¦t samarbejde med ukrainsk forsvarsindustri helt centralt.

Lad mig gÃ¸re det klart: Ruslands fortsatte aggression overtrÃ¦der international lov. Fra europÃ¦isk side skal vi stÃ¥ sammen, vi skal stÃ¥ sammen med Ukraine, og vi mÃ¥ ikke lade russiske provokationer slÃ¥ tvivl om vores vilje til at forsvare os selv.

(formanden fratog taleren ordet)

Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Ãzlem Demirel (The Left). â Frau PrÃ¤sidentin! âWir brauchen einen Drohnenwallâ, hallt es aus der RÃ¼stungsindustrie. Dies folgt aber der Logik der Eskalation. Liebe Kolleginnen und Kollegen, die gegenseitige hybride KriegsfÃ¼hrung lÃ¤uft schon lange, und sie spitzt sich immer weiter zu. Es wÃ¤re falsch zu behaupten, dass hybride KriegsfÃ¼hrung und Angriffe eine Erfindung nur eines Akteurs â also nur Russlands â wÃ¤ren. Auch die EU greift immer wieder zu solchen Mitteln: Regime-Change-Diskurse, Sabotage, verdeckte MilitÃ¤reinsÃ¤tze, direkte oder indirekte Einflussnahme auf Entwicklungen in anderen LÃ¤ndern â das gehÃ¶rt zum Standardrepertoire aller GroÃmÃ¤chte, allen voran der NATOâStaaten. Missverstehen Sie mich bitte nicht. Jede vorsÃ¤tzliche, potenziell gefÃ¤hrliche Verletzung des Luftraums ist ein klarer VerstoÃ gegen das VÃ¶lkerrecht und muss natÃ¼rlich auch verurteilt werden. Aber die Reaktion darauf sollte verhÃ¤ltnismÃ¤Ãig, evidenzbasiert und deeskalierend sein.

Was wir dringend brauchen, sind vertrauensbildende MaÃnahmen: Verhandlungen und RÃ¼stungskontrollâAbkommen, etwa zum Beispiel die RÃ¼ckkehr der USA und Russlands zum OpenâSkiesâAbkommen. Doch stattdessen erleben wir hier eine hysterische Debatte mit unverhÃ¤ltnismÃ¤Ãigen Forderungen, die Spannungen weiter anheizt und einen heiÃen Krieg wahrscheinlicher macht. Eine Debatte, die WÃ¶rter wie Frieden und Entspannung meidet, ist brandgefÃ¤hrlich, liebe Kolleginnen und Kollegen. Wir mÃ¼ssen nicht kriegstÃ¼chtig sein, sondern wir mÃ¼ssen wieder friedensfÃ¤hig werden. Das ist Sicherheitspolitik. Bitte kommen Sie zurÃ¼ck zu echter Sicherheitspolitik im Interesse der BevÃ¶lkerung.

(Die PrÃ¤sidentin entzieht der Rednerin das Wort.)

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Die PrÃ¤sidentin. â Frau Kollegin Demirel, wenn ich das richtig verstanden habe, haben Sie gesagt, dass die europÃ¤ischen Staaten, dass die EuropÃ¤ische Union Sabotageakte verÃ¼bt. Ich gebe Ihnen keine zusÃ¤tzliche Redezeit, ich wollte nur sagen, dass ich das der PrÃ¤sidentin zur ÃberprÃ¼fung vorlegen werde.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Christine Anderson (ESN). â Frau PrÃ¤sidentin! Sehr schade, dass Frau von der Leyen jetzt, kurz bevor ich ans Podium trete, das Plenum verlassen hat. Vielleicht hatte sie Angst vor mir, ich weiÃ es nicht. Aber nichtsdestotrotz: Wenn das Flugzeug der KommissionsprÃ¤sidentin das GPS-Signal verliert, dann ruft BrÃ¼ssel natÃ¼rlich sofort: âEs war Russlandâ, noch bevor Ã¼berhaupt irgendwelche Daten vorlagen. Heute hÃ¶rt man nichts mehr davon. Warum? Tja, es war nicht Russland. Dann war es wohl eine glatte LÃ¼ge, was die KommissionsprÃ¤sidentin hier vom Stapel gelassen hatÂ â wie so oft.

Nord Stream 2 wird gesprengtÂ â schwerster Sabotageakt Europas. Von der KommissionsprÃ¤sidentin nicht ein SterbenswÃ¶rtchen. Tja, es war wohl nicht Russland. Also keine Untersuchung, keine ErklÃ¤rungÂ â nichts, gar nichts. Ja, Russland greift Europa an, mit Cyberangriffen, Propaganda und dergleichen. Und das muss auch beantwortet werden. Aber gerade jetzt, wo wir glaubwÃ¼rdige Institutionen brÃ¤uchten, inszeniert sich diese Kommission in einem billigen Schmierentheater. Sie schÃ¼rt Angst, schlÃ¤gt falschen Alarm, verspielt das wenige Vertrauen, das mÃ¶glicherweise noch vorhanden ist.

Diese Kommission ist alles, aber sie ist nicht glaubwÃ¼rdigÂ â ihr RÃ¼cktritt lÃ¤ngst Ã¼berfÃ¤llig. Morgen wÃ¤re Ã¼brigens ein sehr guter Tag dafÃ¼r. Aus diesem Grund ist dem Misstrauensantrag gegen Ursula von der Leyen und diese lÃ¤cherliche, inkompetente, verlogene Gurkentruppe unbedingt zuzustimmen. Das muss ein Ende haben.

(Die Rednerin ist damit einverstanden, auf eine Frage nach dem Verfahren der âblauen Karteâ zu antworten.)

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Lukas Sieper (NI), Frage nach dem Verfahren der âblauen Karteâ. â Frau Kollegin, ich bin ein bisschen verwirrt, denn Sie kommen ja von der Fraktion Europa der SouverÃ¤nen Nationen, und Sie plÃ¤dieren ja immer dafÃ¼r, dass sich die EU nicht in die ZustÃ¤ndigkeiten der Mitgliedstaaten einmischt. Aber jetzt sagen Sie, dass die EUâKommission zu einer mÃ¶glichen Straftat auf deutschem Boden bzw. auf deutschem Meeresboden Stellung nehmen soll und sich in ein laufendes Strafverfahren in Deutschland einmischen soll. Also abseits davon, dass, wer auch immer das getan hat, uns damit einen Gefallen getan hat: Wie passt das bei Ihnen zusammen? Das wÃ¼rde ich gerne verstehen.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Christine Anderson (ESN), Antwort auf eine Frage nach dem Verfahren der âblauen Karteâ. â Ja, der Punkt ist ganz einfach: Die EU-Kommission mischt sich in alles ein, und da, wo es vielleicht mal vonnÃ¶ten gewesen wÃ¤re, da herrscht Schweigen im Walde. Und im Ãbrigen kann ich nur sagen: Der politische Tod, der politische Tod dieser EU-Kommission ist zwingende Voraussetzung fÃ¼r Frieden, Freiheit und Sicherheit in Europa. Daran sollte sich Ursula von der Leyen messen lassen. Das tut sie bedauerlicherweise nichtÂ âÂ umso schlimmer. Aber diese Frau muss endlich weg!

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Michael von der Schulenburg (NI). â Frau PrÃ¤sidentin! Was hat uns die dÃ¤nische PrÃ¤sidentschaft bisher gebracht? Wir sind versunken in eine Ã¼berzogene Kriegshysterie, in verhÃ¤rtete Feindbilder, in einen tiefen Russlandhass und in fragwÃ¼rdige Geschichtsbilder. Was wollen wir denn damit erreichen? Das ist doch keine Politik. Wollen wir einen Krieg mit Russland? Wollen wir wirklich riskieren, dass die Ukraine vÃ¶llig zerstÃ¶rt wird? Und wollen wir riskieren, dass dieser Krieg sich auf Europa ausweiten wird, also dass wir uns auch in einem Krieg befinden? Es muss doch dazu eine Alternative geben. Hass ist eben ein schlechter Berater. Die Selbstgerechtigkeit, die hier so viele haben, ist eben das Schmiermittel fÃ¼r Kriege und nicht fÃ¼r Frieden.

Wir mÃ¼ssen umdenken, und wir mÃ¼ssen versuchen, zu verhandeln, wir mÃ¼ssen mit den anderen reden. Das bedeutet auch, dass wir Respekt fÃ¼r unsere Gegner haben mÃ¼ssen. Wir mÃ¼ssen den Respekt entwickeln. Wir mÃ¼ssen versuchen, unseren Gegnern â das bedeutet auch: Russland â zuzuhÃ¶ren. Wir mÃ¼ssen auch versuchen, zu verstehen, warum dieser Krieg ist. Sonst werden wir da keine LÃ¶sung finden. Deswegen ist mein Aufruf an dieses Parlament: Lassen Sie sich nicht von der Kriegshysterie einfangen. Versuchen Sie â das ist die Aufgabe aller Parlamentarier â, endlich fÃ¼r eine friedliche LÃ¶sung zu sorgen.

(Der Redner ist damit einverstanden, auf eine Frage nach dem Verfahren der âblauen Karteâ zu antworten.)

Â Â Virginijus SinkeviÄius (Verts/ALE), blue-card question. â You spoke about diplomacy. So first it was the occupation of Crimea, then it was Luhansk and Donetsk. then Putin invaded the whole of Ukraine. Now he is flying drones over Copenhagen, Brussels, Poland. When is the end of that diplomacy? Trump rolled out the red carpet for him in Alaska. US troops on their knees were rolling out that carpet. Diplomacy doesn't work with Putin. When will you and your colleagues realise that?

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Michael von der Schulenburg (NI), blue-card answer. â I forgot your name, you know, I worked for 34Â years for the UN in war countries, and these countries, in the end, had always negotiated. The biggest war was Iran and Iraq, with 1Â million people dead, where they used chemical weapons and all the types of things. In the end, they had to talk and they found a solution. I think that we have to do this here too â we have to do it to protect us. The UN Charter does not say that you should not use force; it says that we should negotiate in order to prevent the use of force. I think if you ask about this, this is what we have not done in the beginning.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Å½eljana Zovko (PPE). â MadamÂ President, for God's sake, I mean, I think we should first manage these speaking times here in order to manage everything in a geopolitical order.

The recent incursion into European airspace showed that the threat is multifaceted, and that a wide range of means is used to destabilise us. The European Union must respond firmly to these hybrid threats. EU Member States must invest into anti-drone systems to protect their critical infrastructure against the drone attack. This is what Croatia has committed to, along with the participation in the drone coalition, to ensure a stable supply of drones to Ukraine to support production of drones in Europe. Moreover, to counter the Russian threat to our security, I welcome the European Commission proposal to develop European defence projects such as the Eastern Flank Watch. However, in designing this project, we must take into account the full scope of NATO's flank, from north to south, from east to west, MrÂ Commissioner, as we already discussed, and we also must increase cooperation with our American allies â that remains key to the European continent's stability and security.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Tomas TobÃ© (PPE). â Madam President, Europe is under attack. Russian fighter jets and drones are violating our skies. Our critical infrastructure is being targeted.

This should not come as a surprise to anyone â this is straight out of Putin's playbook. For more than a decade, my home country, Sweden, has faced Russian airspace violations. In the Baltic Sea, Russia has targeted critical infrastructure. Putin's violations have become bolder, more reckless, more dangerous.

We have to unite to defend ourselves with real capability and force, strengthen our common defence, invest in tanks and ammunition.Â But it is also obvious, colleagues, that we have to do more to strengthen our capability with drones. This is, frankly speaking, more than a drone shield that we need to invest in.

I, therefore, would like to urge all Member States and the European Commission to seriously consider how to tackle this new Russian threat. This is about protecting our Union and our citizens, and making clear that Europe will never bow down to any aggression.

Â 
Â Â 
Â

Â Â Niels Flemming Hansen (PPE). â Fru formand! Det er jo ikke fÃ¸rste gang, at vi i Europa er igennem en svÃ¦r tid. Â Â Victor Negrescu (S&D). â DoamnÄ preÈedintÄ, domnule comisar, astÄzi Parlamentul European transmite un mesaj clar: Europa nu va rÄmÃ¢ne tÄcutÄ cÃ¢nd dronele ruseÈti Ã®ncalcÄ spaÈiul nostru aerian Èi pun Ã®n pericol infrastructura noastrÄ criticÄ.

RomÃ¢nia, Èara mea, a fost vizatÄ Ã®n mod repetat de dronele ruseÈti, care au Ã®ncÄlcat astfel spaÈiul aerian al Uniunii Europene Èi al NATO. Fragmente au cÄzut pe teritoriul nostru. Â Â Nathalie Loiseau (Renew). â Madame la PrÃ©sidente, mes chers collÃ¨gues, lâEurope nâest plus seulement menacÃ©e aujourdâhui; elle est provoquÃ©e. Ce ne sont plus seulement des attaques hybrides qui nous ciblent. Le survol des espaces aÃ©riens souverains par des avions de chasse ou par des drones militaires nâest pas un acte hybride; câest une violation dÃ©libÃ©rÃ©e de la souverainetÃ©.

Face Ã  la gravitÃ© de la situation, jâexprime ma pleine solidaritÃ© avec tous les Ãtats europÃ©ens qui ont Ã©tÃ© provoquÃ©s Ã  lâEst et au Nord de notre Union. Nous sommes une Union parce que nous sommes solidaires, ne lâoublions pas.

Nâoublions pas non plus que nos populations sont inquiÃ¨tes. Face Ã  la confusion que la Russie essaie de semer, nous devons parler clairement et agir en consÃ©quence. Ã quoi joue la Russie? Elle nous provoque pour essayer de nous intimider et pour dÃ©tourner notre attention de lâUkraine et des lieux quâelle piÃ©tine. En violant nos espaces aÃ©riens, Moscou gonfle ses muscles et essaie de se faire passer pour plus forte quâelle ne lâest. Câest sa faiblesse quâil faut voir en face.

Je fais Ã©galement part dâune certaine perplexitÃ©. Face aux provocations russes, notre rÃ©action collective manque sÃ©rieusement de clartÃ©. LâOTAN peine Ã  dÃ©signer la Russie et Ã  dÃ©noncer le caractÃ¨re intentionnel des violations de nos espaces aÃ©riens. Si, comme nous le pensons tous, Vladimir Poutine nous teste, ce nâest pas une trÃ¨s bonne maniÃ¨re de rÃ©ussir ce test.

Nous peinons Ã©galement Ã  nous protÃ©ger de ses incursions dâavions et de drones. Trois ans. Trois ans aprÃ¨s lâinvasion de lâUkraine, et en dÃ©pit de nos appels pressants Ã  nous prÃ©parer, nous ne sommes donc pas prÃªts. La Commission nous parle aujourdâhui dâun mur de drones, le Conseil se montre rÃ©ticent. Que faisons-nous? Quand? Comment? Combien de temps allons-nous ressembler Ã  des lapins pris dans les phares dâune voiture?

Je terminerai en partageant ma conviction: le meilleur moyen de nous protÃ©ger, de protÃ©ger nos populations contre la menace russe, nous le connaissons, câest dâaider lâUkraine Ã  gagner la guerre et Ã  affaiblir la Russie. Le meilleur moyen de rÃ©pondre Ã  un survol de drones, câest de financer plus de soutien militaire Ã  Kiev. Le meilleur moyen de protÃ©ger notre ciel demain, câest de protÃ©ger le ciel ukrainien aujourdâhui. Nous savons ce que nous avons Ã  faire. Nous ne devons cÃ©der ni Ã  la provocation, ni Ã  lâintimidation, ni Ã  la paralysie.

Â Â Jonas SjÃ¶stedt (The Left). â Fru talman! Â Â Diana Riba i Giner (Verts/ALE). â SeÃ±ora presidenta, las incursiones rusas en el espacio aÃ©reo de Polonia, RumanÃ­a, Estonia y Dinamarca son un nuevo llamado de atenciÃ³n para la UniÃ³n Europea. Estas incursiones no son aisladas. Hay sabotajes, ciberataques y campaÃ±as de desinformaciÃ³n que son parte de una estrategia hÃ­brida, que no solo amenaza a las fronteras fÃ­sicas: quieren poner a prueba a Europa como un proyecto polÃ­tico y democrÃ¡tico.

Y su objetivo es explorar nuestras debilidades, las de nuestras infraestructuras clave y las de nuestras instituciones democrÃ¡ticas. Y poner de manifiesto que, lastimosamente, todavÃ­a no estamos suficientemente preparados. Pero el riesgo es actuar Ãºnicamente desde la urgencia y con medidas dispersas.

Frente a estas amenazas, ninguna respuesta exclusivamente militar serÃ¡ suficiente. Europa necesita algo mÃ¡s que armas. Necesita visiÃ³n comÃºn, mÃ¡s integraciÃ³n polÃ­tica y la convicciÃ³n de proteger los valores que la definen.

Defender a Europa no solo es proteger su cielo, sino tambiÃ©n defender con uÃ±as y dientes el proyecto polÃ­tico y democrÃ¡tico comÃºn.

Â Â

Â Â Andrey Novakov (PPE). â MadamÂ President, dear Commissioner, colleagues, I think the fairy tale is over. The world we built and lived in after 1989 is over. It's not the same and it won't be the same again.

We tore down the Berlin Wall in order to escape from the Iron Curtain. Today, we have to build a new wall â a drone wall â to keep the Iron Curtain away.

Those of us who are born and raised east of Eden can sense this very well, because we lived in this. I think this is not a war, a battle for territories or for resources. Those drone attacks are a battle for a way of life, a battle between two civilisations and two cultures, one of which is not recognising human life as the highest possible value.

This is why we have to defend ourselves and the gap between our perception and reality should disappear. We have to face reality and prepare to be a peaceful pacifist with the capabilities to defend ourselves.

(The speaker agreed to take a blue-card question)

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Alexander Jungbluth (ESN), Frage nach dem Verfahren der âblauen Karteâ. â Sehr geehrter Kollege, ich mÃ¶chte Sie erst mal korrigieren: Es waren nicht irgendwelche EuropÃ¤er, die die Berliner Mauer heruntergerissen haben, sondern es waren die Deutschen, die das getan habenÂ â das zur Einleitung. Aber ich mÃ¶chte Ihnen gerne ein Zitat aus Ihrer Fraktion geben. Ich zitiere: âIch weiÃ nicht, ob es die Ukrainer waren, die Nord Stream gesprengt haben, aber selbst wenn sie es waren, ist es aus meiner Sicht ein legitimes Zielâ. Sie und Ihre Fraktion sagen â es ist ein Zitat Ihres Kollegen Gahler Ã¼brigens â ja auch: Wir brauchen Milliarden, um einer angeblichen Bedrohung Russlands zu entgehen. Auf der anderen Seite sind es Personen wie Herr Gahler, die ja tatsÃ¤chliche Angriffe auf unsere Infrastruktur gutheiÃen. Also ist es die Position der EPP, dass solche Angriffe der Ukraine gutgeheiÃen werden?

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Andrey Novakov (PPE), blue-card answer. â What I am saying is that we have to protect ourselves. The drones were flying over Poland, over Germany â your own country â they were flying over Denmark. That has nothing to do with the pipelines on the bottom of the Baltic Sea.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Dolors Montserrat (PPE). â SeÃ±ora presidenta, la pregunta no es si Rusia estÃ¡ atacando a Europa, la pregunta es cuÃ¡ndo vamos a defendernos como UniÃ³n Europea. Lo que comenzÃ³ como la invasiÃ³n de Ucrania ya es hoy una guerra encubierta contra Europa. Putin estÃ¡ midiendo nuestra paciencia con drones y desinformaciÃ³n, sobrepasando los lÃ­mites para provocarnos y dividirnos.

Es urgente que blindemos nuestra seguridad con un escudo de defensa contra drones enemigos yÂ hackers. Debemos exigir a todos los miembros que cumplan su compromiso para una defensa comÃºn en cooperaciÃ³n con la OTAN, porque pongo un ejemplo: en EspaÃ±a, Pedro SÃ¡nchez estÃ¡ lastrando la defensa europea para contentar a sus socios y mantenerse un dÃ­a mÃ¡s en el poder. Esta irresponsabilidad debe acabar. La respuesta de Europa debe ser tajante, porque el apaciguamiento y la desidia no frenan a dictadores imperialistas.

No mendigaremos la paz, protegeremos la libertad hoy para no vivir maÃ±ana bajo las reglas de quienes la pisotean.

Â 
Â Â

Â Â Tobias Cremer (S&D). â Madam President, dear colleagues, Russian fighter jets are violating NATO airspace, Putin's shadow fleet is sabotaging undersea cables, cyberattacks are shutting down our airports, armies of disinformation bots are interfering in our elections, and swarms of drones are spying on our forces and paralysing our infrastructure.

Colleagues, especially those on the far left and the far right: what more do you need to call this the reality that it is? That Putin is conducting a largeâscale hybrid war against our citizens, and that it is our and your responsibility to protect our citizens. And we know that Putin will continue to escalate as long as he thinks that we are weak, divided and ready to be scared. So let's prove him wrong. Let's keep our cool and show our resolve. Let's build a drone wall and secure our skies. But let's also invest in the resilience of our economies and of our societies, so that we don't go into panic mode every time Putin tries to provoke us.

And let's be smart about our response by striking Putin where it hurts: that is his bank account. So now is exactly the right time to use the frozen Russian assets to support Ukraine and to show the schoolyard bully that while we do not want to escalate, we do have the means to protect freedom and security and our citizens in Europe.

(The speaker agreed to take a blue-card question)

Â 
Â Â 
Â

Czy nie uwaÅ¼a Pan, Å¼e nie jest to wÅaÅciwe postÄpowanie rzÄdu Niemiec?

Â Â Tobias Cremer (S&D), blue-card answer. â It is very clear that the Nord Stream project was a mistake. And it is good that we do not have it anymore. But at the same time I believe in the rule of law, and this is the difference between us and Putin. I trust our courts to prosecute and pursue any sort of crime. The difference is that Putin does not, and Radio Moscow over there does not care about the rule of law. And that makes the difference between Europe, Russia and the far right.

Â

Â Â Julien Leonardelli (PfE). â Madame la PrÃ©sidente, madame vonÂ derÂ Leyen, chers collÃ¨gues, les rÃ©centes intrusions de drones russes dans lâespace europÃ©en doivent nous alerter, mais elles ne doivent pas nous prÃ©cipiter dans une logique dâescalade.

Aujourdâhui, lâUnion europÃ©enne est devenue un jouet dont les grandes puissances mondiales sâamusent. Un pantin qui sâagite sans direction, sans stratÃ©gie, sans profondeur. La vraie autonomie stratÃ©gique ne se dÃ©crÃ¨te pas Ã  Bruxelles. Elle se forge Ã  Paris, Ã  Rome, Ã  Budapest, Ã  Vienne, Ã  Helsinki, dans le respect de la souverainetÃ© des nations libres.

Notre devoir nâest pas de les exposer Ã  lâirrÃ©parable, mais de les protÃ©ger et de prÃ©server la paix. Cela doit Ãªtre notre seule boussole.

Â Â NicolÃ¡s Pascual de la Parte (PPE). â SeÃ±ora presidenta, tras tres aÃ±os y medio de guerra en Ucrania, nos vemos sorprendidos por la incursiÃ³n de drones en nuestro espacio aÃ©reo. Tras tres aÃ±os y medio de guerra en Ucrania. Eso no dice mucho a favor de nuestra capacidad de planificaciÃ³n y de previsiÃ³n.

Rusia estÃ¡ utilizando drones y nosotros solamente podemos oponer carÃ­simos PatriotÂ o el despegue de sofisticados aviones F-35, lo cual es insostenible. Eso significa que tenemos que reaccionar rÃ¡pidamente y en muy poco tiempo.

Y lo primero que tenemos que hacer es proponer, de cara a la prÃ³xima reuniÃ³n de jefes de Estado y de Gobierno del 23 y 24 de octubre que se celebrarÃ¡ aquÃ­, en Bruselas, por parte de la ComisiÃ³n, un plan de contingencia. Un plan de acciÃ³n que prevea una acciÃ³n rÃ¡pida, unificada y eficaz de Europa ante los ataques de Rusia, que, si le ha declarado la guerra a Ucrania, a Europa nos ha declarado una guerra hÃ­brida. Y eso supone desinformaciÃ³n âuna campaÃ±a masiva de desinformaciÃ³nâ, ciberataques y amenazas hÃ­bridas. Y para eso tenemos que estar preparados. Pero no solamente desde el punto de vista militar, sino tambiÃ©n desde el punto de vista de la comunicaciÃ³n y el punto de vista polÃ­tico.

Â¡HagÃ¡moslo!

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Sven Mikser (S&D). â Madam President, dear colleagues, Russian violations of our airspace have recently become more frequent and also more provocative. It's clear that Putin is trying to seek out our vulnerabilities, test our resolve and unity, and create confusion and a sense of vulnerability among our citizens. Our response must be united and resolute. Let me make three brief points.

First, when it comes to incursions by manned military aircraft, we do have â both nationally and through NATO alliance â most of the procedures and protocols in place to deal with these situations. We also collectively have the capabilities to identify, intercept and, if the threat posed by the invading aircraft requires, to take it down. However, we must be ready to bolster our posture and presence, particularly in the easternmost and most vulnerable Member States.

Second, drones are a relatively new threat, but it is a rapidly growing threat. The drone wall is a great concept, but we must acknowledge that in real life it is going to be a very intricate combination of different measures and capabilities, and not all of them are there today. So we must proceed with the sense of utmost urgency.

And finally, Russia is doing what it's doing in large part to distract us from standing with Ukraine and providing all the support Ukraine needs to fight off Russia's criminal war of aggression. We must not be distracted. Indeed, we must increase our support to Ukraine and make sure it prevails in its just fight against the aggressor.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Reinier Van Lanschot (Verts/ALE). â MadamÂ President, how many Russian drones did it take to shut down European skies? About five. How many drones are over Ukraine? About 500 every night. If we face that, we're not prepared at all.

Ukraine learnt the hard way how to defend itself against drones, and they understand you should not shoot down a cheap drone with a million-euro missile. Their technology is cutting-edge and Ukrainian instructors are in the EU right now, training European soldiers.

Putin exploits our weakness and depletes our air defence on his terms. We've let a war criminal drag us into a boxing match and made him the referee. But if we stop being afraid to act, we can respond on our terms. Protect our skies. Build the drone wall. Stop buying Russian gas. Give all the frozen Russian assets and set up a no-fly zone in western Ukraine.

Colleagues, Ukraine is helping us, but we don't say thank you enough. So let me say it: MrÂ Zelenskyy, thank you. And let's back our gratitude up with actions.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Lena DÃ¼pont (PPE). â Frau PrÃ¤sidentin, Herr Kommissar! Dieses Mal Drohnen, letztes Mal Kabel, davor waren es Ã¼ber Nacht verschobene Grenzbojen â das russische playbook ist immer das gleiche. FÃ¼r jedes Land der EU ist etwas dabei, fÃ¼r jeden Teil der Gesellschaft eine Botschaft. Das Ziel: Unruhe, Sorge, Untergraben unserer Resilienz, Spaltung schaffen. Uns muss deswegen klar sein: Angst ist immer ein Indikator fÃ¼r das, was uns wichtig ist, aber sie ist niemals ein guter Ratgeber. Deswegen: Schultern gerade machen, put the money where the mouth is, endlich Verteidigung, Krisenvorsorge und innere Sicherheit konsequent zusammendenken, Verfahren straffen, Beschleunigung vereinfachen, Informationsaustausch und Kooperation zwischen den SicherheitsbehÃ¶rden verstÃ¤rken, unsere BevÃ¶lkerung mitnehmen.

Ich sage gleichzeitig aber auch: die Mitgliedstaaten mitnehmen. Polen, Estland, Lettland, Litauen, Schweden, Finnland hatten und haben recht. Wir haben einen gemeinsamen Gegner, wir brauchen eine gemeinsame Antwort. Genau dafÃ¼r haben wir mal die VertrÃ¤ge geschaffen. SicherheitÂ â am AnfangÂ â voreinander, heute: Sicherheit fÃ¼reinander. DafÃ¼r ist die Beistandsklausel ausgelegt: nicht fÃ¼r gutes WetterÂ â das ist viel zu einfachÂ â, sondern genau fÃ¼r diese Zeit jetzt. SolidaritÃ¤t beweist sich bei schlechtem Wetter. Das ist das, was zÃ¤hlt.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Hans Neuhoff (ESN). â Frau PrÃ¤sidentin, Kolleginnen und Kollegen! Die gefÃ¤hrlichsten Drohnen kreisen nicht am Himmel Ã¼ber Polen, Frankfurt oder Kopenhagen, sondern in unseren KÃ¶pfen. Der hier vorgelegte Aufruf der schwarz-grÃ¼nen Glaubenskrieger zur gemeinsamen Reaktion, zum heiligen Kampf gegen Russland ist dafÃ¼r das erschreckendste Beispiel. Was Sie uns zumuten, ist ein Sammelsurium aus unbewiesenen Behauptungen und wilden Spekulationen. Die Ã¼ber Polen abgeschossenen Drohnen waren unbekannter Herkunft. Russland bot Konsultationen an, Belarus den Austausch von RadardatenÂ â Polen ignorierte beides. Interesse an AufklÃ¤rung â und genau das wÃ¤re das Gebot der Stunde â besteht hier offenbar nicht.

Kolleginnen und Kollegen, die franzÃ¶sische Luftwaffe hat bei den Olympischen Spielen letztes Jahr durchschnittlich sechs Drohnen pro Tag im Raum Paris vom Himmel geholt. Warum bitte holt man die Drohnen nicht auch in Frankfurt und MÃ¼nchen herunter? FÃ¼rchtet man vielleicht das Untersuchungsergebnis? Geht es in Wahrheit vielleicht darum, den BevÃ¶lkerungen der EUâStaaten Angst zu machen? Geht es darum, die notorische Uneinigkeit der EUâStaaten durch ein sorgsam gepflegtes Feindbild zu Ã¼berwinden? Geht es darum, Zustimmung zu organisieren fÃ¼r die gewaltigen AufrÃ¼stungsplÃ¤ne der EU, Herr Kubilius?

Meine Damen und Herren, fÃ¼r Luftraumverletzungen gibt es eingefÃ¼hrte internationale Standards und Prozeduren. SelbstverstÃ¤ndlich mÃ¼ssen Drohnen unbekannter Herkunft Ã¼ber kritischer Infrastruktur abgeschossen werden. Abgeschossen werden mÃ¼ssen aber vor allem die Drohnen in den KÃ¶pfen der verrÃ¼ckt gewordenen EUâEliten. Man muss am Ende dankbar sein, dass dieses Parlament keine operativen Befehle erteilen kann, denn sonst wÃ¤ren wir lÃ¤ngst im dritten Weltkrieg.

Â Â BartÅomiej Sienkiewicz (PPE). â Pani PrzewodniczÄca! Szanowni PaÅstwo! Od lat Rosja prowadzi dziaÅania wobec Zachodu, ktÃ³re nie przypominajÄ tradycyjnej wojny â zamiast czoÅgÃ³w i bomb uÅ¼ywa sabotaÅ¼u, dezinformacji, prowokacji.

Â Â HelÃ©ne Fritzon (S&D). â Fru talman! Hoten mot Europa Ã¶kar. Vi ser drÃ¶nare Ã¶ver mitt grannland Danmark. Vi ser ryska stridsflyg Ã¶ver Baltikum. Det Ã¤r helt oacceptabelt.

Precis dÃ¤rfÃ¶r tog jag, tillsammans med mina nordiska kollegor, initiativ till den hÃ¤r debatten. Vi mÃ¥ste skicka en tydlig signal till Putin om att vi stÃ¥r enade, att vi stÃ¥r upp fÃ¶r vÃ¥ra lÃ¤nder och vÃ¥ra grÃ¤nser och att vi inte accepterar nÃ¥gra former av krÃ¤nkningar.

FÃ¶r Sverige, mitt land, Ã¤r drÃ¶narhoten ocksÃ¥ pÃ¥ riktigt. Gotland ligger ju bara 30 mil frÃ¥n ryska Kaliningrad, och vÃ¥rt lÃ¤ge i ÃstersjÃ¶n gÃ¶r att vi ocksÃ¥ Ã¤r en frontstat. Â Â AngÃ©line Furet (PfE). â Madame la PrÃ©sidente, chers collÃ¨gues, la souverainetÃ© aÃ©rienne fait partie intÃ©grante de la souverainetÃ© nationale. C'est un principe essentiel du droit international, dont nous a parlÃ© tout Ã  l'heure MmeÂ Loiseau, affirmÃ© par l'articleÂ premier de la convention de Chicago deÂ 1944.

Chaque Ãtat exerce un contrÃ´le complet et exclusif sur l'espace aÃ©rien au-dessus de son territoire. Si les incursions constatÃ©es en Estonie, en Pologne ou dans la rÃ©gion Baltique sont avÃ©rÃ©es hostiles, elles constitueront une violation du droit international contraire Ã  l'articleÂ 2, paragrapheÂ 4, de la charte des Nations unies.

Mais l'exigence du droit vaut pour tous. Tant que les preuves ne sont pas rendues publiques, l'Union europÃ©enne doit garder la rigueur et la retenue du droit. Accuser sans dÃ©montrer, rÃ©agir sans vÃ©rifier, c'est risquer de se dÃ©tourner de l'Ã©tat de droit si cher Ã  tous mes collÃ¨gues dans cet hÃ©micycle et Ã  MmeÂ vonÂ derÂ Leyen.

L'Europe doit protÃ©ger ses espaces, ses infrastructures et ses citoyens, mais sans cÃ©der Ã  l'engrenage de la guerre. ProtÃ©ger l'Europe relÃ¨ve de notre responsabilitÃ©. Il ne s'agit pas d'enflammer le monde, mais de le stabiliser. Il ne s'agit pas d'imiter les puissances qui provoquent, mais d'incarner celles qui raisonnent et apaisent.

La fermetÃ© n'exclut pas la luciditÃ©. Et la luciditÃ© aujourd'hui, c'est de dÃ©fendre notre sÃ©curitÃ© dans le respect scrupuleux du droit international.

(L'oratrice accepte une question carton bleu)

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Nathalie Loiseau (Renew), question Â«carton bleuÂ». â Madame Furet, votre parti politique a un privilÃ¨ge, celui dâavoir depuis longtemps des relations privilÃ©giÃ©es avec les autoritÃ©s russes. De nombreux dÃ©placements, de nombreuses relations financiÃ¨res, font que vous avez leur numÃ©ro de tÃ©lÃ©phone.

Si vous pensez que lâapaisement est utileÂ â Â«apaisementÂ» est un drÃ´le de mot si nous connaissons un peu son histoireÂ â, je vous engage Ã  appeler les autoritÃ©s russes et Ã  leur demander de cesser les provocations continues contre nos pays, quâil sâagisse de cyberattaques, de sabotage ou de survols dâavions de chasse russes.

Que faut-il pour vous dessiller les yeux afin que vous voyiez ce que reprÃ©sentent rÃ©ellement cette guerre hybride et cette zone grise, et que vous acceptiez enfin, vous qui vous dites patriote, de protÃ©ger les EuropÃ©ens?

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â AngÃ©line Furet (PfE), rÃ©ponse carton bleu. â Madame Loiseau, je ne sais que dire face Ã  vos diffamations et au cÃ´tÃ© pathÃ©tique de vos accusations. Vous Ãªtes bien consciente que personne chez nous nâa le numÃ©ro dâaucun politicien russe. Par ailleurs, votre parti a longtemps Ã©tÃ© un enfant cachÃ© de lâURSS. Ne soyez pas dans la politique politicienne et gardez le niveau du dÃ©bat, Madame. Pour votre Ã¢ge et pour votre responsabilitÃ©, je trouve cela trÃ¨s dÃ©cevant.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Aurelijus Veryga (ECR). â MadamÂ President, colleagues, Russia is continuously testing Europe's preparedness by sending drones or violating airspace with fighter jets. And what does Russia see? That we are trying to fight back ineffectively using very expensive air defence systems. Only now are we starting to talk about building drone defence systems. This is far too late and we have to rush. When attempts to destroy critical infrastructure in the Baltic Sea started, there was fear to react. The tension of shadow fleet tankers finally demonstrated that the EU countries react not only with words.

Now we have Russian fighters violating EU and NATO airspace. There are examples of NATO members who demonstrated that they take it seriously. This time it might come to EU countries also.

What needs to be done today? If the airspace becomes unsafe, this will become not just a matter of security, but also will seriously affect the economy. Defence would be cheaper. Stay consistent with sanctions against Russia and finally stop financing Russia's war machine. Last but not least, provide needed financial and other support to eastern border countries for the development of air space defence systems.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Riho Terras (PPE). â MadamÂ President, dear colleagues, there is no doubt that Russia is already waging war against Europe. The measures that they use to test us are all part of the Russian toolbox. We have witnessed an increasing number of airspace violations by Russian drones and military aircraft recently.

The way Russia is testing and mapping our response is a clear signal that they are calculating the option of military aggression against the whole of Europe.

Today, Europe still lacks a sense of urgency. The fact that this is an existential threat is not understood completely all over Europe. It is our paramount task to demonstrate unity, resilience, strong resolve and more military capabilities. To achieve this, we must invest more in defence and boost the defence-industrial capacity.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Thomas Pellerin-Carlin (S&D). â Madame la PrÃ©sidente, la Russie a ouvertement dÃ©signÃ© la France comme son premier adversaire en Europe. Elle a fait de la France une de ses cibles prioritaires. Ces mots ont Ã©tÃ© prononcÃ©s par le gÃ©nÃ©ral Burckhardt, le chef d'Ã©tat-major des armÃ©es franÃ§aises, le 11Â juillet dernier. Depuis des annÃ©es, la Russie cherche Ã  affaiblir la France. Elle finance des partis extrÃ©mistes, elle sâattaque aux intÃ©rÃªts franÃ§ais et mÃ¨ne une guerre informationnelle, comme lâillustre le charnier de Gossi.

Au-delÃ  de la France, câest lâUnion europÃ©enne que Vladimir Poutine veut dÃ©truire. Si ces drones violent aujourdâhui notre espace aÃ©rien, câest pour nous tester. Face Ã  la menace russe, nous, EuropÃ©ens, nous devons faire bloc. Mais comment le faire? Ici, dans ce Parlement, des agents prorusses participent au dÃ©bat. Pour leur rÃ©sister, nous devons agir dans les domaines de la dÃ©fense, de la diplomatie et de lâÃ©nergie. Notre honneur et notre responsabilitÃ© sont dâavancer ensemble au sein dâune coalition pro-europÃ©enne, la seule capable de prÃ©server notre libertÃ©.

(L'orateur accepte une question carton bleu)

Â 
Â Â

Â Â Thierry Mariani (PfE), question Â«carton bleuÂ». â Je fais simplement remarquer Ã  notre collÃ¨gue que FranceÂ Info, qui nâest pas une radio dâextrÃªmeÂ droite, a clairement indiquÃ© dans sa revue Le Vrai du Faux que personne en Russie nâavait prononcÃ© cette phrase que le gÃ©nÃ©ral, chef dâÃ©tat-major, a rÃ©pÃ©tÃ©e. Si vous avez la source de cette phrase imaginaire, je vous invite Ã  la communiquer au journaliste de la radio nationale qui, pourtant, ne partage pas nos idÃ©es.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Thomas Pellerin-Carlin (S&D), rÃ©ponse carton bleu. â Monsieur Mariani, vous dÃ©montrez Ã  lâinstant mon propos sur les agents prorusses au sein de ce Parlement. La phrase est celle du gÃ©nÃ©ral Burckhardt, chef dâÃ©tat-major des armÃ©es. En tant quâancien militaire moi-mÃªme, je sais ce que nous devons Ã  lâhonneur de nos soldats et je regrette que vous vous attaquiez aujourdâhui Ã  lâun des plus grands gÃ©nÃ©raux que nous ayons eus dans lâhistoire rÃ©cente de notre pays.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â MÄrtiÅÅ¡ StaÄ·is (Verts/ALE). â MadamÂ President, dear colleagues, Russia is collapsing: over 1Â million soldiers dead or wounded; more than 11Â 000 armoured vehicles, tanks, have been destroyed; and billions of euros lost. War spending now consumes almost 40Â % of the state budget.

Yet, after three and a half years, Russia still has no victory in sight. That's why the Kremlin has changed its tactics. Unable to win on the battlefield, it now seeks to divide and intimidate Europe by financing corrupt politicians, spreading disinformation and striking our infrastructure to test our readiness.

So, Europe must respond with decisive action: frozen Russian assets to support Ukraine; provide long-range weapons capable of striking deep inside enemy territory; apply Ukraine's drone-war experience; and rebuild our defence industries.

Above all, we must discard peacetime thinking. This is how we will secure the victory.

Â 
Â Â 
Â Â 

VORSITZ: SABINE VERHEYEN
VizeprÃ¤sidentin

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Merja KyllÃ¶nen (The Left). â Madam President, dear colleagues, recent Russian violations of EU Member States' airspace and critical infrastructure are not isolated incidents. They are deliberate acts testing our unity, our resilience and our credibility.

Each drone incursion, each cyberattack, each interference attempt carries a clear message: to divide, to intimidate and to erode trust in the European project and our support to Ukraine.

Our response must be equally clear and united: Europe cannot afford hesitation or fragmentation. We must strengthen our collective defence, deepen intelligence cooperation and ensure that hybrid attacks are met with real consequences, not just statements.

At the same time, unity means more than deterrence; it means political, technological and moral solidarity. Protecting airspace or energy grids is not only a matter of national security, but of shared European sovereignty. The credibility of our deterrence depends not on the strength of any single nation, but on the certainty that an attack on one is an attack on all.

So let us respond not with fear, but with resolve.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Lukas Sieper (NI). â Frau PrÃ¤sidentin, liebe Menschen Europas! Die Abgeordneten der AfD rufen nach meinen Reden hier gerne: âDer kleine Mann hat gesprochenâ. Ich finde das amÃ¼sant, denn genau den behaupten sie ja immer zu vertreten, wÃ¤hrend sie in Wahrheit Verrat an ihm Ã¼ben. Sie Ã¼ben Verrat, weil ihre Abgeordneten wie Kollege Bystron sich mutmaÃlich von Russland bestechen lassen. Sie Ã¼ben Verrat durch das Verbreiten von Fake News. Denn von allem, was auf dem Spiel steht, ist der Verlust einer objektiven RealitÃ¤t, so befÃ¼rchte ich, womÃ¶glich die allergrÃ¶Ãte Gefahr. Und sie Ã¼ben Verrat durch ihre Hetze gegen die Schwachen, denn das ist nur die bloÃe primitive Doktrin, dass Macht gleich Recht ist.

Ich sage es einmal ganz deutlich: Die Politik der AfD ist Verrat am deutschen Volk. Die Politik der ESN ist Verrat an den VÃ¶lkern Europas. Der kleine Mann hat gesprochen.

Mums ir jÄrada tÄdas gaisa aizsardzÄ«bas spÄjas, kas Krieviju atturÄtu no dronu desantu lidinÄÅ¡anas Eiropas gaisa telpÄ. Mums ir jÄmÄcÄs no Ukrainas, kura ir attÄ«stÄ«jusi tehnoloÄ£ijas un katru nakti veiksmÄ«gi notriec Krievijas dronu simtus.

Â Â Thijs Reuten (S&D). â MadamÂ President, testing us must not be a consequence-free exercise. Russia's hybrid war is not only probing our defences; it's also testing our political resolve. We cannot assume any incursion is accidental. Russia wants us to doubt its intentions. Hybrid war happens because of a deterrence gap. Capabilities mean little without the will to deliver consequences. Putin respects only strength, not diplomacy. And we need clear red lines, credible consequences and preventive measures.

And yes, Council, it includes also action on full fossil fuel ban on the assets now, but there's more: binding travel restrictions for so-called Russian diplomats; no free movement; mandatory notification for all travel; real interdiction of the Russian shadow fleet; full synergy with Ukraine's defence industry; integrating them into a strong and efficient European defence against drones, and our best prevention â ensuring that Russia is occupied elsewhere because we have finally provided sufficient military support to Ukraine.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Kristoffer Storm (ECR). â Fru Formand! Vi ser det desvÃ¦rre hver eneste dag i Ukraine: Krig, der bliver kÃ¦mpet med droner, der Ã¸delÃ¦gger elnettet og anden infrastruktur, rammer byer og civile. Men deres befolkning kÃ¦mper fortsat nu pÃ¥ fjerde Ã¥r en heroisk kamp for at skubbe Rusland ud af deres territorium. Nu ser vi ogsÃ¥ i Danmark og andre EU-lande, at droner flyver over vores huse og lufthavne. Selv vores militÃ¦rbaser bliver overflÃ¸jet af disse droner. Det er vores nye og ubehagelige hverdag, og det krÃ¦ver selvfÃ¸lgelig, at vi handler og reagerer. Fru von der Leyen sagde i sin indledning, at det her er hybrid krigsfÃ¸relse, og vi skal tage det meget alvorligt. Det er jeg helt enig i, men jeg er absolut ikke tryg ved, at EU skal stÃ¥ for at beskytte min familie og mit land. Det er jo netop det, vi har NATO til. NATO har erfaringen og knowhow i, hvordan man skal beskytte Europa i situationer som den her. At fÃ¥ EU med von der Leyen i spidsen for vores forsvar er lidt ligesom at skifte Harry Maguire ind i Manchester United. Von der Leyen har en tendens til at lÃ¸be i vejen for alt. Hun elsker at tackle sine medspillere og hele tiden obstruere muligheden for Europa til rent faktisk at samarbejde. Hver eneste gang hun har ordet, sÃ¥ opildner hun mere splittelse i det her hus i stedet for at forsÃ¸ge at samle os. Vi er nÃ¸dt til at tage os sammen i Europa for at kunne stÃ¥ stÃ¦rkt mod Rusland.

Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Lucia Annunziata (S&D). â Signora Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, l'Europa al momento non Ã¨ in guerra, ma il fatto che ci siano paesi europei minacciati e tensioni ci impone una discussione che non Ã¨ piÃ¹ possibile evitare.

La guerra Ã¨ cambiata e i droni sono il simbolo di questo cambiamento: il passaggio dall'era dell'acciaio a quella del web, che pone nuovi pericoli rispetto a cui l'Europa Ã¨ completamente impreparata.

Dobbiamo prenderne atto.Â Serve un'Europa capace di difendersi al suo esterno, ma non con un riarmo generalizzato. Ã possibile una nuova difesa e sta a noi definirla: una difesa integrata e coordinata che non sia strumento di offesa della libertÃ  dei popoli.

Finisco qui richiamando l'articolo forse piÃ¹ importante della Costituzione italiana, l'articolo 11, che rifiuta la guerra, ma che al contempo considera anche parte della Costituzione inalienabile la difesa del paese e dei cittadini.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Alexandr Vondra (ECR). â MadamÂ President, ladies and gentlemen, I see two points as critical here.

First, instead of just reacting, we must be proactive. Since 2014, we have always been one step behind: too late to provide air defence, too late to allow Ukrainians to hit targets inside Russia. Putin is not a peacemaker but a conqueror, and understands only one language: power.

Secondly, it is the national governments that must do their part first, by raising defence budgets, enhancing the industrial base, strengthening civil preparedness.

But we cannot overlook the elephant in the room: our economies, our industries, are shrinking under the excessive climate regulations. If we want to succeed, this legislative Moloch must be dramatically cut down.

Â 
Â Â 
Â

Â Â Pekka Toveri (PPE). â MadamÂ President, dear colleagues, even after these recent violations by Russia, some people say that we are not at war with Russia. This is true. However, Russia is at war with us. This is not me saying this; it's Putin himself saying so.

Russia has waged a hybrid war against us, and it has prepared for conventional attack against us for over 15 years, constantly increasing its efforts to destabilise us. And to those who suspect GPS jamming coming from Russia, well, come to Finland. We can show it.

Why would they stop when there is no attribution? No price, just rewards for its actions against us. In stopping Russian attacks, the 'drone wall' is good, but attribution is better. Russia won't stop before it pays for its crimes. Let's put that price to Russia. Let's stop the shadow fleet for good. Let's take the Russian frozen assets to support Ukraine. Let's increase sanctions also to the third countries supporting Russia. That is the only language that Russia understands and the only way to stop its attacks.

Â 
Â Â 
Â

Â Â Thierry Mariani (PfE). â Madame la PrÃ©sidente, lorsque les violations de lâespace aÃ©rien sont clairement identifiÃ©es, elles sont inadmissibles. Mais le courage rÃ©side aussi dans le fait dâappliquer cette indignation Ã  toutes les violations, et pas seulement Ã  celles qui vous arrangent.

Entre juillet et dÃ©cembreÂ 2024, 900Â violations de lâespace aÃ©rien et 45Â violations de lâespace maritime ont Ã©tÃ© commises par la Turquie contre Chypre. EnÂ 2025, la GrÃ¨ce a dÃ©jÃ  subi plus de 150Â violations de son espace aÃ©rien et plus de 1Â 800Â violations de son espace maritime par des avions et des bateaux turcs. Pourtant, aucun dÃ©bat nâa lieu Ã  ce sujet; tout le monde semble trouver cela normal et garde le silence.

Il faut dÃ©fendre notre souverainetÃ©, toute notre souverainetÃ©, pas seulement celle qui justifierait une nouvelle guerre sainte face Ã  la Russie, car cette guerre nâest pas la nÃ´tre. Jâattends la mÃªme indignation quand la Turquie viole aussi lâespace aÃ©rien dâÃtats europÃ©ens.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Cristian TerheÅ (ECR). â Madam President, dear colleagues, Russia's incursions into EU and NATO airspace over Romania, Poland, Estonia and other countries are not accidents or navigational errors. They are deliberate provocations designed to test our response time, radar coverage and political will. Each airspace violation is reconnaissance. Moscow studies our coordination and readiness without crossing a formal threshold of war.

These calculated violations are part of a broader psychological and hybrid warfare campaign aimed at intimidating and shifting public opinion, as well as normalising aggression. Scrambling jets and issuing diplomatic protests or condemnations are necessary EU and NATO responses, but they are not sufficient. From a military standpoint, true deterrence demands visible readiness, joint patrols, and immediate and firm consequences for repeat violations. Any hesitation is perceived in Moscow not as an act of goodwill, but of weakness. And weakness always invites more aggression.

I call, therefore, that, in the event of a future EU and NATO airspace violation, after an initial warning, the object be shot down.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Juan Ignacio Zoido Ãlvarez (PPE). â SeÃ±ora presidenta, seÃ±or comisario, en cuestiÃ³n de semanas, las incursiones de drones rusos en el espacio aÃ©reo europeo se han vuelto mÃ¡s frecuentes y mÃ¡s atrevidas. Ya no se lanzan solo desde suelo ruso, ahora despegan desde los cientos de buques fantasmas que rodean nuestras costas. Los drones ya no llegan Ãºnicamente del este, ahora lo hacen desde todas las direcciones. Lo que comenzÃ³ en Polonia, se expande hoy por toda Europa. Los aeropuertos de Copenhague y MÃºnich se han visto forzados al cierre, lo que ha dejado a decenas de miles de pasajeros en tierra. Se han localizado drones no identificados mientras sobrevolaban bases militares en Bruselas, y yo me temo que solo es cuestiÃ³n de tiempo que lleguen al sur de Europa.

Putin pretende poner a prueba nuestro compromiso con la defensa colectiva y es plenamente consciente de que cada titubeo europeo es una victoria rusa. Por eso, tenemos la obligaciÃ³n de reaccionar firmes, de reaccionar unidos, porque nadie estÃ¡ suficientemente lejos. Nadie estÃ¡ suficientemente a salvo.

Â Â Salvatore De Meo (PPE). â Signora Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, le violazioni dello spazio aereo e delle infrastrutture critiche degli Stati membri sono episodi che hanno messo a dura prova la sicurezza europea. Sono dei veri e propri test di resistenza politica e militare che mirano a misurarne la vulnerabilitÃ  e la capacitÃ  di reazione.

Ã per questo che serve una risposta unita e determinata, rafforzare la sorveglianza congiunta, sviluppare rapidamente la rete paneuropea di difesa aerea e dronica, migliorare la condivisione di dati e meccanismi di allerta. La sicurezza dei nostri cieli, delle nostre infrastrutture, dei nostri mari significa garantire la sicurezza dei nostri cittadini e delle nostre economie.

Questo perÃ² non significa armare l'Europa, ma costruire una cultura comune della difesa che vada oltre la dimensione militare, che unisca la diplomazia, l'industria e l'innovazione. Oppure, rendiamoci conto, resteremo vulnerabili e dipendenti. Una difesa comune che non sostituisca la NATO, ma ne rafforzi il pilastro europeo, rendendo l'Alleanza atlantica piÃ¹ solida e credibile.

Alcide De Gasperi, piÃ¹ di settant'anni fa, aveva compreso che la pace e la sicurezza dell'Europa passano da una difesa comune. Oggi spetta a noi dare finalmente concretezza a quella visione, trasformandola in realtÃ .

Â 
Â Â
