Â Â Elena Donazzan (ECR). â Signor Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, signor Commissario, Lei ha dichiarato il futuro dell'auto elettrica, riprendendo le parole di von der Leyen, e qualcuno l'ha anche detto. Mi chiedo se siete produttori, analisti, esperti. A me date un po' l'idea di essere con la dimensione dei Soviet, con la proposta della piccola car elettrica, la Lada, o le fabbriche di batterie che non ci sono ancora.

Allora c'Ã¨ del realismo e i numeri dicono un'altra cosa. I numeri dicono che non si vedono nÃ© auto nÃ© camion elettrici. E, se facciamo due conti sui camion, provate a vedere quanto sta una batteria in un camion, quanto spazio prende, quanto spazio di trasporto, quanto costa, quanto ci mette a ricaricarsi e quanto ci costano le grid. Quanto costa la rete di elettrificazione in tutta Europa? Abbiamo questi soldi per elettrificare tutta la nostra Europa?

Intanto altri dati: le fabbriche chiudono, il mercato cinese ci ha invaso, il 55Â % delle importazioni di auto elettriche sono cinesi. Se questo Ã¨ il vostro risultato di difesa dell'industria europea dell'automotive, io vi consiglio di cambiare rotta. E se andiamo a votare, in quest'Aula sono convinta ci sarÃ  un'altra maggioranza.

Â Â Christophe Grudler (Renew). â Monsieur le PrÃ©sident, Monsieur le Commissaire, il est temps de passer Ã  la vitesse supÃ©rieure pour sauver le secteur automobile europÃ©en et lutter contre une concurrence Ã©trangÃ¨re massive et sur-subventionnÃ©e.

Soyons clairs, l'avenir de l'automobile sera Ã©lectrique, pas seulement en Europe, mais aussi Ã  travers le monde. La plupart des constructeurs l'ont bien compris. Les milliards d'euros investis ne doivent pas Ãªtre gÃ¢chÃ©s. Il faut des lignes claires sur la durÃ©e pour encourager les investissements, mais il faut aussi permettre Ã  toutes les technologies Ã  zÃ©ro Ã©mission nette d'exister.

Le vrai dÃ©bat ne porte pas sur le dÃ©lai fixÃ© Ã Â 2035; il porte sur la maniÃ¨re de faciliter l'achat de voitures europÃ©ennes. Osons une vraie prÃ©fÃ©rence europÃ©enne. Osons valoriser les constructeurs qui produisent ici en Europe, avec des contenus europÃ©ens. Â Â Sara Matthieu (Verts/ALE). â Mr President, dear colleagues, by 2027 there will be 19Â electrical models under EURÂ 25Â 000 on our market â those are affordable cars, European cars. I am talking about Volkswagen ID.2, Renault 5; not some distant dream, but on our road, in your driveway.

So here's the choice, and I think it's a pretty simple one.Â Option A â you spend EURÂ 25Â 000 on a car produced in Europe that actually strengthens our industry. You plug it in with renewable electricity that we produce right here in Europe. So that's EURÂ 25Â 000 that actually stays in our economy, that supports our workers, that strengthens our green industry. Or, option B â you buy a combustion engine and watch EURÂ 25Â 000 on fossil fuels literally go up in smoke over the whole car's lifetime. That's petrol, that's diesel, straight into the pockets of the oil sheikhs and Putin's war chest.

Now, here's what I find absolutely fascinating about the right-wing parties that never stop talking about sovereignty, about autonomy; they are the ones desperately clinging to the biggest drain on European purchasing power in history. They want us to keep bleeding billions abroad for oil. You can't make it up. That's not even following ideology, they are just protecting yesterday's industry. The ones that didn't see the future coming while the world has moved on. So, colleagues, let's choose clean air, let's choose green jobs, let's choose made in Europe.

Â 
Â Â 
Â

Â Â Massimiliano Salini (PPE). â Signor Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, devo dire che il dibattito sul futuro dell'industria dell'automotive avviato oggi in Parlamento ha caratteristiche nuove rispetto al passato, e di questo va dato atto al Commissario. Sarebbe stato utile tener conto degli appelli che, anche da questa Camera, da questo Parlamento, arrivarono nel corso della precedente legislatura sul tema della neutralitÃ  tecnologica.

Oggi la Commissione assume un nuovo atteggiamento, perchÃ© il medesimo messaggio arriva anche dal Consiglio. Ecco, in futuro il mio auspicio Ã¨ che il Parlamento venga ascoltato di piÃ¹, perchÃ© questo dibattito dura da tempo qua dentro. Il fatto che mancassero le condizioni abilitanti per il raggiungimento dei target definiti nel regolamento era stato ampiamente detto all'interno di questa casa della democrazia, ampiamente documentato con dati ripetutamente illustrati. Che mancasse una valutazione vera dell'impatto era stato ampiamente detto.

Ã evidente infatti che, sia per quanto riguarda i veicoli leggeri che per quelli pesanti, il motore a combustione interna deve poter convivere in questa fase con il motore elettrico, affinchÃ© l'evoluzione, che da sempre il modello europeo promuove, avvenga in modo compiuto, equilibrato e sostenibile da tutti i punti di vista.

Non Ã¨ per un caso che il continente europeo oggi Ã¨ il continente piÃ¹ sostenibile al mondo, perchÃ© si sono sempre tenute in equilibrio la domanda e l'offerta, rispettando il principio della neutralitÃ  tecnologica. Speriamo che questo accada anche nel settore dell'automotive.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Thomas Pellerin-Carlin (S&D). â Monsieur le PrÃ©sident, aujourd'hui, l'extrÃªmeÂ droite nous montre encore qu'elle n'a aucun sens des responsabilitÃ©s. Soyons clairs, l'avenir de la voiture est Ã©lectrique. Les chercheurs le disent depuis dixÂ ans, et c'est aujourd'hui une telle Ã©vidence que mÃªme la droite allemande le reconnaÃ®t publiquement par la voix d'Ursula vonÂ derÂ Leyen.

Pourtant, l'extrÃªmeÂ droite refuse toujours de voir la rÃ©alitÃ© en face. Est-ce parce que vous Ãªtes trop influencÃ©s par le lobby du diesel? Ou est-ce parce que vous avez peur que VladimirÂ Poutine manque d'argent du pÃ©trole pour continuer Ã  vous financer?

Face Ã  l'irresponsabilitÃ© de l'extrÃªmeÂ droite, que faire? Il faut tout d'abord prÃ©server la stabilitÃ© rÃ©glementaire. Ensuite, il faut investir en Europe dans la production et le recyclage de petites voitures Ã©lectriques adaptÃ©es aux besoins rÃ©els des gens.

Enfin, je vous entends dire que les voitures Ã©lectriques sont trop chÃ¨res et ne sont pas accessibles aux plus pauvres. Mais câest parce que vous refusez le dÃ©ploiement Ã  lâÃ©chelle europÃ©enne du leasing social. Car une voiture Ã©lectrique Ã  80Â eurosÂ par mois, câest cela lâEurope que nous voulons.

Â Â Silvia Sardone (PfE). â Signor Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, signor Commissario, Lei ha detto, riprendo le parole della von der Leyen, che il futuro Ã¨ dell'elettrico. Bene, dato che questa Ã¨ la Sua delega, io vorrei chiederle: Lei sa quanti mezzi pesanti e camion sono stati venduti nel mio paese, in Italia, negli ultimi anni? Nel 2023, 72; nel 2024, 213; cioÃ¨, stiamo parlando di circa l'1Â % del mercato. Sono numeri ridicoli, su questo converrÃ , rendiamocene conto.

Avete trattato i consumatori come se fossero degli idioti, pensando che avrebbero ingoiato facilmente l'obbligo di auto elettrica che, evidenzio, non viene venduta nonostante i miliardi di soldi pubblici messi negli incentivi. Dovete ridare semplicemente alle persone la possibilitÃ  di scegliere, la libertÃ  di scegliere se comprare un'auto a benzina, a diesel, elettrica, biocarburanti, ibride, idrogeno, metano. La libertÃ !

Dovete archiviare il Green Deal e smetterla con la vostra mania di regolamentare tutto, di imporre tutto, di decidere tutto. Dovete avere il coraggio di cancellare il divieto di vendita di auto diesel e benzina al 2035. Con le vostre scelte avete portato solo licenziamenti. Basta! Fermatevi!

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Alexandr Vondra (ECR). â MrÂ President, ladies and gentlemen, this summer, Luca de Meo made the announcement which tells us everything about where we are. The CEO of Renault â and, in fact, the man who produced the only successful, competitive, small electric automobile â has decided to leave as CEO of Renault to accept an offer from the French luxury industry.

That's where we are. No trust. The best manager has no trust in the future of the automotive industry and goes to the domain which is the last competitive one in Europe. We should blame the Commission â and, finally, maybe, you are waking up to the reality.

But it's time to act now. This House must be unblocked and be ready to join forces with the Member States to come forward to scrap the 2035 ban and ease the pressure on the industry towards fully fledged technological neutrality. The sooner we do it, the better.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Emma Wiesner (Renew). â MrÂ President,Â I've got a message for you, the climate deniers and the climate delayers of this House â you who love to blame electric vehicles for everything. You blame them for higher energy prices and lack of competitiveness. Well, guess what? You are the ones to blame for the higher energy prices and the lack of competitiveness.

You are so stubborn, but the logic isn't there. First, you say it isn't true. Then science comes and proves you wrong. Then you say if we can't fix it, there's no technical solution. Then industry comes and gives you the technology and shows that it's possible. Then you say it costs too much, it will be too expensive. Then businesses come and show you the business case. They show you that it's done and it's possible, and it is feasible. Then you say it's too risky.

But you know what? You are the ones hesitating. You are the ones creating this risk for industry when you're drawing back on policy after policy. Your dependence on dirty energy, your refusal to let go of all the fossil energy â that's what's driving up prices and that's what's causing us lack of competitiveness.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Marie Toussaint (Verts/ALE). â Monsieur le PrÃ©sident, chers collÃ¨gues, Monsieur le Commissaire, je mâadresse au groupe dâextrÃªmeÂ droite qui a demandÃ© ce dÃ©bat. Vous vous prÃ©tendez patriotes, vous prÃ©tendez vouloir sauver lâindustrie automobile europÃ©enne. Mais tout cela, câest de la poudre aux yeux. Votre cabale sur le pacte vert vous amÃ¨ne en rÃ©alitÃ© Ã  lutter contre lâintÃ©rÃªt gÃ©nÃ©ral europÃ©en, non seulement contre le climat, mais aussi contre notre Ã©conomie et nos emplois.

Au cours de la derniÃ¨re dÃ©cennie, la Chine a dÃ©ployÃ© son emprise sur lâensemble de la chaÃ®ne de valeur, des Ã©quipementiers aux batteries de derniÃ¨re gÃ©nÃ©ration. Les emplois europÃ©ens ont quant Ã  eux Ã©tÃ© largement sacrifiÃ©s, dÃ©localisÃ©s avec nos savoir-faire, sans que cela nâait rien Ã  voir avec lâobjectif du Â«tout Ã©lectriqueÂ» enÂ 2035. La preuve: 100Â 000Â emplois ont Ã©tÃ© dÃ©truits rien quâen France entreÂ 2010 etÂ 2020. Vous avez entendu les dates? CâÃ©tait avant lâadoption de lâobjectif deÂ 2035.

La rÃ©alitÃ©, câest que la filiÃ¨re automobile que vous prÃ©tendez dÃ©fendre aujourdâhui a pÃ¢ti de lâobsession de ses actionnaires pour la profitabilitÃ© Ã  court terme. Nous constatons dâailleurs des taux de profit exceptionnellement Ã©levÃ©s dans ce secteur, malgrÃ© la destruction des emplois et une stratÃ©gie commerciale contre-productive, celle-lÃ  mÃªme que vous prÃ´nez, qui a prÃ©fÃ©rÃ© les marges Ã©levÃ©es mais de court terme sur les vÃ©hicules haut de gamme polluants, plutÃ´t que dâinvestir dans les marges, certes plus faibles mais plus durables, des petits vÃ©hicules utilitaires lÃ©gers et abordables.

Pour sauver lâindustrie automobile, il faut investir afin de rendre ces vÃ©hicules encore plus abordables. Cela exige une conditionnalitÃ© stricte des aides publiques et une prÃ©fÃ©rence europÃ©enne cruciale, sans oublier de faire des travailleurs le moteur de la transformation automobile europÃ©enne.

Â 
Â Â

Â Â Dario Tamburrano (The Left). â Signor Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, ogni giorno importiamo 2 milioni di tonnellate di carburanti fossili. Ogni volta che si svuota un serbatoio, dobbiamo attaccarci alla canna di un oleodotto o di una nave cisterna, come dei poppanti bisognosi al biberon.

Ã noto, inoltre, che tra i primi obiettivi di un conflitto troviamo raffinerie e oleodotti. Pensate che nessuno avrebbe la tentazione di colpire un tubo, una petroliera o una nave gasiera in transito verso lâEuropa?

Per lâelettrico Ã¨ parzialmente vera la dipendenza da materiali critici, ma una volta sul territorio dellâUnione essi sono riutilizzabili, potenzialmente all'infinito. E le batterie si ricaricano con lâelettricitÃ , che possiamo produrre internamente.

Un sistema energetico elettrificato di generazione rinnovabile con reti intelligenti e di accumulo distribuito, del quale le auto elettriche saranno una componente, Ã¨ milleÂ volte piÃ¹ resiliente.

Infine, oggi, di fronte al progresso eccezionale delle batterie, per durata, capacitÃ  e utilizzo ridotti di materiali critici, parlare di neutralitÃ  tecnologica Ã¨ come se si fosse chiesto ad Apple, negli anni Novanta, di non produrre piÃ¹ computer, ma di mettersi a vendere macchine da scrivere.

Â Â Petr Bystron (ESN). â Herr PrÃ¤sident, liebe Kollegen! Ich komme aus MÃ¼nchen, und ich fahre einen BMWÂ â nicht, weil die Firma in meinem Wahlkreis ist, sondern weil sie einfach die besten Autos baut. Und ich fahre einen DieselÂ â nicht, weil mir das ein Kommissar vorgeschrieben hat, sondern weil das eine Supertechnologie ist: sparsam, umweltfreundlich und absolut unverwÃ¼stlich.

Genauso haben sich Millionen BÃ¼rger in der ganzen EuropÃ¤ischen Union entschieden, dass sie einen Verbrenner fahren wollen. Und was machen Sie? Sie wollen sie zwingen, eine unterlegene Technologie zu nutzen wie das Elektroauto. Unter fadenscheinigen ErklÃ¤rungen haben Sie ihnen das aufgezwungen. Und was ist das Ergebnis? Allein in Deutschland letztes Jahr 50Â 000Â ArbeitsplÃ¤tze vernichtet in der Automobilindustrie und seit 2019 Ã¼ber 120Â 000Â ArbeitsplÃ¤tze vernichtet. Das ist das Ergebnis Ihrer Politik. Das wollen die BÃ¼rger nicht. Das wollen wir nicht. Und damit ist jetzt Schluss! Morgen gibt es zwei Abstimmungen gegen Ursula von der Leyen â Misstrauensantrag. Das ist das, was Sie geerntet haben.

Â Â Dennis Radtke (PPE). â Herr PrÃ¤sident, Herr Kommissar, liebe Kolleginnen und Kollegen! Die Automobilindustrie in Europa erlebt gewaltige Disruptionen und steht wirklich vor einem Scheideweg, wo wir als politisch Verantwortliche auch mit in der Verantwortung sind, darÃ¼ber zu befinden, welchen Weg diese SchlÃ¼sselindustrie in Europa gehen soll. Es zeigt sich mehr und mehrÂ â und deswegen haben wir als CDU/CSUâGruppe auch geschlossen dagegen gestimmtÂ â, dass das dogmatische Verbrenner-Aus 2035 ein groÃer Fehler war.

Mir geht es gar nicht darum, dass wir hier eine Grundsatzdiskussion immer und immer wieder fÃ¼hren: Was ist jetzt der bessere Antrieb? Wohin werden sich MÃ¤rkte entwickeln? Wie werden sich Verbraucher entscheiden? Sondern wir mÃ¼ssen doch ganz nÃ¼chtern feststellenÂ â und im Ãbrigen haben wir als EVP auf der Internationalen Automobil-Ausstellung in MÃ¼nchen einen Austausch mit den Betriebsratsspitzen der deutschen Automobilindustrie organisiertÂ â, die Botschaft ist doch klar: Die Zukunft wird hÃ¶chstwahrscheinlich vollelektrisch sein. Darauf stellt sich die Industrie ein.

Aber mittlerweile hat jeder einfach verstanden, dass die BrÃ¼cke bis 2035 viel zu kurz ist. Wir mÃ¼ssen jetzt diese BrÃ¼cke technologieoffen verlÃ¤ngern im Sinne der Industrie, im Kampf um die ArbeitsplÃ¤tze, im Kampf um die ZukunftsfÃ¤higkeit. Gleichzeitig mÃ¼ssen wir unsere Anstrengungen im Hochlauf der EâMobilitÃ¤t auch mit Blick auf den Ausbau von Ladeinfrastruktur, mit Blick auf Bezahlbarkeit â Stichwort Social Leasing â, all die VorschlÃ¤ge, die auf dem Tisch liegen, das mÃ¼ssen wir jetzt gemeinsam vorantreiben. Aber an diesem ideologischen Aus 2035, da muss jetzt gerÃ¼ttelt werden, die BrÃ¼cke muss verlÃ¤ngert werden, und dann kann es was werden mit der ZukunftsfÃ¤higkeit der deutschen und europÃ¤ischen Automobilindustrie.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Elisabetta Gualmini (S&D). â Mr President, dear Commissioner, we know the numbers of the crisis of the automotive industry, we know the numbers of the production fall and the sales decrease. It is a perfect storm with multiple explaining factors: the transition to electric vehicles is costly and difficult given the shortage of raw materials and delays in the infrastructure; changing lifestyles; competition from China; tariffs from the US.

However, these challenges cannot justify abandoning the climate targets already fixed and carried out so far; we need to give certainty to producers and investors who have planned their activities precisely because of those targets. It would be stupid to cancel the 2035 ban on combustion engines because we need to safeguard competitiveness. But, at the same time, it is crystal clear that we need more flexibility, we need pragmatic action. We cannot resign to ideology, for instance, by rethinking the principle of technological neutrality. We are the policy makers, we have to find the conditions in which our core industry and its employees can innovate without perishing, can embrace sustainability without losing salaries and rights. It is not easy, but what is sure is that the answer cannot be always and forever the status quo.

Â 
Â Â 
Â

Â Â Engin Eroglu (Renew). â Herr PrÃ¤sident, sehr geehrter Herr Kommissar! Wir wollen Klimaschutz mit Vernunft und Innovation und nicht mit dem Verbot von Verbrennern. Dann haben es CDU und CSU geschafft, Frau von der Leyen zur KommissionsprÃ¤sidentin zu machen. 2020 hat Frau von der Leyen mit ihrer Mehrheit im EuropÃ¤ischen Parlament dieses Verbrenner-Aus beschlossen und hat Milliarden an Wert in der EuropÃ¤ischen Union zerstÃ¶rt.

Mit diesen Milliarden, die in unserer Wirtschaft zerstÃ¶rt worden sind, hÃ¤tten wir sehr, sehr viel Klimaschutz machen kÃ¶nnen in der EuropÃ¤ischen Union, hÃ¤tten sehr, sehr, sehr viele BÃ¤ume pflanzen kÃ¶nnen, die CO2 binden. Â Â Kai Tegethoff (Verts/ALE). â Mr President, German car manufacturing â or, in von der Leyen words, 'the pride of Europe' â is fighting for its life. We are watching others win a race we should be leading. Why? Because we have an establishment that is terrified of losing voters to the right. Those who called this debate today are in the business of fear tactics and, as we saw in the introduction already, also of plain lies. They are far more obsessed with protecting corporate interests than the workers they claim to speak for.

Meanwhile, the world is going electric â with or without us. Colleagues, every moment you delay, you are actively destroying our industrial base and handing market share to Beijing. Stop the foot dragging, not electrification â your hesitation is costing jobs. Let's put all of Europe's talent and expertise into our car industries' electric future. Commit to the 2035 target, and let's prove and ensure that 'made in Europe' still means 'number one'.

Â Â Marina Mesure (The Left). â Monsieur le PrÃ©sident, le secteur automobile traverse une crise sans prÃ©cÃ©dent. Personne ne peut le nier. Mais la rÃ©ponse quâapporte aujourdâhui lâextrÃªmeÂ droite est Ã  la fois simpliste et dangereuse.

Â Â RaÃºl de la Hoz Quintano (PPE). â SeÃ±or presidente, seÃ±or comisario, la partida en la que nos jugamos el futuro de la automociÃ³n ha comenzado. Ganar o perder implica mantener o destruir nuestra industria bandera de la economÃ­a europea y tambiÃ©n millones de puestos de trabajo. Y hoy sabemos ya que no ganaremos en absoluto la partida si apostamos por la electrificaciÃ³n absoluta en el aÃ±o 2035 y que solamente ganaremos la partida si sabemos garantizar la competitividad del sector antes y despuÃ©s de ese aÃ±o 2035.

Y para ello debemos jugar correctamente tres cartas. La carta del pragmatismo, la del tiempo y la del diÃ¡logo. Pragmatismo para reconocer que no existe una Ãºnica soluciÃ³n tecnolÃ³gica para reducir emisiones. Y esto no es una resignaciÃ³n ante el fracaso de la implantaciÃ³n del vehÃ­culo elÃ©ctrico en Europa. Es mÃ¡s que asumir que el proceso de descarbonizaciÃ³n debe ser, en todo caso, econÃ³micamente sostenible, tÃ©cnicamente posible y tecnolÃ³gicamente abierto.

La segunda carta es la carta del tiempo. El proceso no puede imponerse de forma abrupta. Necesitamos introducir el criterio de flexibilizaciÃ³n. La industria, y tambiÃ©n el mercado, necesita tiempo y certidumbre. Tiempo para implantar el modelo y certidumbre para canalizar adecuadamente las inversiones. Pedir tiempo no es pedir retraso en los plazos, es asumir que el proceso debe ser justo, viable y ordenado.

Y por Ãºltimo, el diÃ¡logo. DiÃ¡logo con las empresas, con el sector y tambiÃ©n, por cierto, con los trabajadores. El diÃ¡logo como puente entre la ambiciÃ³n climÃ¡tica y la realidad de nuestra industria. La transiciÃ³n hacia una movilidad descarbonizada no se va a ganar con imposiciones ni de tiempos ni de tecnologÃ­as, sino con la inteligencia de jugar adecuadamente estas tres cartas.

Â Â CÃ©sar Luena (S&D). â SeÃ±or presidente, querido seÃ±or comisario âya que estamos aquÃ­ casi en la intimidadâ, ustedes estÃ¡n corrigiendo y rebajando âcomo si fuera un salchichÃ³n, rodaja a rodajaâ todo el Pacto Verde Europeo. Â Â Oihane Agirregoitia MartÃ­nez (Renew). â SeÃ±or presidente, seamos muy claras y claros. No estamos de acuerdo en renunciar a los objetivos de sostenibilidad y medioambientales que hemos alcanzado. Y no vamos a dar alas al negacionismo del cambio climÃ¡tico.

Tenemos el Plan de AcciÃ³n lanzado en marzo y, en el mes de septiembre, el tercer diÃ¡logo estratÃ©gico con el sector. Se va a adelantar la evaluaciÃ³n intermedia de la normativa y tenemos un abanico de soluciones tecnolÃ³gicas con las que debemos trabajar de manera que cumplamos con los objetivos de descarbonizaciÃ³n y electrificaciÃ³n, con resiliencia en las cadenas de suministro y con una clara agenda industrial compartida.

Â Â Angelika Niebler (PPE). â Herr PrÃ¤sident, Herr Kommissar, liebe Kolleginnen, liebe Kollegen! Das Verbrennerverbot greift einfach zu kurz. Wir mÃ¼ssen auf Technologieoffenheit setzen, denn nur so bleiben wir wettbewerbsfÃ¤hig und kÃ¶nnen flexibel auch auf kÃ¼nftige Entwicklungen reagieren. Â Â Barbara Bonte (PfE). â Voorzitter, commissaris, collegaâs, de Europese Commissie verandert onze economie in ijltempo in een planeconomie. Men verbiedt binnenkort de verkoop van autoâs met een verbrandingsmotor, een technologie waarin Europa steeds een pionier is geweest en die miljoenen mensen mobiliteit en welvaart heeft opgeleverd. Men wil de vraag naar elektrische wagens kunstmatig opdrijven, tegen de wil van de automobilist in.

Waarom wil de automobilist deze wagens niet? Omdat ze te duur zijn, omdat het netwerk niet klaar is en de laadinfrastructuur tekortschiet. Er worden ideologische wensbeelden verkocht die in de praktijk niet haalbaar zijn. Productie en banen verdwijnen naar landen buiten de EU, terwijl de rekening wordt doorgeschoven naar onze gezinnen en bedrijven.

Mijn oproep is duidelijk: laat de vrije markt zijn werk doen en stop het verbod op diesel-Â en benzinewagens. PlaneconomieÃ«n hebben nog nooit gewerkt, integendeel. Laat verschillende technologieÃ«n met elkaar concurreren en geef de consument zijn vrijheid terug, want alleen vrije keuze brengt echte vooruitgang.

Â Â Susana SolÃ­s PÃ©rez (PPE). â SeÃ±or presidente, seÃ±or comisario, seÃ±orÃ­as, este debate no va solo de tecnologÃ­a, va de personas y de evitar pÃ©rdidas de empleo y de industria, porque la realidad es tozuda: vender solo coches elÃ©ctricos en 2035 no es realista.

Pero no se trata tampoco de dar marcha atrÃ¡s, porque sabemos que hay miles de millones invertidos. Lo que nos estÃ¡ pidiendo el sector es algo mucho mÃ¡s sensato. Nos pide mÃ¡s tiempo, mÃ¡s flexibilidad y poner en marcha las medidas para que esto sea posible: puntos de recarga, precios asequibles, incentivos y acceso a la red, porque este debate tampoco va de ideologÃ­a, va de pragmatismo y de sentido comÃºn, y eso significa reconocer que todas las tecnologÃ­as son necesarias para reducir emisiones. A v souladu s lÃ­dry automobilovÃ©ho prÅ¯myslu jsem pÅesvÄdÄen, Å¾e budoucnost je elektrickÃ¡. OtÃ¡zkou jen zÅ¯stÃ¡vÃ¡, zda bude evropskÃ¡ nebo ÄÃ­nskÃ¡. MÃ­sto nekoneÄnÃ½ch debat o ukonÄenÃ­ prodeje aut se spalovacÃ­m motorem v roce 2035, kterÃ© samy automobilky jeÅ¡tÄ pÅed tÅemi lety vÃ­taly, rozhodne skuteÄnÃ¡ nabÃ­dka.

Pokud evropskÃ© automobilky nedokÃ¡Å¾ou pÅijÃ­t s konkurenceschopnÃ½m a dostupnÃ½m elektromobilem, zaplavÃ­ nÃ¡s ÄÃ­nskÃ¡ auta a Å¾Ã¡dnÃ© Ãºpravy regulace na tom nic nezmÄnÃ­. O ÃºspÄchu Äi neÃºspÄchu evropskÃ©ho automobilovÃ©ho prÅ¯myslu se nebude rozhodovat u kulatÃ½ch stolÅ¯ politikÅ¯, ale pÅÃ­mo v automobilkÃ¡ch, zejmÃ©na v jejich vÃ½vojovÃ½ch oddÄlenÃ­ch. Â Â

Â Â Julien Leonardelli (PfE). â Monsieur le PrÃ©sident, chers collÃ¨gues, lâUnion europÃ©enne sâapprÃªte Ã  renier lâun de ses plus grands succÃ¨s industriels: lâautomobile. Lâinterdiction des moteurs thermiques enÂ 2035 nâest pas un progrÃ¨s, câest une folie stratÃ©gique. Sous prÃ©texte dâÃ©cologie, vous fragilisez nos constructeurs, vous dÃ©truisez des centaines de milliers dâemplois et vous livrez nos marchÃ©s Ã  la Chine, plus grand pollueur mondial. VoilÃ  la rÃ©alitÃ©.

Partout sur le terrain, ouvriers, ingÃ©nieurs, entrepreneurs le disent, le calendrier vonÂ derÂ Leyen est irrÃ©aliste. Les coÃ»ts explosent, les infrastructures ne suivent pas. Cette transition brutale nâest plus une ambition Ã©cologique, câest une punition sociale. Elle frappe les classes populaires et rurales, celles qui ont besoin de leur voiture pour vivre et travailler, pendant que Bruxelles sacrifie notre compÃ©titivitÃ© sur lâautel de lâidÃ©ologie. Nous, les patriotes pour lâEurope, avec JordanÂ Bardella, nous proposons un autre chemin, celui de la libertÃ© technologique, des biocarburants, de lâhydrogÃ¨ne et de lâinnovation. Lâavenir de lâautomobile europÃ©enne doit se bÃ¢tir avec nos nations et nos travailleurs, et non contre eux.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Marion MarÃ©chal (ECR). â Monsieur le PrÃ©sident, chers collÃ¨gues, en mettant ce dÃ©bat Ã  lâordre du jour, mon groupeÂ ECR a voulu rappeler lâurgence de lever lâinterdiction des moteurs thermiques neufs Ã  partir deÂ 2035. Fruit du dogmatisme vert, cette interdiction insensÃ©e a mis lâindustrie automobile europÃ©enne Ã  lâagonie et a Ã©videmment jouÃ© en faveur des constructeurs chinois. Le bilan des tartufes du climat se compte en centaines de milliers dâemplois europÃ©ens perdus.

En France, en seulement cinqÂ ans, 40Â 000Â emplois ont Ã©tÃ© perdus. Câest plus de 10Â % des effectifs. Des pans entiers de notre Ã©conomie, de lâindustrie lourde aux Ã©quipementiers, sont aujourdâhui en pÃ©ril. Je vois certains de ceux qui prÃ©tendent venir au chevet des constructeurs europÃ©ens Ãªtre en rÃ©alitÃ© responsables dâavoir mis lâindustrie automobile europÃ©enne au bord de la faillite. Câest la Commission de vonÂ derÂ Leyen qui a initiÃ© lâinterdiction des moteurs thermiques en faisant adopter le pacte vert. Câest le commissaire SÃ©journÃ©, alors prÃ©sident du groupe Renew, qui, le 14Â fÃ©vrierÂ 2023, a fait basculer le vote de ce Parlement en faveur de lâinterdiction des vÃ©hicules neufs Ã  moteur thermique.

En France, une expression dit: Â«Câest un grand tort que dâavoir raison trop tÃ´t.Â» Nous le savons, nous, avec le groupeÂ ECR qui alertons depuis le premierÂ jour sur le risque de cette interdiction. Mais une autre expression dit quâil nây a que les idiots qui ne changent pas dâavis. Il nâest donc pas trop tard pour ouvrir les yeux et rÃ©parer les erreurs du passÃ©.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â FranÃ§ois-Xavier Bellamy (PPE). â Monsieur le PrÃ©sident, Monsieur le Commissaire, je nâoublierai jamais ce jour oÃ¹ a Ã©tÃ© votÃ©e dans cet hÃ©micycle, il y a quelques annÃ©es, lâinterdiction des moteurs thermiques enÂ 2035. Je nâoublierai jamais la cÃ©lÃ©bration de tant dâÃ©lus Ã  qui jâobjectaisÂ â comme lâa fait le groupe PPE, qui sâest opposÃ© Ã  cette mesure dÃ©lÃ©tÃ¨reÂ â que lâEurope venait de faire le plus grand cadeau Ã  la Chine et de commettre le plus grand crime contre sa propre compÃ©titivitÃ©.

Quelques annÃ©es plus tard, le rÃ©sultat est lÃ . Nous voyons les voitures chinoises inonder dÃ©sormais nos marchÃ©s, et des dizaines de milliers dâemplois sont dÃ©truits dans les usines de nos pays; des millions dâEuropÃ©ens ne peuvent plus acheter de voiture neuve ni circuler librement. Ce tort immense causÃ© Ã  lâindustrie automobile est prÃ©cisÃ©ment dÃ» au choix dâune seule technologie, qui dÃ©truit le principe mÃªme de notre responsabilitÃ©.

Le rÃ´le des responsables politiques nâest pas de dÃ©cider de la bonne technologie; leur rÃ´le est de fixer la finalitÃ© en laissant la libertÃ© aux industriels, aux chercheurs et aux techniciens de dÃ©velopper les moyens dâatteindre cet objectif. Oui, nous voulons dÃ©carboner, mais dÃ©carboner ne veut pas dire se mettre Ã  lâÃ©cole de la Chine, qui est aujourdâhui le premier pollueur du monde.

Notre devoir est de sauver la neutralitÃ© technologique et, pour cela, dâabroger cette norme absurde qui a fixÃ© le 100Â % de vÃ©hicules Ã©lectriques enÂ 2035, afin de rendre Ã  ceux qui produisent dans nos pays et Ã  ceux qui veulent se dÃ©placer la libertÃ© Ã  laquelle ils ont droit et la prospÃ©ritÃ© que nous devons retrouver.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Gilles Pennelle (PfE). â Monsieur le PrÃ©sident, il y a deuxÂ ans, dans cet hÃ©micycle, une majoritÃ© de dÃ©putÃ©s votait la fin des voitures thermiques enÂ 2035. Dans un Ã©lan d'aveuglement collectif inspirÃ© par les Khmers verts, l'Europe scellait en rÃ©alitÃ© le destin de son industrie et reniait l'une des plus Ã©clatantes expressions de son gÃ©nie: l'automobile.

DeuxÂ ans auront suffi pour mesurer l'ampleur de ce choix suicidaire. Les consÃ©quences visibles et souvent irrÃ©versibles terrassent nos Ã©conomies, au premier rang desquelles se situe la France, oÃ¹ des Ã©tudes sÃ©rieuses annoncent la disparition de plus de 100Â 000Â emplois industriels dans la dÃ©cennie. Il s'agit en rÃ©alitÃ© d'un vÃ©ritable dÃ©sastre social, Ã©conomique et donc politique. Le camp des nationaux, auquel appartient le groupe des Patriotes pour l'Europe, n'a cessÃ© d'alerter sur les ravages de cette politique dÃ©croissantiste.

Aujourd'hui, l'Ã©vidence s'impose, le chancelier allemand lui-mÃªme appelle Ã  lever l'interdiction fixÃ©e Ã Â 2035. Preuve que, comme toujours en politique, le rÃ©alisme finit par triompher des chimÃ¨res. Il est dÃ©sormais plus qu'urgent de lever cette interdiction et de rendre Ã  nos industriels la libertÃ© de produire. C'est une question de souverainetÃ© et, osons le mot, de survie.

Â Â

Â Â Charlie Weimers (ECR). â Mr President, Commissioner, the 2035 ban on combustion engines was never about the climate. It was about Brussels. It was about you. You politicians know better than engineers, better than workers, better than the market itself. Tell you what: factories are closing not because of Beijing, but because of Brussels.

The EPP now admits the ban was a mistake.

(The speaker gave a slow handclap)

The EPP leads the way? Really? To what? To where? Ah, but the EPP Chair, Manfred Weber, promised Europeans the end of the combustion engine phaseâout. When? We have a centreâright majority. Why are you so afraid to use it? Forget about the extensions, the reviews and revision. End the 2035 combustion engine ban! Let's go!

Â Â

Â Â Georgiana Teodorescu (ECR). â Domnule preÈedinte, vÄ mirÄ cÄ Ã®n doar trei luni de zile am ajuns sÄ votÄm trei moÈiuni de cenzurÄ Ã®mpotriva Ursulei von der Leyen, iniÈiate de grupuri politice diferite?

Unul din principalele reproÈuri aduse Comisiei Ã®n aceste moÈiuni se referÄ la infamul Pact verde Èi la efectele lui distructive asupra economiei Èi industriei europene.

AstÄzi analizÄm situaÈia industriei automotive Èi nu putem sÄ o facem fÄrÄ sÄ amintim despre cÃ¢t de afectat a fost acest domeniu de politicile Green Deal: fabrici Ã®nchise, sute de mii de oameni rÄmaÈi fÄrÄ locuri de muncÄ, costuri din ce Ã®n ce mai crescute la producÈia de maÈini, ba mai mult, apariÈia pe piaÈÄ a unui competitor periculos de ieftin, China.

Pe locul Èase Ã®n Europa la producÈia de maÈini, RomÃ¢nia e printre cele mai afectate state. Ãn timp ce dezbatem Ã®n plen, astÄzi, 200 de muncitori de la o fabricÄ din Dej tocmai Èi-au pierdut locul de muncÄ. Alte mii au trecut prin disponibilizÄri lunile trecute.

Pactul verde trebuie suspendat de urgenÈÄ Èi economia europeanÄ trebuie salvatÄ.

Â Â Carlo Ciccioli (ECR). â Signor Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, sono arrivato in Europa con molto entusiasmo, pensando che qui si potesse discutere con onestÃ , portando la voce dei miei territori e facendo capire, anche ai piÃ¹ ideologizzati, che non si possono fare leggi con totale disconnessione dalla realtÃ . Presto mi sono accorto che in questo Parlamento la politica non conta niente, le decisioni non le prendiamo noi, ma la Commissione europea, politicizzata, ideologica, che non valuta gli impatti, che ignora i cittadini e le imprese, assolutamente autoreferente.

Il settore dell'automotive Ã¨ il simbolo di questo approccio fallimentare. Mette a rischio milioni di posti di lavoro e industrie, penalizza i consumatori, riduce l'Europa alla dipendenza da altri paesi.

Noi chiediamo di puntare sull'innovazione, sulla libertÃ  tecnologica, sulla ricerca e non nella burocrazia e nella dittatura delle regole ottuse. C'Ã¨ bisogno di un cambio vero, profondo, di una rivoluzione culturale aggressiva che porta alla riscossa dell'Europa, che Ã¨ in mano a funzionari che, da decenni, pensano di poterla cambiare con arroganza. Questo tempo Ã¨ finito.

Â 
Â Â 
Â Â 

PREDSEDÃ: MARTIN HOJSÃK
Podpredseda

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Wopke Hoekstra, Member of the Commission. â Mr President, thanks to the honourable Members for this very insightful debate, which was also, at times, entertaining â I'm not sure whether that was always deliberate, but it certainly was. I did manage to scare away at least half of the audience, but I'll try to suck that up afterwards.

What is at least very clear to me, with all the differences that I've heard, is that we all see that we are up for very significant change in this hugely important sector.

In my view, therefore, it is hugely important to continue to not just focus on where we stand today, but also look at the longer-term trend and make sure that our industry â and we as a society â can actually thrive and prosper in the future.

That means, in my view, that we need to make sure we are at the nexus of climate, of competitiveness and of independence. That is true for the economy at large, but it is certainly also very much true for this hugely important sector.

So, we know why we are doing it: again, for the economy, for climate and for independence. We also know what it is that we are doing: we are providing a clear signal to manufacturers, to their suppliers and investors in clean technologies, and we want to build trust and give them long-term certainty.

At the same time, we are also coming up with pragmatic solutions on how to make it work. I have given you the full list already at the very beginning.

So, it is a combination of being ambitious, of being street-smart, and of making sure we articulate and stay close to our true north. That is what we are going to do. I already told you about the review that is going on in the next three months.

I am very much looking forward to continuing this debate as we go along â again, in order to make sure we reach each and every one of our goals that we have articulated.

Â 
Â Â 
Â

Â Â Piotr MÃ¼ller (ECR), na piÅmie. â Komisja Europejska juÅ¼ dawno oderwaÅa siÄ od rzeczywistoÅci.

Zakaz sprzedaÅ¼y samochodÃ³w spalinowych po 2035 roku to ideologiczna decyzja, ktÃ³ra zagraÅ¼a europejskiemu przemysÅowi i uderza w miliony kierowcÃ³w.

Â 

11. Promoting EU digital rules: protecting European sovereignty (debate)
Video of the speeches
Â 

Â Â PredsedajÃºci . â A ÄalÅ¡Ã­m bodom programu je vyhlÃ¡senie Rady a Komisie â Presadzovanie digitÃ¡lnych pravidiel EÃ: ochrana eurÃ³pskej suverenity. A na Ãºvod poprosÃ­m za Radu pani ministerku Bjerre (2025/2871(RSP)).

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Marie Bjerre, President-in-Office of the Council. â MrÂ President, honourable Members, Commissioner, the debate on the promotion of EU digital rules is very much a debate on the protection of European sovereignty. I consider this both a very important and timely debate. Denmark took over the Presidency of the Council of the European Union at a time of rising uncertainty on the world stage. In the digital space, we see transatlantic tensions rising; both the US administration and private companies are questioning the EU's digital regulatory framework.

Our goals remain clear: first, to protect our citizens online, as we do offline. Second: to defend our autonomy in setting our own rules. The European Union is today a global leader in digital regulation. We have adopted groundbreaking legislation such as the Digital Services Act and the Digital Markets Act. Yet, with leadership comes responsibility not only to regulate, but also to ensure that regulation has a direct impact on people's lives.

The Digital Services Act aims to create a safe online environment for our citizens. Platforms have to remove illegal content and address the risk of disinformation and other harmful material. At the same time, the Digital Markets Act ensures that large platforms â the so-called gatekeepers â behave in a fair way online. Both of these acts set a high global benchmark. They establish clear obligations based on the importance of the online platform. In other words, bigger players have bigger responsibilities.

But these laws are now being contested. The US President has threatened tariffs against the EU. Large technology firms also resist. A recent example is Apple's call to scrap the Digital Markets Act. What I can say to these statements is that the rules apply regardless of the headquarters of the companies. They apply equally to companies of European, Chinese or US origin. To put it in short, the rules do not discriminate. They try to create a level playing field. Doing business in the EU is highly profitable for digital players. The single market is attractive for platforms, in particular for many US large online platforms and search engines. The cost of compliance with the DSA and the DMA is very low compared to the financial benefits of operating in the single market.

The EU has the sovereign right to regulate economic activities within its territory, in line with our own democratic values. We have always enforced and will continue to enforce our laws in a fair manner towards all companies operating in the EU. This is done in full compliance with global rules and in regular contact with online platforms. As in all other policy areas, we will continue to take the decisions we think are needed to ensure a fair and safe digital space. We count on the European Parliament as a counterpart in this effort.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Henna Virkkunen, Executive Vice-President of the Commission. â Mr President, honourable members, I remember very well when, in July 2022, this House approved two very groundbreaking laws; two laws that are crucial for our democracy and for our single market in the digital age â the Digital Services Act and the Digital Markets Act. Back then, this House confirmed both laws with an overwhelming majority, and in the Council both laws were agreed by all Member States. Over time, this House has also made very clear that it strongly supports the implementation of both laws. Today's discussion is a testament to that.

European citizens and businesses have also signalled clearly that they want to see the DSA and DMA implemented effectively. Last month, President von der Leyen was crystal clear in her State of the Union speech here: when it comes to digital regulation, the EU sets its own standards and decides for itself.

So, clearly, this Parliament, the Commission, our citizens and European businesses agree: we need the DSA and the DMA. We need the DSA because it protects our fundamental rights online, because it protects our children from online harm, because it protects our democratic processes, and because it protects us from illegal, unsafe and unsustainable products. The DMA is important because it opens the door to the next waves of innovation, it brings the power of choice back to consumers.

The co-legislators gave the Commission a very strong mandate to enforce the DSA and DMA. Today, I can assure you that we are taking this duty very seriously.Â In April this year, we took the first two decisions under the DMA for Meta and Apple. We will not hesitate to take further action to ensure compliance. We also provided guidance to Apple to make interoperability a reality by the end of this year. Smartwatches, earbuds, speakers: no matter the brand everything will work seamlessly with your iPhones, just like Apple's own products.

Under the DSA, we have already designated 25Â platforms to whom we have addressed more than 150Â requests for information, and we have opened proceedings in 14Â cases, and have issued preliminary findings against providers of four very large online platforms. So workshops, roundtables, guidelines, work is ongoing all the time. We now have more than 250Â staff enforcing DSM and DMA, and we are hiring 60 more to further strengthen our hard-working teams.

We can already see many positive changes which have taken place thanks to these two laws. There is now much more transparency; thanks to the DSA, users can more easily exercise their rights. Many citizens have already used their right to complain when platforms are removing their content. As we can see on the DSA Transparency Database, social media platforms have reversed their removal decisions in 20â35% of those cases. So we have been empowering our users and citizens. Because of the DSA, users can also see now why content on online platforms is recommended to them and they can also opt out of personalised recommendations based on profiling.

Under the DSA, we also, very recently in July, issued guidelines on the protection of minors. The scope and ambition of these guidelines are unparalleled globally, and now we are working with online platforms to make sure that they are changing their practices to also comply with these rules and following the guidelines, which very concretely state what kind of practices we are expecting from online platforms to make sure that our children have their safety, security and privacy ensured when they are using online services. Thanks to the DMA, Europeans can now choose the web browser they prefer and they also have better control over their own data.

What is true for the DSA and DMA is also true for the application of our competition rules in the digital sphere. In September, we concluded that Google breached EU antitrust rules in the AdTech industry and fined it with EURÂ 2.95Â billion. Google now has 60Â days to present us with a viable solution. Structural remedies are likely to be the only way to cease Google's conflicts of interest in the industry on both sides of the Atlantic.Â We also received comprehensive commitments from Microsoft to address EU competition concerns relating to the Teams collaboration platform. This decision helps us to open crucial markets to competitors.

Some of the very large online platforms and gatekeepers have called for the DSA and DMA to be repealed or reset. Some refer to non-existent free speech concerns to avoid respecting the DSA. Some pretend to speak on behalf of European consumers, arguing that the DMA is worsening their experience. Honourable members, you surely remember the similar strong lobbying when we â I was also a member of Parliament at that time â were shaping and adopting these two legislations. Some of the platforms and gatekeepers do not want to accept legislation that holds them accountable to their promises or legislation that opens up their walled gardens to offer opportunities for new innovators. Let me be clear: in Europe, we believe in the force of democratic rules, not in the rule of force. It is not up to the companies to decide whether to comply with our rules, and it is not up to them or the other countries to decide how we enforce our rules. Europe decides for itself.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Axel Voss, im Namen der PPE-Fraktion. â Herr PrÃ¤sident, Frau VizeprÃ¤sidentin der EuropÃ¤ischen Kommission, liebe Kolleginnen und Kollegen! Wir mÃ¼ssen den amerikanischen Unternehmen schon die Frage stellen, ob sie eigentlich noch ein Interesse haben, sich an unseren Regeln zu orientieren. Denn aktuell scheint das irgendwie nicht mehr ganz so der Fall zu sein. Statt Kooperation erleben wir hier zunehmend offene InfragestellungÂ â und Missachtung eigentlichÂ â eines europÃ¤ischen Rechtsrahmens.

Dann mÃ¼ssen wir uns eben auch fragen, ob das nicht auch Konsequenzen haben soll fÃ¼r ihre AktivitÃ¤ten in unserem Binnenmarkt. Und Europa ist ja kein Regelraum nach Belieben, sondern wir haben etwas vorzuweisen, wir haben auch etwas zu bieten, und wir sind diejenigen, die eben auch Werte mit Technologie entsprechend verbinden wollen. Deshalb sollten wir auch ernsthaft darÃ¼ber nachdenken, dann auch mitunter hÃ¤rtere Konsequenzen vorzusehen, wenn diese Haltung entsprechend anhÃ¤lt, etwa durch eine ÃberprÃ¼fung oder eben auch gegebenenfalls eine Beendigung des transatlantischen Datenaustauschs. Das ist eine Drohung, glaube ich, die auch nicht ungehÃ¶rt bleibt.

Deshalb mÃ¼ssen wir uns solche Dinge Ã¼berlegen. Wir haben etwas zu bieten als Europa, aber das muss nach unseren Regeln passieren. Wir brauchen deshalb die europÃ¤ische Strategie, neue europÃ¤ische Geschwindigkeiten, die MÃ¶glichkeit zu skalieren, neue IntensitÃ¤ten, neue Investments, auch Risikokapital entsprechend aufzubauen. Dann haben wir denen auch was entgegenzusetzen.

Â

Â Â Virginie Joron, au nom du groupe PfE. â Monsieur le PrÃ©sident, Madame la Vice-PrÃ©sidente, chers collÃ¨gues, le dÃ©bat d'aujourd'hui porte sur la promotion et la protection de la souverainetÃ© numÃ©rique de l'Union europÃ©enne. Macron a lÃ©guÃ© nos donnÃ©es de santÃ© Ã  Microsoft. Sous Macron, nous avons aussi perdu Technip, Lafarge et mÃªme Doliprane. Bruxelles a abandonnÃ© Nokia, a choisi Microsoft pour sa messagerie interne et un prestataire amÃ©ricain pour ses recrutements. Bruxelles a choisi cette annÃ©e Amazon et Microsoft pour son cloud et, dans une moindre mesure, OVH. Pourquoi dans une moindre mesure?

ArrÃªtez de prÃªcher la prÃ©fÃ©rence europÃ©enne, arrÃªtez dâaller Ã  Washington pour recevoir lâeau bÃ©nite du Conseil de l'Atlantique ou lâonction de Soros. Fini l'hypnose de GÃ©rardÂ Miller et les journaux subventionnÃ©s. Le peuple a retrouvÃ© sa voix grÃ¢ce aux rÃ©seaux sociaux. You are the media, a dÃ©clarÃ© Musk. Et câest ce qui vous dÃ©range? Macron qui hurle Ã  Sarrebruck contre les plateformes numÃ©riques. Un nouveau variant Covid Frankenstein pour cet hiver. Mais les gens ne vous Ã©coutent plus. Vous prÃ©textez la protection de la souverainetÃ© numÃ©rique uniquement pour censurer et Ã©touffer un narratif qui ne vous convient pas. Vous avez tuÃ© l'industrie automobile. Qui peut croire ici que vous voulez sauver celle du numÃ©rique?

Â Â StÃ©phanie Yon-Courtin, au nom du groupe Renew. â Monsieur le PrÃ©sident, Madame la Vice-PrÃ©sidente Virkkunen, y a-t-il encore un pilote dans lâavion de notre souverainetÃ© numÃ©rique europÃ©enne? Quelquâun tient-il vraiment les commandes pour Ã©viter le crash de lâEurope numÃ©rique? Permettez-moi dâen douter.

Nos rÃ©gulations ambitieuses sont aujourdâhui attaquÃ©es de toute part, et dâabord en interne. Depuis six mois, vous, Ã  la Commission, nous dites que TikTok et X ont violÃ© le rÃ¨glement sur les services numÃ©riques (DSA). Mais quand allez-vous enfin les sanctionner? En ce qui concerne le rÃ¨glement sur les marchÃ©s numÃ©riques (DMA), combien de temps allons-nous encore nous excuser dâappliquer nos propres rÃ¨gles ou de les brader contre des droits de douane diminuÃ©s?

Vous venez de rappeler lâimportance du DMA et du DSA. Assumez-les, dÃ©fendez-les, parlez-en publiquement. OÃ¹ est passÃ© le leadership europÃ©en qui faisait de nous une rÃ©fÃ©rence mondiale pour imposer un espace numÃ©rique Ã©quitable? Nâoublions pas les attaques venues de lâextÃ©rieur, avec Donald Trump et les oligarques des grandes entreprises technologiques qui rÃªvent de voir tomber notre modÃ¨le europÃ©en de concurrence loyale.

Aujourdâhui, ce sont Apple et Google qui piÃ©tinent publiquement le DMA pour continuer Ã  tuer la concurrence. Demain, ce seront toutes les grandes entreprises technologiques qui tenteront de rÃ©tablir la jungle numÃ©rique pour sâapproprier tout le gÃ¢teau europÃ©en et Ã©craser nos propres entreprises.

Donnez Ã  la Commission europÃ©enne les moyens, tous les moyens, plus de moyens, la rapiditÃ©, le courage dâagir. DerriÃ¨re ces rÃ¨gles se cachent 450Â millions de consommateurs, des milliers dâentreprises europÃ©ennes et des petites entreprises que nous devons protÃ©ger et soutenir.

Il est temps dâagir, au risque de voir lâEurope mourir. Appliquons nos rÃ¨gles, toutes nos rÃ¨gles, rien que nos rÃ¨gles, et pas la loi du plus fort. Avec lâomnibus numÃ©rique, simplifions quand câest nÃ©cessaire. Jamais au prix de nos principes. Jamais au prix de nos valeurs. Jamais au prix de notre Europe.

Â Â Alexandra Geese, im Namen der Verts/ALE-Fraktion. â Herr PrÃ¤sident, Frau Kommissarin, sehr verehrte Kolleginnen und Kollegen! PrÃ¤sident Trump lÃ¤sst TikTok in den USA von seinen VerbÃ¼ndeten kaufenÂ â jemand erinnert sich vielleicht an OrbÃ¡nÂ â und hat bereits angekÃ¼ndigt, dass er das gerne in eine MAGA-Propagandamaschine verwandeln wÃ¼rde. Und die EUâKommission kann bis heute die Frage nicht beantworten, ob wir in Europa denn weiter das chinesische TikTok behalten oder die amerikanische Wahlkampfpropaganda bekommen.

Macht es Ã¼berhaupt noch einen Unterschied? Bereits jetzt zeigt TikTok neuen Nutzern mehr rechtsextremen Content als alles andere. Im deutschen Bundestagswahlkampf kamen ganze 78Â Prozent des Inhaltes, der neutralen Nutzern, neuen Nutzern ausgespielt wurde, von der AfD. Bei der Stichwahl in Polen war das VerhÃ¤ltnis fÃ¼nf zu eins fÃ¼r den rechtsextremen Kandidaten.

EuropÃ¤ische BÃ¼rgerinnen und BÃ¼rger fragen mich jeden Tag, warum sie sich diese GehirnwÃ¤sche gefallen lassen mÃ¼ssen. Und dann wird der Ruf nach einem europÃ¤ischen Gesetz laut. Aber das VerrÃ¼ckte daran ist doch: Wir haben dieses Gesetz. Aber die Kommission weigert sich, es wirklich anzuwenden. Der DSA sagt klipp und klar: Wenn groÃe Digitalplattformen ein systemisches Risiko fÃ¼r Ã¶ffentlichen Diskurs und Wahlen auslÃ¶sen, dann muss die Kommission handeln. Das tut sie aber nicht, weil sie Angst hat, genauso wie viele Regierungen. Demokratie ist, wenn die Leute sich ihren Inhalt selber aussuchen kÃ¶nnen auf TikTok, nicht wenn Elon Musk oder die chinesische Regierung den vorgibt â genau das! Und Sie ducken sich weg und machen den Weg frei fÃ¼r diesen digital gesteuerten Faschismus.

Frau Kommissarin, es ist Ihre Aufgabe, fÃ¼r Demokratie, Meinungsfreiheit und Medienvielfalt auch im Internet zu sorgen, indem Sie dafÃ¼r sorgen, dass die Menschen wieder das auf den Plattformen sehen, was sie sich wirklich ausgesucht haben und nicht irgendwelche Tech Bros oder die chinesische Regierung. Und handeln Sie jetzt, denn bald ist es zu spÃ¤t.

Â 
Â Â

Â Â Leila Chaibi, au nom du groupe The Left. â Monsieur le PrÃ©sident, depuis quelques mois, la souverainetÃ© numÃ©rique europÃ©enne est sur toutes les lÃ¨vres Ã  Bruxelles. Mais dans les faits, la Commission europÃ©enne est soumise Ã  Trump. Elle agit en vassal des Ãtats-Unis: câest une Commission qui refuse de taxer les GAFAM, qui voulait nommer une ancienne cadre de Facebook Ã  la tÃªte de sa politique de concurrence et qui dÃ©tricote sa propre rÃ©gulation pour plaire Ã  Washington.

Toujours avec le mÃªme refrain: les GAFAM se plaignent, Washington ordonne, Bruxelles obÃ©it.

Mais la souverainetÃ© numÃ©rique, cela ne se dÃ©crÃ¨te pas. Elle se construit en instaurant une vÃ©ritable prÃ©fÃ©rence europÃ©enne, en soutenant nos propres infrastructures et nos services publics numÃ©riques et en taxant enfin les profits colossaux des gÃ©ants de l'internet. Une souverainetÃ© numÃ©rique au service des EuropÃ©ens, non soumise aux actionnaires de la SiliconÂ Valley ni aux fantasmes de milliardaires pro-Trump: voilÃ  la souverainetÃ© que nous dÃ©fendons.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Christine Anderson, on behalf of the ESN Group. â Mr President, five years ago, the Commission promised a Europe fit for the digital age. We were to have worldâclass chips, sovereign clouds and 10Â 000 green data centres humming in harmony. The Chips Act, the Data Act, the AI Act: the Commission seems convinced that if only they regulated innovation hard enough, it would just magically come into existence.

Today, the dream of chip sovereignty remains a mere PowerPoint slide. Yet amid all these noble failures, there is one area where the EU truly excels: controlling and restricting speech. The Digital Services Act has turned platforms into pre-emptive censors. Saying the wrong things online will actually now land you in jail. Congratulations for that! And now chat control â mass scanning of private messages â looms on the dystopian EU horizon.

With your digital rules, you do not seek to protect European sovereignty. You seek to protect yourself from scrutiny and people's justified criticism. Instead of a digital future that empowers citizens, you are building a system to intimidate and silence them. You are indeed creating a totalitarian hellhole. Thank you for your attention to this matter.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â DÃ³ra DÃ¡vid (PPE). â Tisztelt ElnÃ¶k Ãr! A versenykÃ©pessÃ©gÃ¼nk, az Ã©letmÃ³dunk Ã©s az Ã©rtÃ©keink a tÃ©t. NyomÃ³s okaink vannak arra, hogy miÃ©rt hoztuk meg a digitÃ¡lis jogszabÃ¡lyainkat. Â Â Christel Schaldemose (S&D). â Hr. formand! Vores verden er mere digital nu end nogensinde fÃ¸r. Og for at vi ikke skal have et vildt Vesten, er der altsÃ¥ brug for regulering, og det har vi heldigvis i EU. DSA'en er et historisk skridt i den retning. Men arbejdet stopper ikke der. Europa er under pres fra udenlandske techgiganter og fra udenlandske regeringer. Men de skal ikke have lov at styre vores digitale liv. I alt for mange Ã¥r har deres forretningsmodeller drevet rovdrift pÃ¥ europÃ¦ernes data, privatliv og mentale velfÃ¦rd. Og det gÃ¦lder isÃ¦r vores bÃ¸rn. De sociale medier flyder over med afhÃ¦ngighedsskabende indhold, manipulation, reklamer og i Ã¸vrigt andet farligt indhold. Og vores bÃ¸rn, ja, de er blevet afhÃ¦ngige af skÃ¦rmene. Det skal vi ikke acceptere. Vi er nÃ¸dt til at beskytte vores bÃ¸rn. Og her har EU et sÃ¦rligt ansvar. Fru Virkkunen, du og jeg ved, at det er EU, der skal vise vejen. Vi er det eneste sted i verden, der laver digitale regler med borgerne i centrum. Derfor skal vi ogsÃ¥ gÃ¥ et skridt videre i beskyttelsen af vores bÃ¸rn online. Vi skal gÃ¸re afhÃ¦ngighedsskabende design ulovligt. Vi har brug for en fÃ¦lleseuropÃ¦isk aldersgrÃ¦nse for sociale medier. Mange lande er i gang. Danmark har i gÃ¥r foreslÃ¥et en aldersgrÃ¦nse pÃ¥ 15 Ã¥r. Vi er nÃ¸dt til at sige det klart: Udenlandske techgiganter og udenlandske regeringer skal ikke have lov til at diktere vores digitale liv og isÃ¦r ikke vores bÃ¸rns digitale liv. Vi har brug for regler.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Anders Vistisen (PfE). â MrÂ President, the so-called 'chat control' proposal is nothing less than an EU attempt to read every private message, monitor every conversation and decide what speech is acceptable. They claim it's about protecting citizens, but in reality, it's about controlling citizens.

Once governments gain the power to scan your emails and your private chats, freedom of expression is gone â our democratic debate is finished.

These essential freedom rights are now under attack from the EU â a Union who apparently have an ambition to build its own digital surveillance state, censoring dissent, and all doing it under the banner of safety.

I say no bureaucrat in Brussels should ever have the right to spy on private communication for free Danish or European citizens.

We must defend privacy, not destroy it. We must defend liberty, not sacrifice it to the technocrats.

Threat control must be stopped before Ursula von der Leyen and Mette Frederiksen turn every smartphone in our pockets into government spyware.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Stefano Cavedagna (ECR). â Signor Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, premetto che non accetto alcun tipo di morale su quella che Ã¨ la democrazia, quello che Ã¨ lo Stato di diritto, quando quest'Aula proprio ieri ha deciso di calpestare lo Stato di diritto e di consentire l'immunitÃ  parlamentare ad una persona come Ilaria Salis. Quindi non accettiamo alcun tipo di commento e di morale nei nostri confronti.

Dico perÃ² una cosa: troppo tempo le norme che sono state approvate a livello europeo finiscono per tappare la bocca a qualcuno, tendenzialmente a chi sta antipatico a una certa sinistra presente in quest'Aula. Ã per questo che, pur di attaccare Donald Trump o gli Stati Uniti, finite per consegnarci mani e piedi alle politiche e alle strategie digitali sviluppate in Cina, che sicuramente non Ã¨ una realtÃ  liberal-democratica.

Ã per questo che dobbiamo sviluppare una sovranitÃ  tecnologica europea, cercando di capire una differenza tra chi sono i nostri alleati e chi invece non si dimostra un nostro alleato. Quindi sÃ¬, lavorando e tenendo assieme l'Occidente unito, dobbiamo sviluppare delle garanzie di sovranitÃ  tecnologica nel nostro continente.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â MichaÅ Kobosko (Renew). â MrÂ President,Â dear colleagues, there can be no doubt whether our digital rules should be properly enforced. There is no place for bargaining when it comes to the interests of our citizens and our businesses.

Presidents â including US presidents â come and go. As a person who worked many years on developing transatlantic relations, I can tell you: 'keep calm and make Europe competitive'. The US will be back. Our relations have to be based on mutual respect and equal treatment.

In the meantime, we have to focus on our own digital economy. We need an ambitious vision for building a European digital ecosystem from connectivity through AI, semiconductors, cloud infrastructure and data centres. We need comprehensive industrial policy investments in the base layer of the digital infrastructure, and driving demand for the European alternatives to the hyperscalers.

It is key that we keep competitiveness at the heart of this process and we do it together with our own digital industry. That is why we need to embrace the EuroStack concept and make it finally happen.

(The speaker agreed to take a blue-card question)

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â President. â (LukasÂ Sieper held up a blue card in paper form)

MrÂ Sieper,Â if you want a blue card, you should push the button.

(Lukas Sieper responded off mic)

Yes, I can understand, but that's why we have the electronic system. I like that you are trying to play with the rules of procedure. Your diligent study of the rules is highly appreciated, but I would really like to ask you to use the electronic system. That's why we use the taxpayers' money to install it.

You did it, okay.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Lukas Sieper (NI), blue-card question. â Excuse me for this little hint on the matter of digital competence in all of this!

Thank you, dear colleague, for accepting my blue card, whether it was raised on paper or digitally. I listened very carefully to you, and I agree that this is all a question of competitiveness and often the modern digital environment. At the same time, I know that our citizens fear many of the things that we want to do, and especially chat control is used by populists and extremists in this House to stir these fears. So regarding this topic, what alternatives do you see on chat control?

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â MichaÅ Kobosko (Renew), blue-card answer. â I'm not sure if I fully understood your question, frankly speaking, perhaps because it was not a digital one! But as I said, I do believe that we have no other choice than to develop our own digital solutions and that we have to do it now, do it immediately, because, as the Draghi report stated, we are so dependent that we are fully not independent when it comes to the digital solutions. So I know, and I can hear the populist voices, this is not about censorship, this is about our digital existence. That is the real issue on the table.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Kim Van Sparrentak (Verts/ALE). â MrÂ President, Commissioner, colleagues, it's pretty clear that the Trump administration and his tech-bro cheer squad aren't fans of our digital rulebook. But before the Commission listens to the tech lobby and decides to chainsaw Europe's AI and privacy rules, let's do a little fact check.

AI to detect diseases and discover new medicines? Perfectly allowed under European tech rules and not covered by the AI Act.

AI to make production processes more sustainable and less wasteful? Allowed under European rules and not high-risk under the AI Act.

Using AI and its outputs in research and innovation? Completely exempted from the AI Act â and, actually, we encourage all of this.

Sure, some things are not allowed. AI companionship, or chatbots recommending you commit suicide? No, that's not allowed under European tech rules.

LinkedIn and Meta using all your data to train their AI systems unless you opt out (but they're making it really hard)? No, that's also not allowed under European tech rules.

Because in Europe we ensure that AI is safe, and technology actually contributes to people, society and the environment, while fostering innovation.

So, the question is: will the Commission be fooled by tech CEOs and their top lobbyist, Donald Trump? Or will the Commission stand up for European tech companies and the citizens of Europe? Because don't be fooled: the people who will benefit from slashing AI or privacy rules are big tech, and not your promising, innovative EU companies.

With clear and strong rules, Europe will create the best, most safe and most accurate AI in the world, without being locked into systems that turn out to fail, steal or discriminate. Rules foster innovation.

Â 
Â Â 
Â

Î§ÏÎµÎ¹Î±Î¶ÏÎ¼Î±ÏÏÎµ ÏÎ·ÏÎ¹Î±ÎºÎ® ÎºÏÏÎ¹Î±ÏÏÎ¯Î± Î¼Îµ Î´Î¹Î±ÏÎ¬Î½ÎµÎ¹Î± ÎºÎ±Î¹ ÎºÎ¿Î¹Î½ÏÎ½Î¹ÎºÏ ÏÏÎ¿ÏÎ±Î½Î±ÏÎ¿Î»Î¹ÏÎ¼Ï, ÎºÎ±Î¹ ÏÏÎ¹ Î¼Î¹Î± ÎÏÏÏÏÎ· ÏÎ¿Î²Î¹ÎºÎ® Î±ÏÎ­Î½Î±Î½ÏÎ¹ ÏÏÎ·Î½ ÏÎµÏÎ½Î¿Î»Î¿Î³Î¹ÎºÎ® ÏÏÏÎ¿Î´Î¿ ÎºÎ±Î¹ ÎµÎ¾Î±ÏÏÎ·Î¼Î­Î½Î· Î±ÏÏ ÏÎµÏÎ½Î¿Î»Î¿Î³Î¹ÎºÎ¿ÏÏ ÎºÎ¿Î»Î¿ÏÏÎ¿ÏÏ ÎµÎºÏÏÏ ÏÏÎ½ ÏÏÎ½ÏÏÏÎ½ ÏÎ·Ï. Î ÏÎ·ÏÎ¹Î±ÎºÎ® Î±Î½Î¬ÏÏÏÎ¾Î· Î´ÎµÎ½ Î¼ÏÎ¿ÏÎµÎ¯ Î½Î± ÏÏÎ¬ÏÎ¾ÎµÎ¹ ÏÏÏÎ¯Ï ÏÏÎ¿ÏÏÎ±ÏÎ¯Î± ÏÏÎ½ Î´Î¹ÎºÎ±Î¹ÏÎ¼Î¬ÏÏÎ½ Î¼Î±Ï ÎºÎ±Î¹ ÏÏÏÎ¯Ï Î¾ÎµÎºÎ¬Î¸Î±ÏÎ· ÏÏÎ®ÏÎ· ÏÏÎ½ Î±Î»Î³Î¿ÏÎ¯Î¸Î¼ÏÎ½. ÎÎ·ÏÎ¬Î¼Îµ ÎµÏÎµÎ½Î´ÏÏÎµÎ¹Ï ÏÎµ Î´Î·Î¼ÏÏÎ¹ÎµÏ ÏÏÎ¿Î´Î¿Î¼Î­Ï ÎºÎ±Î¹ Î±Î½Î¿Î¹ÏÏÎ­Ï ÏÎµÏÎ½Î¿Î»Î¿Î³Î¯ÎµÏ, ÏÏÎ®ÏÎ¹Î¾Î· ÏÏÎ½ Î¼Î¹ÎºÏÎ¿Î¼ÎµÏÎ±Î¯ÏÎ½ ÎºÎ±Î¹ ÎºÎ±Î¹Î½Î¿ÏÏÎ¼ÏÎ½ ÎµÏÎ¹ÏÎµÎ¹ÏÎ®ÏÎµÏÎ½, ÏÏÏÎµ Î· ÏÎ·ÏÎ¹Î±ÎºÎ® Î¼ÎµÏÎ¬Î²Î±ÏÎ· Î½Î± Î³Î¯Î½ÎµÏÎ±Î¹ Î´Î¯ÎºÎ±Î¹Î± ÎºÎ±Î¹ ÏÏÏÎ¯Ï Î±ÏÎ¿ÎºÎ»ÎµÎ¹ÏÎ¼Î¿ÏÏ. Î ÏÎµÏÎ½Î¿Î»Î¿Î³Î¯Î± Î¿ÏÎµÎ¯Î»ÎµÎ¹ Î½Î± ÏÏÎ·ÏÎµÏÎµÎ¯ ÏÎ¿ÏÏ Î±Î½Î¸ÏÏÏÎ¿ÏÏ ÎºÎ±Î¹ Î½Î± Î´Î·Î¼Î¹Î¿ÏÏÎ³ÎµÎ¯ ÎµÏÎºÎ±Î¹ÏÎ¯ÎµÏ, ÎºÎ±Î¹ ÏÏÎ¹ Î½Î± ÏÎ¿ÏÏ ÏÎ±ÏÎ±ÎºÎ¿Î»Î¿ÏÎ¸ÎµÎ¯.

Â Â Stine Bosse (Renew), blue-card question. â I actually do agree with the first two or three sentences that you had in your speech that we are living in completely different times, and there are many things that we have to get used to using these devices. But I want to understand, could you tell us, amongst the biggest companies in the world â because you said it's impossible for the companies to obey the rules that we set out here to protect our citizens â can you name how many of the 10Â biggest companies in the world are tech companies?

Â Â

Â Â Zsuzsanna BorvendÃ©g (ESN), kÃ©kkÃ¡rtyÃ¡s vÃ¡lasz. â A legnagyobb amerikai cÃ©gekre gondoltam elsÅsorban: az Apple, a Microsoft, de egyÃ©bkÃ©nt ott van a francia nagy Mistral nevÅ± cÃ©g is, amelynek a felhÅalapÃº tÃ¡rolÃ³helyeihez gyakorlatilag az amerikai Ã¡llam, az amerikai titkosszolgÃ¡latok abszolÃºt hozzÃ¡fÃ©rhetnek. Suverenita, svrchovanost je pojmovÃ½ znak stÃ¡tÅ¯. Evropa ani ve smyslu EvropskÃ© unie nenÃ­ stÃ¡t, tudÃ­Å¾ nemÃ¡ suverenitu. ZejmÃ©na v Äase voleb je bohuÅ¾el digitÃ¡lnÃ­ prostor zahlcen produkty politickÃ½ch entit placenÃ½ch z unijnÃ­ch grantÅ¯, ze zdrojÅ¯ spÅÃ¡telenÃ½ch ambasÃ¡d Äi nÄkterÃ© ze stran informaÄnÃ­ vÃ¡lky probÃ­hajÃ­cÃ­ ve SpojenÃ½ch stÃ¡tech.

A absence zdrÅ¾enlivosti evropskÃ½ch institucÃ­ vÄetnÄ tohoto Parlamentu pÅi komentovÃ¡nÃ­ nÃ¡rodnÃ­ch voleb se stÃ¡vÃ¡ legendÃ¡rnÃ­. NemÃ¡ smysl, kolegovÃ©, hledat technickÃ¡ ÅeÅ¡enÃ­ na nÄco, co je projevem nedostatku respektu a politickÃ© kultury ve vztahu ke svrchovanosti jednotlivÃ½ch ÄlenskÃ½ch stÃ¡tÅ¯.
