Â Â Marit Maij (S&D). â Voorzitter, commissaris, maar liefst 50Â miljoen mensen leven en werken vandaag in moderne slavernij. Ook in Europa zijn er mensen die in erbarmelijke arbeidsomstandigheden werken en worden uitgebuit. Tegelijkertijd organiseren we een top in een land als Qatar, een land dat bekend staat om de uitbuiting van arbeiders. Is het dan rechtvaardig om deze top uitgerekend in Qatar te houden, terwijl we weten dat daar grove mensenrechtenschendingen plaatsvinden?

We spreken over sociale ontwikkeling. We moeten aan de slag op deze Wereldtop over Sociale Ontwikkeling. Tegelijkertijd stellen we vast dat meer dan honderd landen zo diep in de schulden zitten dat ze moeten bezuinigen op sociale ontwikkeling. Zij moeten bezuinigen op zorg en op onderwijs en het gevolg van die grote bezuinigingen is een wereldwijde gender poverty gap. We zien immers dat het vaak vrouwen zijn die werken in de zorg, de sociale zekerheid en de onderwijssector â en zij verliezen hun banen.

We moeten ervoor zorgen dat er meer financiÃ«le middelen vanuit de sociale ontwikkelingsprogrammaâs naar vrouwenrechten en vrouwenorganisaties gaan, zodat we SDGÂ 5, de duurzameontwikkelingsdoelstelling voor gendergelijkheid, snel kunnen behalen.

Â Â Ioan-RareÅ Bogdan (PPE). â Domnule preÈedinte, ExcelenÈele Voastre, Ã®n august 1963, pe treptele Memorialului Lincoln, Martin Luther King a rostit celebrul discurs âI Have a Dreamâ, la o sutÄ de ani dupÄ ce Abraham Lincoln proclama sfÃ¢rÈitul sclaviei.

Èi energia este scumpÄ, deÈi piesele pentru Ã®ntreÈinerea ei sunt ieftine, chinezeÈti.

DacÄ am cumpÄra Ã®n primul rÃ¢nd european Èi am angaja la fel, nu pentru cÄ decide cineva, ci pentru cÄ aÈa ne putem ridica din nou, am schimba lucrurile radical, aici, acasÄ, Ã®n Europa.

Am Èi eu un vis: scoatem fermierii europeni din depresia provocatÄ Ã®n mare mÄsurÄ tot de noi, amÃ¢nÄm interzicerea maÈinilor cu combustie pÃ¢nÄ Ètim sigur cÄ tranziÈia verde nu Ã®nseamnÄ milioane de Èomeri, folosim resursele europene sÄ producem alimente, medicamente, jucÄrii, mai ales jucÄrii, pentru cÄ ar trebui sÄ construim aceastÄ lume Ã®n care vrem sÄ eradicÄm sÄrÄcia, aici, acasÄ, Ã®n Europa noastrÄ, pentru europeni.

Â 
Â Â 
Â Â 

Catch-the-eye procedure

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Sebastian Tynkkynen (ECR). â Mr President,Â the Second World Summit for Social Development will be held in Qatar, in a country which still, in the year 2025, implements the Sharia law.

Women under the Sharia law are like puppets to men. Women can't marry freely. They can't travel freely. They can't even say no to sex with their husband without a 'legitimate reason'. When a woman gets raped, she can get prosecuted for having sex outside of marriage. This is sick and insane.

What message is sent to the women in the world by organising a summit for social development in a country like Qatar?

Will gender equality be praised with the royal family, while women in the country are treated like slaves?

To me, social development and Qatar don't belong in the same sentence.

Â 
Â Â 
Â Â 

(End of catch-the-eye procedure)

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Jessika Roswall, Member of the Commission. â MrÂ President, honourable Members, let me start by saying thanks again for having me on this debate and also thank you in particular to MsÂ Li Andersson and all the shadow rapporteurs for your work on this resolution.

It is a strong contribution, and it echoes many of the priorities that the Commission is already pursuing that I also mentioned in my first intervention. We have taken good note of your points that were raised in the debate, and in the preparation for the summit, the EU and its Member States took a strong role in negotiating a political declaration that is a true call to action: to eliminate poverty in all forms and all dimensions and to ensure decent work for all.

With the declaration now finalised, we can be proud that there is a strong commitment at a global level to fully realising the fundamental principles and rights of work, strengthening social protection systems, tackling informality, reducing inequalities within and among countries, fighting all forms of discrimination and ensuring access to education, healthcare and housing.

I know that you know I am not going to the summit, but I can assure you that my colleague, Commissioner MÃ®nzatu, is really looking forward to going. Europe's voice in the summit is a strong voice, which is strong because it is united. And we look forward to working with you to make sure that 30 years from now, we can say with pride that we did not waste this moment.

Â 
Â Â 
Â Â 

PRZEWODNICTWO: EWA KOPACZ
WiceprzewodniczÄca

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â PrzewodniczÄca. â Na zakoÅczenie debaty otrzymaÅam, zgodnie z art. 136 ust. 2 Regulaminu, jeden projekt rezolucji.

Zamykam debatÄ.

GÅosowanie odbÄdzie siÄ w czwartek 9 paÅºdziernika 2025 r.

Â 

16. EU strategy with regard to Iranâs nuclear threat and the implementation of EU sanctions resulting from the snapback mechanism (debate)
Video of the speeches
Â 

Â Â PrzewodniczÄca. â Kolejnym punktem porzÄdku obrad jest oÅwiadczenie Rady i Komisji: Strategia UE wobec zagroÅ¼enia jÄdrowego ze strony Iranu oraz wykonanie sankcji unijnych wynikajÄcych z klauzuli przywrÃ³cenia wczeÅniejszych warunkÃ³w (2025/2903(RSP)).

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Marie Bjerre, President-in-Office of the Council. â MadamÂ President, honourable Members, Commissioner, during the debate on the situation in the Middle East in July, I recalled what has been for a long time our position on the Iranian nuclear issue: Iran must never be allowed to acquire a nuclear weapon, and Iran must comply with these legally binding obligations under the NonâProliferation Treaty.

I also expressed concerns on the announcement that the Iranian Government had put cooperation with the International Atomic Energy Agency on hold. As you know, the EU has been engaged in diplomatic efforts on Iran's nuclear programme for many years. Our efforts paved the way to Iran agreeing to limit its nuclear programme; the agreement was the Joint Comprehensive Plan of Action. Since then, it is clear that Iran has departed from its commitments, as evidenced by more than 60 IAEA reports over six years. The Iranian nuclear programme lacks any credible civilian justification and is unprecedented for a state without a nuclear weapons programme.

Also, for more than five years, the International Atomic Energy Agency could not carry out important verification and monitoring activities. The situation has led France, Germany and the UK to notify the United Nations Security Council of Iran's nonâcompliance under theÂ Joint Comprehensive Plan of Action. With the notification, they invoke the process known as the snapback mechanism. Intensive diplomatic efforts with Iran during a 30-day timeframe foreseen in the snapback process took place. Unfortunately, the result of these efforts did not allow for a diplomatic solution as Iran did not engage constructively.

As a consequence, the UN Security Council has reimposed all nuclearârelated UN sanctions and restrictions against Iran. The EU has already reimposed all UN and also relevant EU sanctions that we previously lifted. We call on the international community to implement these sanctions.

Let me be clear, the snapback of sanctions and nuclear restrictions does not mean the end of diplomacy with Iran. In fact, a sustainable solution to the Iranian nuclear issue can only be achieved through negotiations and diplomacy. But the prerequisite is that Iran returns to the negotiation table in a meaningful way. The EU, together with partners in the region, will continue to engage in this key issue for regional and international security.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Jessika Roswall, Member of the Commission. â MadamÂ President, honourable Members of the European Parliament, MinisterÂ Bjerre, 10Â years ago, the Joint Comprehensive Plan of Action (JCPOA) established a framework to ensure that the Iranian programme remained exclusively peaceful, putting in place an effective monitoring and verification system implemented by the International Atomic Energy Agency. This represents a key element of the global nuclear nonâproliferation architecture, as well as an achievement of multilateralism and nuclear diplomacy.

The EU has been supportive of the JCPOA and has fully implemented it, despite unprecedented challenges, including the departure of the US from the agreement in 2018, the reimposition of the extension of US sanctions, as well as the subsequent actions taken by Iran in contradiction to its JCPOA commitments.

The reinstated UN sanctions covers three main categories. First, the nuclear program itself, which would again be under a binding obligation to suspend all enrichmentârelated and reprocessing activities. Second, Iran's ballistic missile and military capabilities. And third, financial and economic restrictions.Â We call on the international community to implement these sanctions.

The EU is implementing the return of UN sanctions along with EU autonomous restrictive measures. This has been done under Iran's weapons of mass destruction regime. Our EU measures include, first, travel bans for individuals, asset freezes for individuals and entities, and in line with this, the prohibition on making funds or economic resources available to those listed.

Second, economic and financial sanctions covering the trade, financial and transport sectors. Allow me to mention that the EU never lifted the arms embargo, nor the listing of the Iranian Revolutionary Guards in line with the mechanism of the JCPOA.Â The European Commission will follow closely the implementation of the reimposed sanctions.

EU sanctions are targeted and designed to avoid unintended negative consequences to the Iranian population. EU sanctions are not meant to stand in the way nor impede the supply of humanitarian aid, including medical assistance. EU measures comply with all obligations under international law, in particular international human rights law, international refugee law and international humanitarian law.

With the reimposition of all previously lifted UN and EU sanctions, we are now in a postâJCPOA phase. As the HR/VP has made clear, this postâJCPOA phase will not mark the end of diplomacy with Iran on the nuclear issue. We continue to believe that a sustainable solution to the Iranian nuclear issue, which remains a key challenge to the region and international security, can only be achieved through dialogue and diplomacy.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Javier Zarzalejos, on behalf of the PPE Group. â Madam President, Commissioner, Iran is in serious breach of basic obligations with regard to its nuclear programme. But this is hardly news.

The introduction of sanctions is therefore both welcome and long overdue. The sanctions should be meaningful, fully enforced and closely monitored.

But let's not forget that this is not the issue. The real issue is the Iranian regime, which is a challenge that requires an entirely new approach of our policy towards Teheran.

With the Iranian regime in place, there will be no peace in the region. The Ayatollahs took good care to destroy the Abraham Accords through their proxies: Hamas, Houthis and Hezbollah. And now, as the new opportunity for peace emerges in the region, Tehran will do whatever they can to disrupt the peace process.

With the Iranian regime in place Russia will continue to have a useful ally in its aggression against Ukraine. With the Iranian regime in place, brutal repression with no legal or moral limits will continue to fall on anyone who dares to challenge the tyranny. Over 1000 people executed in the first nine months of 2025.

So at this point, let me ask, why do Iranian ambassadors live so comfortably in European capitals? And why is the listing of the Revolutionary Guard as a terrorist organisation still not included in the sanctions adopted, despite the consistent and strong request from this Parliament?

Dear colleagues, many Iranians inside and outside Iran risked their lives. They deserve to be supported, to be protected and to share their commitment for a free and democratic Iran.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Lucia Annunziata, on behalf of the S&D Group. â Madam President, in 2025, Iran's inflation is up to 45Â %. Youth unemployment is up to 20Â %, while 30Â % of the population is below the poverty threshold. Economic crisis due to the sanctions have reduced the regime consensus, and the defeat of Hezbollah by Israel has ruined Iran's major project of expansion towards the West.

Iran is weaker than ever, and yet can still count on a solid alliance with Russia, and particularly also with China, which are the main buyers of Iran's oil.

The West at this point has two options: taking advantage of this crisis, but risking a major escalation, or making Iran part of a larger, what we once called a 'comprehensive peace process', a process that could involve and stabilise all the major players of the region, Israel, Arab nations and Iran and global players as US, Europe and China.

It is the type of process that I like to feel that a little bit catch Ms Kallas in her recent statement.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Juan Carlos Girauta Vidal, en nombre del Grupo PfE. â SeÃ±ora presidenta, el rÃ©gimen teocrÃ¡tico islamista de IrÃ¡n es el principal agente desestabilizador en Oriente PrÃ³ximo y el mayor promotor del terrorismo internacional. Un sicario suyo atentÃ³ contra la vida de don Alejo Vidal-Quadras, que fue presidente de esta CÃ¡mara durante muchos aÃ±os; estÃ¡ vivo de milagro.

La agresiva polÃ­tica exterior de los ayatolÃ¡s lleva aÃ±os atentando contra la paz y la seguridad a travÃ©s de agentes subsidiarios como HamÃ¡s, HizbulÃ¡ o los huzÃ­es. Tiene vÃ­nculos con Rusia, con las tiranÃ­as caribeÃ±as narcosocialistas... Sus afiladas garras llegan a Europa, donde patrocinan atentados, islamismo y hasta grupos de extrema izquierda. No respeta los derechos mÃ¡s bÃ¡sicos de su propio pueblo, cebÃ¡ndose con especial saÃ±a en mujeres disidentes y en homosexuales. Hay cuatro âÂ¡cuatro!â ejecuciones al dÃ­a.

No deberÃ­a encontrar en Europa mÃ¡s que rechazo. Â¡Basta de apaciguamiento! Hay que neutralizar la amenaza que supondrÃ­a para el mundo un IrÃ¡n con capacidad nuclear militar. Parece mentira que la anterior ComisiÃ³n amparara sus siniestros planes con la estafa del uso civil. Â¿Acaso no se daba cuenta el seÃ±orÂ Borrell? Â¿No se daba cuenta la ComisiÃ³n de los niveles de enriquecimiento de uranio?

La resistencia iranÃ­ lleva exponiendo esta amenaza desde hace treinta y cuatroÂ aÃ±os. Suyas fueron las revelaciones de instalaciones nucleares secretas en Natanz y Arak en el aÃ±o 2002. Sus proyectos incluyen borrar a Israel del mapa; han sido felizmente frustrados, pero ojo, mantengÃ¡monos firmes o acabarÃ¡ siendo una cuestiÃ³n de hechos consumados.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Nathalie Loiseau, au nom du groupe Renew. â Madame la PrÃ©sidente, qui voudrait dâun Iran dotÃ© de lâarme nuclÃ©aire? Soyons clairs: personne. Depuis des dÃ©cennies, le rÃ©gime iranien poursuit deux obsessions: la destruction dâIsraÃ«l et lâaccÃ¨s Ã  lâarme atomique. Il est Ã©vident que nous ne pouvons pas le laisser faire.

Partant de ce constat, lâEurope a agi en responsabilitÃ©. Sans les EuropÃ©ens, il nây aurait pas eu de nÃ©gociations et lâaccord de Vienne aurait manquÃ© de fermetÃ©. GrÃ¢ce au plan dâaction global commun, les activitÃ©s dâenrichissement iraniennes Ã©taient Ã  la fois limitÃ©es et contrÃ´lÃ©es.

Malheureusement, nous nâen sommes plus lÃ . Donald Trump, en se retirant du plan dâaction global commun, a donnÃ© Ã  lâIran tous les prÃ©textes pour ne plus le respecter. TÃ©hÃ©ran a dÃ©veloppÃ© un programme dâenrichissement de lâuranium que seules des ambitions militaires pourraient justifier. En frappant lâIran, IsraÃ«l et les Ãtats-Unis ont, certes, affaibli et retardÃ© ce programme, mais malheureusement, ils ne lâont pas anÃ©anti.

Aujourdâhui, le risque de prolifÃ©ration, au Moyen-Orient comme ailleurs dans le monde, est rÃ©el et la question du nuclÃ©aire iranien nâest pas vraiment rÃ©solue. TÃ©hÃ©ran aurait pu choisir de donner des gages; il a prÃ©fÃ©rÃ© tergiverser. Nous, EuropÃ©ens, devons continuer Ã  agir en responsabilitÃ© et rÃ©tablir les sanctions, car il est impensable de laisser lâIran se doter de lâarme nuclÃ©aire.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Hannah Neumann, on behalf of the Verts/ALE Group. â Madam President, finally: the snapback. For too long, the Iranian regime bent the rules, mocked our patience and profited from our hesitation â that, at least, is over now.

But sanctions only matter if they are fully implemented. So we need to close the loopholes that EU companies still use for dual-use goods, end the golden visa schemes that the regime still exploits, ensure that countries fully comply as UN sanctions apply to everyone and above all, sanctions must hit the regime, not the people.

Dear colleagues, this snapback alone will not bring security. The regime still hides enriched uranium, it supplies Shahed drones to Russia, killing Ukrainians, and expects fighter jets in return, threatening Saudi Arabia and Israel â a partnership of repression that fuels instability far beyond Iran's borders.

Inside Iran, repression gets worse. Three people are executed every day, each one a message of fear to a generation that dares to dream of freedom. Political prisoners are on hunger strike, risking their lives to end this madness. They need our attention and our solidarity.

The best way to stop a nuclear Iran is to break this cycle of violence and fear that sustains the regime is to finally put the IRGC on the EU terrorist list. Our support belongs not to the mullahs, but to the people in Iran who risk everything for freedom and dignity.

(The speaker concluded in a non-EU language)

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Marc Botenga, on behalf of The Left Group. â MadamÂ President, dear colleagues, you say you care about non-proliferation and human rights, but if we look at the Middle East, why do we accept that Israel has a nuclear bomb? This is absolutely unacceptable. You call Iran the main threat, but look at Israel bombing Lebanon, Syria, Iraq, Iran, Yemen. What else? Qatar recently.

Secondly, why did you not condemn or even mention the fact that it was the United States that pulled out of the nuclear deal? It was not Iran. It was the United States pulling out.

Finally, why did we not condemn, as the European Union, the illegal US-Israeli strikes on Iran, which made a lot of civilian casualties and which made nuclear diplomacy more difficult, not less difficult. Basically, we are worsening the situation, and we are imposing sanctions that hurt ordinary Iranians, who have more difficulties now to buy medicine and to buy basic goods. We are saying, 'Hey, we're the good guys. We care about nuclear non-proliferation and about human rights.' While our policies do exactly the same.

Let us be honest â and I will conclude on this â our policies have only one goal, and that is weakening Iran in order to turn it into a new Syria, a new Lebanon, a new Iraq. That the people of the region will not stand for.

Â 
Â Â 
Â

Podczas ostatniego szczytu Szanghajskiej Organizacji WspÃ³Åpracy zobaczyliÅmy ich wszystkich: Chiny i paÅstwa osi zÅa razem, coraz bardziej zdeterminowane, deklarujÄce zacieÅnienie wspÃ³Åpracy. Zerwanie wspÃ³Åpracy z MiÄdzynarodowÄ AgencjÄ Energii Atomowej, odmawianie wglÄdu w program nuklearny, brak poddawania siÄ kontrolom stanowi zagroÅ¼enie dla Åwiata. Minister Sikorski podczas ostatniego zgromadzenia ogÃ³lnego ONZ mÃ³wiÅ i wezwaÅ szefa iraÅskiego MSZ do powrotu do rozmÃ³w z agencjÄ energii atomowej. OdwaÅ¼na decyzja Francji, Niemiec, Wielkiej Brytanii o uruchomieniu mechanizmu snapback, przywrÃ³ceniu sankcji to pokaz stanowczoÅci Unii Europejskiej. ChcÄ bardzo wyraÅºnie powiedzieÄ: Potrzebujemy wzmocnienia stanowiska transatlantyckiego. Potrzebujemy rÃ³wnieÅ¼ wzmocnienia budowy strategicznych partnerstw Unii Europejskiej na rzecz bezpieczeÅstwa i obrony. To musi byÄ nasza twarda postawa.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Daniel Attard (S&D). â MadamÂ President, Iran is no longer simply breaching the nuclear deal, but is intentionally dismantling it. Uranium enrichment has surged, international inspections are restricted, and threats to regional stability are growing. And as such, the EU was right to support the triggering of the snapback mechanism. But unless we enforce these sanctions fully and consistently, we send the wrong message â that Europe will respond only on paper. Sanctions must be coordinated, visible and enforced by all Member States.

And let us be clear, those enabling Iran to bypass international restrictions are not neutral actors, they are undermining global security. This is not about escalation, it is about credibility. If we want diplomacy to succeed, it must be backed by consequences. We, the S&D Group, support dialogue, but dialogue on its own will not stop a nuclear threat.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Rihards Kols (ECR). â Madam President, dear colleagues, Iranâs nuclear programme is no longer just a regional problem â itâs a direct threat to European and global security.

The regime is enriching uranium far beyond any civilian need and shutting out IAEA inspectors â this is not ambiguity, it is escalation.

The E3 triggered the snapback mechanism, all nuclear-related UN sanctions are back in force.

The EU has gone further â freezing Iranian central and commercial banks, cutting off oil and gas imports, banning gold and sensitive equipment, and closing our airspace to Iranian cargo.

This is the right path â but letâs not pretend sanctions matter if theyâre not enforced.

Iran arms Russia, fuels regional proxies, and rejects oversight. Appeasement got us here. Strength is overdue.

And still â still! â the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps is not listed as a terrorist organisation in the EU.

This is the same force that brutalises Iranians, funds terrorism, and supplies weapons used against Ukrainian civilians.

How many times must this Parliament call for the IRGC to be designated as a terrorist organisation before the Council acts?

Both the Council and the Commission say we are for diplomacy. I say yes to diplomacy, but to hard diplomacy.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Helmut BrandstÃ¤tter (Renew). â Frau PrÃ¤sidentin, Frau Kommissarin, Frau Ministerin! Der Iran darf niemals Atomwaffen besitzen. DarÃ¼ber sind wir uns ja hoffentlich einig. Das ist ein Terrorregime, das nicht nur die Opposition, die Menschen, die Frauen im eigenen Land umbringt, terrorisiert und ihnen kein normales Leben ermÃ¶glicht, sondern â und der Herr Kollege Botenga will das nicht verstehen â auch verspricht, alle Menschen in Israel ermorden zu wollen und Israel auszulÃ¶schen. Das ist das Programm der Mullahs, und deswegen ist das nicht irgendein Regime. Dieses Regime darf keine Atomwaffen haben. Wir wissen, jedes diktatorische Regime wird das zur Erpressung anderer verwenden und zu sonst nichts.

Wir wissen â auch das haben die beiden Damen angesprochen â, ja, es gab diesen JCPOAâVersuch, und den hat Trump leider kaputtgemacht. Deswegen muss man natÃ¼rlich versuchenÂ â wenn es eine MÃ¶glichkeit gibtÂ â, irgendwie den Iran zu kontrollieren. Aber eines ist auch wichtig: Diese Revolutionsgarden mÃ¼ssen auf die Sanktionsliste, weil sie die Wirtschaft des Iran beherrschen. Solange wir die nicht auf die Sanktionsliste geben, haben die Sanktionen auch keinen Sinn. Also: Die Terrororganisation der Revolutionsgarden muss sanktioniert werden.

Â Â Marc Botenga (The Left), blue-card question. â Today, what is really happening in the Middle East is that Israel is erasing the Palestinian people. Israel is erasing the Palestinian people from the map in Gaza through a genocide, in the West Bank through ethnic cleansing. Are you ready to condemn that?

My personal belief is that, yes, we need a Middle East without nuclear weapons. This means take away Israeli nuclear weapons and indeed, get a nuclearâweaponâfree zone in the Middle East. But it also means that everyone should respect international law and that we cannot have double standards here by talking about Iran, but giving Israel a free pass.

Do you condemn Israeli genocide, yes or no?

Â Â Helmut BrandstÃ¤tter (Renew), blue-card answer. â First of all, it was Iran financing Hezbollah and Hamas. They told Hamas to do the terrible crimes of 7Â October. And then Israel reacted.

No, I am not in favour of bombing Gaza. But Israel has a right to exist, and it is our job to do everything so that Israel has the possibility to exist. And Iran does not have the possibility to erase all Jews and all Israelis, because that is their plan. And there we have to be very clear.

Â

Â Â Thomas Geisel (NI). â Frau PrÃ¤sidentin, liebe Kolleginnen und Kollegen! Man fragt sich: Warum kommt der sogenannte Snapback eigentlich jetzt? Warum â und darum geht es ja wohl offenbar â mÃ¼ssen ausgerechnet jetzt die Sanktionen gegen den Iran wieder verschÃ¤rft werden, um das Land an der Entwicklung von Atomwaffen zu hindern? Vielleicht mÃ¶chte sich Europa auch beim amerikanischen PrÃ¤sidenten beliebt machen, der ja vom Atomabkommen mit dem Iran noch nie etwas gehalten hat und den man mit derlei Wohlverhalten vielleicht dazu bringen kann, wieder Waffen an die Ukraine zu liefern. Â Â Evin Incir (S&D). â Madam President, no regime must ever succeed in silencing our voices when we speak out against its crimes and violations of international law and human rights, including those committed by the regime in Tehran. The reimposition of the UN sanctions on Iran is a direct response to Tehran's repeated breaches of its nuclear commitments under the 2015 agreement. The European Union must remain fully engaged, ensuring that the implementation of these sanctions is closely and effectively monitored.

The situation in Iran has deteriorated further in recent years, despite the hope ignited by the Jina Revolution. The regime's repression of its people, especially women, remains severe. Minority communities, including Kurds, Baloch, Ahwazi, BahÃ¡Ê¼Ã­s and others, continue to suffer systematic discrimination, compounded by a disturbing rise in executions that has disproportionately affected these groups.

When is the EU going to label the IRGC as a terrorist organisation? We cannot and must not allow intimidation or setbacks to deter us from standing up for peace, justice and dignity.

Â Â Carlo Ciccioli (ECR). â Signor Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, io mi proclamo amico del popolo iraniano ma, dopo dieci anni di sospensione, l'Unione europea ha finalmente deciso di reintrodurre le sanzioni contro il regime iraniano.Â Ã una decisione giusta, necessaria e moralmente doverosa.

Le ambizioni e le attivitÃ  nucleari di Teheran violano gli impegni internazionali e minacciano la sicurezza dell'intero Medio Oriente e dell'Europa stessa. Vogliamo parlare di Hezbollah, di Hamas, dell'attentato all'ex parlamentare europeo Vidal-Quadras nel 2023, giÃ  vicepresidente del Parlamento europeo dal 1999 al 2014?

Con queste misure l'Unione europea dimostra di non accettare piÃ¹ l'inganno e l'arroganza di un regime che usa il dialogo solo per guadagnare tempo e reprimere il proprio popolo. Le sanzioni, voglio sottolinearlo, non sono contro l'Iran, ma contro chi lo opprime. Sono un segnale politico e morale, contro un potere che imprigiona, tortura, uccide i dissidenti e le donne che chiedono libertÃ .

La vera guerra, fin dall'inizio, Ã¨ quella tra il regime e il popolo iraniano, soprattutto contro le donne. Come ricordÃ² Maryam Rajavi, presidente del Consiglio nazionale Resistenza iraniana, la crisi iraniana non si risolve con la compiacenza nÃ© con la guerra. La via Ã¨ il cambiamento. Siamo nelle scelte giuste. Sottolineo di appoggiare questa decisione doverosa dell'Unione europea.

Â Â Petras AuÅ¡treviÄius (Renew). â Madam President, Commissioner, Minister, colleagues, the Tehran regime has deceived the West, including the European Union, by misleading and pretending to be a partner in negotiations regarding its nuclear program.

Last month, Khamenei acknowledged that the uranium enrichment activities began 30 years ago. We must remember that without the initial revelations of the MEK and NCRI, the regime would likely have continued with the nuclear weapons programme and have had those weapons by now. The Iranian people are tired of the mullahs' regime, which has become an agent of religious fanaticism, the suppression of women, the persecution and killing of opponents, and international terrorism.

We must increase political and economic pressure on Tehran. We must cut the regime from the modern technologies and innovations that are used for oppression. Let's agree that only regime change can bring stability to Iran and the region. Let us support the constructive opposition in Iran and their tireless efforts to fight the oppressive regime.

Â Â Reinhold Lopatka (PPE). â Madam President, Iran has crossed every red line by enriching uranium to 90Â % weaponsâgrade levels and advancing its ballistic missile programme in blatant defiance of UN Security Council Resolution 2231. Â Â Diana Iovanovici ÅoÅoacÄ (NI). â DoamnÄ preÈedintÄ, am auzit aici niÈte chestiuni care, pe mine cel puÈin, m-au siderat.

Deci nu au voie decÃ¢t anumite state sÄ deÈinÄ arma nuclearÄ, celelalte nu au voie. Vorbim despre drepturi Èi libertÄÈi Ã®n Iran, dar nu vedem cenzura Èi inchiziÈia din Uniunea EuropeanÄ, condusÄ de Ursula von der Leyen Èi Comisia EuropeanÄ, precum Èi de Parlamentul European. Nu vedeÈi cenzura Èi inchiziÈia din RomÃ¢nia, unde oameni, ca mine, de exemplu, eu am dosar penal pentru declaraÈii Èi opinii politice Ã®n Parlamentul European Èi Ã®n Parlamentul RomÃ¢niei.

DumneavoastrÄ acuzaÈi Iranul, dar nu acuzaÈi Israelul pentru genocid Èi crime Ã®mpotriva umanitÄÈii? SusÈineÈi Israelul Èi sunteÈi complici la genocidul de acolo de doi ani de zile? InstituiÈi doar sancÈiuni Èi vÄ Ã®ntreb: ce aÈi fÄcut cu sancÈiunile impuse ÈÄrilor pe care nu le suportaÈi? Am ajuns fÄrÄ economie, fÄrÄ industrie, fÄrÄ agriculturÄ Èi Europa cade.

Â 
Â Â 
Â Â 

(Koniec zgÅoszeÅ z sali)

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Jessika Roswall, Member of the Commission. â Madam President, honourable Members, Minister, thank you for the rich debate. I take note of the strong support for reimposing sanctions on Iran. EU sanctions are, as I said in my first intervention, targeted and designed to avoid negative consequences that would hit the Iranian people.

I also want to say that at the same time the European Union will remain engaged with all relevant parties, including Iran, in support of political and diplomatic efforts to find negotiated solutions to the Iranian nuclear issue. In this context I also want to say that we urge Iran to fully resume cooperation with the International Atomic Energy Agency without delay, in line with its legal obligations under the Treaty on the Non-Proliferation of Nuclear Weapons and its binding comprehensive safeguard agreements.

At the same time, we remain firmly committed to addressing all other issues or concerns with Iran. With that, I thank you for the debate.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Marie Bjerre, President-in-Office of the Council. â Madam President, honourable Members, Commissioner, thank you for the debate. Let me underline once more that a sustainable solution to the Iranian nuclear issues can only be achieved through diplomacy. But the ball is in Iran's court. Iran must engage in a meaningful way.

The proliferation risk remains profound and urgent. We express our full support for the International Atomic Energy Agency in its essential task of monitoring Iran's nuclear programme. To this end, we call on Iran to fully resume cooperation with the agency without delay. This is Iran's legal obligation under the Treaty on the Non-Proliferation of Nuclear Weapons.

At the same time, we remain firmly committed to addressing all other issues of concern with Iran.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â PrzewodniczÄca. â ZamykamÂ debatÄ.

OÅwiadczenia pisemne (art. 178)

Â 
Â Â 
Â 		Â 

Â Â Adam Bielan (ECR), in writing. â Lifting sanctions on Iran in 2015 under the Nuclear Deal was a fundamental mistake. Only 10 years later, sanctions had to be reimposed.

The Iranian regime has repeatedly proven that it cannot be trusted on nuclear weapons. Despite its commitments, Tehran has continued expanding its nuclear program while destabilizing the Middle East and threatening global security.

When President Trump warned us about Iranâs true intentions and withdrew from the Nuclear Deal, many in Europe laughed. Today, no one truly knows how close Iran is to developing a nuclear weaponâ¦and that uncertainty is the result of failed diplomatic illusions.

The Joint Comprehensive Plan of Action gave Tehran time, money, and legitimacy - and was a failure.

Iran must never be allowed to possess a nuclear weapon. A nuclear-armed Iran would endanger the world, while its regime continues to brutally repress women, minorities, and political opponents.

Only strength, not appeasement, can stop Iran.

Â 

17. Debate on cases of breaches of human rights, democracy and the rule of law (debate)

17.1. Arbitrary detention of EU citizens Javier MaraÃ±Ã³n Montero and David RodrÃ­guez Ballesta in Equatorial Guinea
Video of the speeches
Â 

Â Â PrzewodniczÄca. â Kolejnym punktem porzÄdku dziennego jest debata nad piÄcioma projektami rezolucji w sprawie arbitralnego zatrzymania obywateli UE Javiera MaraÃ±Ã³na Montero i Davida RodrÃ­gueza Ballesty w Gwinei RÃ³wnikowej (2025/2904(RSP)).

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Elena Nevado del Campo, autora. â SeÃ±ora presidenta, seÃ±orÃ­as, Javier MaraÃ±Ã³n y David RodrÃ­guez son dos de los ciudadanos espaÃ±oles detenidos de manera arbitraria en Malabo por un rÃ©gimen, el de Guinea Ecuatorial, considerado como una dictadura autoritaria caracterizada por graves violaciones de los derechos humanos, detenciones y encarcelamientos arbitrarios que ponen en peligro la vida con el uso de la violencia y torturas indiscriminadas.

Hoy queremos alzar la voz por todos ellos, por Javier y por David, por sus familias. Fueron detenidos a mediados de enero de 2025. Desde su captura han sido sometidos a condiciones inhumanas, y el pasado mes de abril fueron trasladados a la prisiÃ³n de Black Beach, conocida por su extrema brutalidad y la grave vulneraciÃ³n de los derechos de los prisioneros. Ambos fueron aislados y privados del contacto con sus abogados y sus familias.

El deterioro de su salud es alarmante âsobre todo la de Javier, que le ha llevado a una huelga de hambre obligadaâ y no han recibido atenciÃ³n mÃ©dica adecuada. La situaciÃ³n de ambos es insostenible y requiere una acciÃ³n urgente. La situaciÃ³n requiere una respuesta clara y firme por parte de la UniÃ³n Europea y de las autoridades espaÃ±olas. No podemos aceptar que ningÃºn ciudadano europeo sea privado arbitrariamente de su libertad.

La corrupciÃ³n sistemÃ¡tica del rÃ©gimen, sus vÃ­nculos con Rusia, la continua violaciÃ³n de los derechos humanos, hacen que no podamos mirar hacia otro lado y que hoy condenemos enÃ©rgicamente los reiterados casos de detenciones inaceptables y maltratos a los presos en Guinea Ecuatorial y exijamos la inmediata liberaciÃ³n de Javier Montero MaraÃ±Ã³n y de David RodrÃ­guez Ballesta.

Javier y David son nuestros compatriotas y como ciudadanos europeos deben ser defendidos con firmeza. No podemos ni debemos permitir que la injusticia prevalezca. Es hora de alzar la voz y de que el rÃ©gimen de Guinea Ecuatorial se responsabilice de estos abusos. Pedimos que Javier y David puedan estar a salvo, que vuelvan a abrazar a sus familias. Son ciudadanos europeos. Europa no puede ni debe permanecer indiferente ante la tortura y ante la injusticia. Por eso exigimos la inmediata liberaciÃ³n de Javier Montero MaraÃ±Ã³n y de David RodrÃ­guez Ballesta.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Laura BallarÃ­n Cereza, autora. â SeÃ±ora presidenta, hablamos hoy de dos ciudadanos espaÃ±oles, Javier MaraÃ±Ã³n Montero y David RodrÃ­guez Ballesta, que llevan meses detenidos en Guinea Ecuatorial en circunstancias muy preocupantes y sin cargos formales. EstÃ¡n en la prisiÃ³n de Black Beach en condiciones infrahumanas, sin acceso a familiares ni abogados, y su situaciÃ³n de salud es extremadamente delicada, sobre todo en el caso de Javier, que estÃ¡ en huelga de hambre.

Con este debate y con la ResoluciÃ³n de urgencia firmada y negociada por la mayorÃ­a de grupos de esta CÃ¡mara, el Parlamento Europeo no mira hacia otro lado y manda dos mensajes claros.: condenamos firmemente las condiciones de los presos en Guinea Ecuatorial en clara violaciÃ³n del Derecho internacional humanitario y pedimos a las autoridades ecuatoguineanas que tomen acciones inmediatas e incondicionales, en particular el levantamiento de la detenciÃ³n preventiva para asegurar el bienestar de Javier y de David y el respeto a su derecho a un juicio justo, al acceso a asistencia mÃ©dica y al asesoramiento jurÃ­dico.

Esperamos que la voz de este Parlamento Europeo llegue hasta Malabo y que sirva para aliviar el sufrimiento de estos ciudadanos espaÃ±oles y de sus familias.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Michal Wiezik, author. â Madam President, EU citizens Javier MaraÃ±Ã³n Montero and David RodrÃ­guez Ballesta were arbitrarily detained in Malabo in January 2005, in a prosecution that appears politically motivated and aimed at shifting responsibility away from the members of the regime. They are being held in a prison infamous for torture and inhumane conditions, without any guarantee of due process and a fair trial.

We in Renew Europe believe in a European Union built on the rule of law, due process, fair trial standards and humanitarian protections. At a time when these very principles are being challenged within and beyond our boundaries, we must act decisively to defend and strengthen them so that we never create space for criminal and dictatorial regimes to thrive.

Through this resolution, this House reaffirms its united commitment to the rule of law, human rights and the right to a fair trial: principles on which the European Union is built.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Antonio LÃ³pez-IstÃºriz White, en nombre del Grupo PPE. â SeÃ±ora presidenta, Javier MaraÃ±Ã³n Montero y David RodrÃ­guez Ballesta, dos ciudadanos europeos y espaÃ±oles que llevan meses detenidos arbitrariamente en Black Beach, una de las peores prisiones del continente africano en Guinea Ecuatorial, estÃ¡n privados de libertad, sin garantÃ­as procesales, sin acceso a sus abogados ni a sus familias.

Sus condiciones son inhumanas y su salud se deteriora gravemente. No se trata de un caso solo consular: es una cuestiÃ³n europea, porque cuando se vulneran los derechos de un ciudadano espaÃ±ol se estÃ¡n vulnerando los derechos de todos los europeos. Por eso pedimos la liberaciÃ³n inmediata de Javier y David y su acceso urgente a la atenciÃ³n mÃ©dica y a un proceso justo conforme al Derecho internacional.

Este Parlamento no puede permanecer en silencio. Nuestra credibilidad como UniÃ³n se mide por la defensa de nuestros valores y de nuestros ciudadanos, estÃ©n donde estÃ©n. Guinea Ecuatorial debe entender que la cooperaciÃ³n con la UniÃ³n Europea pasa por el respeto a la dignidad humana. Ãfrica necesita socios, no carceleros, y Europa debe seguir siendo la voz de los que no la tienen.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Hannes Heide, im Namen der S&D-Fraktion. â Frau PrÃ¤sidentin! Wir fordern die BehÃ¶rden Ãquatorialguineas auf, unverzÃ¼glich und bedingungslos MaÃnahmen zu ergreifen, um die unmenschlichen Haftbedingungen von Javier MaraÃ±Ã³n Montero und David RodrÃ­guez Ballesta zu beenden. Die beiden EUâBÃ¼rger sind seit dem 22. Januar im berÃ¼chtigten Black-Beach-GefÃ¤ngnis inhaftiert. Sie haben keine ausreichende medizinische Versorgung, eingeschrÃ¤nkten Zugang zu AnwÃ¤lten, isolierte Haft und kaum Kontakt zu ihren Familien. Javier MaraÃ±Ã³n Montero befindet sich aufgrund eines Hungerstreiks in kritischem Gesundheitszustand.

Wir fordern daher die vollstÃ¤ndige Respektierung ihrer Rechte: ein faires Verfahren, medizinische Versorgung, Rechtsbeistand, konsularischen Schutz und familiÃ¤re UnterstÃ¼tzung. Ihre Haftbedingungen verletzen Menschenrechte, schaden der internationalen Reputation Ãquatorialguineas und behindern die Zusammenarbeit mit der EU, die auf Rechtsstaatlichkeit und Achtung der Menschenrechte basiert. Ich appelliere an den EuropÃ¤ischen AuswÃ¤rtigen Dienst und die Mitgliedstaaten, alle diplomatischen Mittel zu ergreifen, sich fÃ¼r die Gefangenen einzusetzen, solche FÃ¤lle zu verhindern und den Schutz von EUâBÃ¼rgern weltweit zu stÃ¤rken.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Marta Temido (S&D). â Senhora Presidente, Senhora ComissÃ¡ria, quero expressar a minha profunda preocupaÃ§Ã£o com as condiÃ§Ãµes de detenÃ§Ã£o dos cidadÃ£os espanhÃ³is Javier MaraÃ±Ã³n Montero e David RodrÃ­guez Ballesta na GuinÃ© Equatorial.

Existem sÃ©rios indÃ­cios de violaÃ§Ã£o dos direitos fundamentais de defesa e de falta de transparÃªncia no processo judicial, o que suscita legÃ­timas dÃºvidas sobre o respeito pelo Estado de direito neste paÃ­s, estando os detidos sem acesso a defesa e em isolamento prisional desde abril.

O Sr. Javier MaraÃ±Ã³n Montero corre, aliÃ¡s, sÃ©rio risco de vida na sequÃªncia de uma greve de fome. Um ato desesperado que reflete a gravidade da situaÃ§Ã£o.

Ã essencial que as autoridades da GuinÃ© Equatorial garantam o bem-estar fÃ­sico e psicolÃ³gico destes e de todos os detidos, bem como o respeito integral pelas normas internacionais de justiÃ§a e direitos humanos.

Junto-me aqui aos apelos desta CÃ¢mara para que ambos os detidos tenham acesso imediato a uma defesa justa, a contacto regular com as famÃ­lias, a cuidados de saÃºde e ao necessÃ¡rio apoio consular.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Alvise PÃ©rez (NI). â SeÃ±ora presidenta, para poder defender ây estoy de acuerdo con la mayorÃ­a de compaÃ±eros que han intervenido hoy aquÃ­â los derechos fundamentales en Guinea Ecuatorial, tenemos que tener tambiÃ©n la capacidad de defender los derechos fundamentales en Europa. Por eso, cambio mi discurso porque me ha pasado algo gravÃ­simo, y os lo voy a contar tal cual, queridos compaÃ±eros y colegas europeos.

Ayer, transferÃ­ 106Â 000Â EUR a mi banco, el banco espaÃ±ol Arquia, con toda la documentaciÃ³n legal necesaria, para donar, como suelo hacer, el 100Â % de mi sueldo como eurodiputado. En la carta que acabo de recibir leo Â«Debemos finalizar la relaciÃ³n comercial con usted por ideologÃ­a polÃ­ticaÂ», no por un proceso judicial o una deuda, sino tan solo porque critico el sistema bancario espaÃ±ol, y a este banco espaÃ±ol no le gustan las ideas de un eurodiputado con mÃ¡s de 800Â 000 votos de los espaÃ±oles.

Si no hay derechos fundamentales y, por una cuestiÃ³n estrictamente polÃ­tica, todos los que estamos aquÃ­ podemos sufrir un bloqueo de una cuenta (y cito textualmente) Â«por cuestiones polÃ­ticasÂ», Â¿quÃ© defensa tenemos para expresarnos libremente en âse supone, en finâ el edificio de la soberanÃ­a popular del pueblo europeo?

Â Â PrzewodniczÄca. â Panie poÅle, zupeÅnie nie na temat. Mamy w tej chwili procedurÄ catch the eye. W ramach tej procedury mamy tylko jedno zgÅoszenie.

ZgÅoszenia z sali

Â 
Â Â

Â Â Diana Iovanovici ÅoÅoacÄ (NI). â DoamnÄ preÈedintÄ, Èi ca eurodeputat, Èi ca avocat, mÄ Ã®ntreb cum este posibil sÄ avem cetÄÈeni ai Uniunii Europene, de cÃ¢teva luni Ã®nchiÈi Ã®ntr-o Ã®nchisoare din Guineea EcuatorialÄ, care este o ÈarÄ extrem de micÄ din Africa CentralÄ, care a fost efectiv o colonie a Spaniei, iar noi pÃ¢nÄ acum nu am fÄcut nimic?

Cu tot respectul, dar Uniunea EuropeanÄ ce face pentru a-Èi apÄra cetÄÈenii? Èi atunci, dacÄ Uniunea EuropeanÄ nu e Ã®n stare nici mÄcar Ã®n faÈa Guineei Ecuatoriale sÄ Ã®Èi impunÄ respectul, puterea Èi legea, dumneavoastrÄ vorbiÈi de drepturi Èi libertÄÈi, ce sÄ mÄ mai mir de ce e Ã®n RomÃ¢nia? SÄ ÈtiÈi cÄ Ã®n RomÃ¢nia avem la fel oameni Ã®nchiÈi Ã®n Ã®nchisoare, pentru cÄ Ã®Èi permit sÄ spunÄ adevÄrul pe social media sau la televizor. Oameni politici cÄrora, ca Èi colegului anterior, li se interzice de cÄtre bancÄ sÄ mai aibÄ conturi.

Noi, oamenii politici, inclusiv eu, suntem duÈi la Ã®nchisoare pentru cÄ ne permitem sÄ spunem dreptul la liberÄ exprimare Èi opinie.

De cÃ¢nd am ajuns Ã®n Europa sÄ nu ne apÄrÄm nici cetÄÈenii, nici aici, nici dincolo?

Â 
Â Â 
Â Â 

(Koniec zgÅoszeÅ z sali)

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Jessika Roswall, Member of the Commission. â Madam President, honourable Members, the continuing restrictions of freedom and arbitrary arrests and detentions taking place in Equatorial Guinea confirmed the deterioration of the human rights situation in the country and also represent a source of serious concern.

Strengthening respect for the rule of law and promoting human rights remains a long-standing key priority for the European Union. We always emphasise that states must take active steps to protect human rights.

Unfortunately, impunity for human rights violations and abuses and corruption remain far too widespread. In Equatorial Guinea, we are deeply concerned about the prison conditions and ill treatment of and violence against detainees, and the use of torture, especially in prisons, continues to be reported and widespread. Prisons continue to be overcrowded and imprisonment conditions remain harsh.

The legal case against Spanish citizens Javier MaraÃ±Ã³n Montero and David RodrÃ­guez Ballesta should be properly investigated without further delay and without undue interference in the judiciary, in full respect of the right of defence and a fair trial. The authorities should ensure access to detainees to legal assistance and to contact with their families.

Exchange and progress on human rights, including prison conditions and arbitrary detentions are necessary for the incentivisation of our relations with the country. The EU encourages the authorities of Equatorial Guinea to make concrete progress on the recommendations of the recent Universal Periodic Review, and expresses its readiness to support these efforts.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â PrzewodniczÄca. â Zamykam debatÄ.

GÅosowanie odbÄdzie siÄ w czwartek 9 paÅºdziernika 2025 r.

Â 

17.2. Humanitarian and security situation in Haiti, in particular the rising power of criminal gangs and the recent massacre in Cabaret
Video of the speeches
Â 

Â Â PrzewodniczÄca. â Kolejnym punktem porzÄdku dziennego jest debata nad siedmioma projektami rezolucji w sprawie sytuacji humanitarnej i stanu bezpieczeÅstwa w Haiti, zwÅaszcza rosnÄcej siÅy gangÃ³w i niedawnego mordu ludnoÅci w Cabaret (2025/2905(RSP)).

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Reinhold Lopatka, author. â Madam President, dear colleagues, on 11Â September, not one month ago, in the Haitian town of Cabaret, the gang coalition Viv Ansanm perpetrated a brutal massacre of over 40 innocent people, among them children, elderly people and entire families. This is not an isolated event â in the last three years, 16Â 000 people lost their lives, and more than 1.3Â million people are displaced. Half of Haiti's population faces acute food insecurity. Hospitals, schools and courts have collapsed under gang domination. Sexual violence has become systematic.

What we are witnessing is the erosion of law and humanity itself. Haiti's suffering is a humanitarian catastrophe and a moral test for the whole international community. Europe cannot remain a spectator. We must act with determination, grounded in our values and guided by responsibility.

Firstly, we must restore justice and accountability. Gangs act because impunity reigns. The European Union should help rebuild Haiti's judicial capacity, training judges, investigators and prosecutors. And we have to support the investigation of these massacres and corruption. Without justice, there will be no stability.

Secondly, we must intensify humanitarian aid. The UN Humanitarian Response Plan remains gravely underfunded. Europe should step up: food assistance, healthcare and protection of those most in need; ensuring transparency, monitoring and secure access for humanitarian workers.

Thirdly, we must help re-establish security and the rule of law. The UNâmandated Gang Suppression Force, approved by the Security Council, must be staffed properly, equipped and financed. The European Union should support this effort politically and technically.

And so, coming to a conclusion, I think this massacre in Cabaret must serve as a turning point. By adopting this resolution, our Parliament sends a united and clear message: Europe stands with the people of Haiti, works against terror and works for security, justice and restoration of human dignity.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Giorgio Gori, autore. â Signora Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, ci sono tragedie umanitarie di cui si parla poco o non si parla affatto. Una di queste Ã¨ la tragedia in corso ad Haiti.

Qualche settimana fa, a Cabaret, le gang criminali hanno ucciso quaranta persone, tra cui bambini e intere famiglie. Sono sedici mila le persone uccise dal 2022. La violenza delle bande, con omicidi, sequestri e stupri, Ã¨ alimentata dal collasso delle istituzioni e del sistema giudiziario. Un milione e trecento mila persone sono senza casa. La metÃ  della popolazione ha fame. Gli aiuti umanitari sono ostacolati e il piano ONU Ã¨ cronicamente sottofinanziato.

Chiediamo quindi alla Commissione europea e agli Stati membri di agire con urgenza per garantire un immediato sostegno umanitario, fornendo cibo e farmaci innanzitutto.

Per sostenere la forza di soppressione delle gang e riformare la giustizia e per porre fine all'impunitÃ  sono necessarie sanzioni mirate contro i leader delle bande e va fermato il flusso di armi verso Haiti. La situazione Ã¨ gravissima e noi non possiamo voltarci dall'altra parte.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â MaÅgorzata Gosiewska, autorka. â Pani PrzewodniczÄca! Sytuacja humanitarna na Haiti jest tragiczna. Gangi kontrolujÄ dziÅ ponad 85% stolicy. TysiÄce ludzi ginie lub ucieka przed przemocÄ. W Cabaret doszÅo niedawno do brutalnego mordu ludnoÅci cywilnej. Przemoc osiÄgnÄÅa bezprecedensowy poziom. Gangi zabijajÄ, gwaÅcÄ, palÄ domy, szkoÅy, szpitale, siejÄc terror w caÅym kraju. Ponad milion osÃ³b zostaÅo przesiedlonych, a w obozach panuje gÅÃ³d i przemoc seksualna. PoÅowa mieszkaÅcÃ³w Haiti Å¼yje w dramatycznej biedzie bez wody, lekÃ³w i bezpieczeÅstwa.

W tym piekle jest jednak ÅwiateÅko nadziei: misje katolickie. Od 14 lat siostry Felicjanki w Jacmel prowadzÄ mobilnÄ przychodniÄ, szkoÅÄ szycia i program edukacyjny. DziÄki ich pracy i wsparciu darczyÅcÃ³w z Polski setki dzieci, a czÄsto teÅ¼ i dorosÅych majÄ dostÄp do codziennych posiÅkÃ³w, nauki, leczenia i edukacji. Polska zawsze w trudnych sytuacjach wspieraÅa Haiti. OsobiÅcie byÅam tam z pomocÄ medycznÄ i humanitarnÄ po tragicznym trzÄsieniu ziemi w 2010 r. Ale Haiti potrzebuje Åwiatowej mobilizacji. Potrzebne sÄ dziaÅania ONZ i wspÃ³lnoty miÄdzynarodowej, by zagwarantowaÄ bezpieczeÅstwo cywilÃ³w, dostarczyÄ Å¼ywnoÅÄ, wodÄ, leki, wspieraÄ kobiety i dzieci, a takÅ¼e odbudowaÄ struktury paÅstwa i edukacjÄ.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Helmut BrandstÃ¤tter, auteur. â Madame la PrÃ©sidente, Madame la Commissaire, chers collÃ¨gues, HaÃ¯ti nâest pas seulement confrontÃ© Ã  une crise. LâÃtat est dÃ©faillant, les institutions sâeffondrent et les grands criminels rÃ¨gnent dÃ©sormais en toute impunitÃ©.

Les massacres de Cabaret, oÃ¹ des vies innocentes ont Ã©tÃ© brutalement fauchÃ©es, ne sont pas une tragÃ©die isolÃ©e. Câest le symptÃ´me dâun pays oÃ¹ lâÃ©tat de droit a disparu et oÃ¹ la justice nâexiste plus.

Que faire? PremiÃ¨rement, la population en HaÃ¯ti a besoin de justice. Les auteurs des crimes doivent en assumer les consÃ©quences. DeuxiÃ¨mement, nous devons briser le cycle de la violence. TroisiÃ¨mement, nous devons soutenir le peuple haÃ¯tien. LâUnion europÃ©enne doit renforcer lâaide humanitaire dâurgence et garantir un accÃ¨s sÃ»r aux travailleurs humanitaires.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Catarina Vieira, author. â Madam President, colleagues, the suffering in Haiti does not make the news or our social media screens, but it should. Armed gangs, corruption and impunity have overtaken the country, driving fear and despair. More than 1Â million people are internally displaced and over half the population faces food insecurity. Women and girls are particularly at risk and sexual violence is widespread. Yet, instead of solidarity, the people of Haiti face rejection. USAID funding cuts have disrupted health services, including HIV treatments, while massive forced repatriation from the US and the Dominican Republic risks worsening the already dire socio-economic conditions.

We must send a clear message: these forced returns violate international human rights standards. To help Haiti move to a safer, more democratic future we must sanction those who are fuelling the violence, protect human rights defenders and support local civil society. It's time to work with Haiti on a strategy that combines humanitarian aid, development and inclusive political dialogue, recognising that military interventions alone have consistently failed. The people of Haiti deserve our empathy and our action â let us not hesitate to give them both now.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Tomasz Froelich, Verfasser. â Frau PrÃ¤sidentin! Eskalierende Gang-Gewalt, wirtschaftlicher Kollaps durch Korruption, millionenfache Vertreibung, Ã¼ber 5Â Millionen hungernde Haitianer und zudem eine vÃ¶llig fehlgeleitete USâ und UNâInterventionspolitik Ã¼ber Jahrzehnte. Haiti ist ein failed state, in dem kriminelle Gangs das Sagen haben. Unser Handlungsspielraum, um das zu Ã¤ndern, ist sehr begrenzt, denn wir sind dort eine raumfremde Macht. Die fast 20Â Millionen Euro Budgethilfe, die wir erst im Februar an Haiti Ã¼berwiesen haben, sind leider rausgeschmissenes Steuergeld, das nur korrupte Strukturen vor Ort stÃ¤rkt. Wir kÃ¶nnen nur eines tun: Akteure und Staaten in der Region anregen, enger zusammenzuarbeiten, um kriminellen Gangs die Ressourcen abzuwÃ¼rgen, etwa durch BekÃ¤mpfung des Drogenâ und des Menschenhandels.

Aber als moralische Instanz taugt dieses Parlament hier ganz bestimmt nicht. Denn wer linken Terror der âHammerbandeâ deckt, hat kein Recht, sich Ã¼ber BandenkriminalitÃ¤t auf Haiti aufzuregen. Wer sich zu Komplizen von Linksterroristen macht, braucht dem Rest der Welt nichts Ã¼ber Menschenrechte zu erzÃ¤hlen. Die linke HÃ¤lfte dieses Hauses und leider auch Teile der Christdemokratie haben gestern endgÃ¼ltig ihren moralischen Tiefpunkt erreicht.

Â 
Â Â

Â Â PrzewodniczÄca. â ChcÄ powiedzieÄ, Å¼e naruszyÅ pan regulamin Parlamentu, art. 10. W zwiÄzku z tym bardzo proszÄ o tym pamiÄtaÄ, Å¼e nie uÅ¼ywa siÄ fotografii i emblematÃ³w.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Leire PajÃ­n, en nombre del Grupo S&D. â SeÃ±ora presidenta, seÃ±ora comisaria, hay mÃ¡s de un millÃ³n y medio de personas desplazadas, y la mitad de la poblaciÃ³n sufre hambre. La tragedia de HaitÃ­ es un claro ejemplo de una crisis âde una tragediaâ profundamente olvidada, como lo son la de SudÃ¡n y otras muchas. Una crisis que, a pesar del sufrimiento humano que acarrea, no ocupa ni nuestro tiempo, ni nuestras agendas, ni mucho menos nuestros debates, porque no ocupa nuestros telediarios. Es por eso.

Y miren que al final ha tenido que ocurrir otra vez una atrocidad para que la comunidad internacional y este Parlamento hablemos de esta realidad. Y no serÃ¡ porque no lo hemos intentado. El pasado 11 de septiembre, en Cabaret, mÃ¡s de cuarenta personas fueron ejecutadas sumariamente vÃ­ctimas de una crueldad indescriptible a manos de las pandillas. HaitÃ­ hoy es el sÃ­ntoma mÃ¡s claro de que hemos relegado, ademÃ¡s, y dejado solas a las Naciones Unidas: solo el 10Â % de la financiaciÃ³n para eso.

Hay esperanza, pero tenemos que decirles a los Estados miembros que fortalezcan su cooperaciÃ³n y su compromiso. Y, sobre todo, necesitamos que la masacre de Cabaret no sea un nÃºmero mÃ¡s en las estadÃ­sticas, sino que despierte nuestras conciencias y nos obligue a actuar ya.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Murielle Laurent (S&D). â Madame la PrÃ©sidente, Madame la Commissaire, chers collÃ¨gues, il est des situations effroyables pour lesquelles les mots manquent, et HaÃ¯ti en fait partie. Comment qualifier la violence extrÃªme qui a abouti au massacre de Cabaret? Comment dÃ©crire les circonstances atroces dans lesquelles ont pÃ©ri des dizaines dâinnocents, y compris des enfants?

Mais ce nâest pas parce que les mots nous font dÃ©faut quâil faut se taire et, surtout, rester passifs. LâUnion a les moyens dâagir. Elle peut faire pression pour stopper ce cycle de violence, aider Ã  une transition juste et Ã  la poursuite des criminels pour briser enfin lâimpunitÃ©.

Je me rÃ©jouis quâHaÃ¯ti soit Ã  lâordre du jour de cette session plÃ©niÃ¨re et que notre Parlement adopte une rÃ©solution sur la situation dramatique de ce pays. Câest un signal important et attendu, mais cette rÃ©solution doit Ãªtre Ã  la hauteur et suivie dâactes concrets. LâUnion europÃ©enne doit sâengager concrÃ¨tement aux cÃ´tÃ©s du peuple haÃ¯tien, car il nây a pas de fatalitÃ©, il nây a que des renoncements politiques auxquels je refuse de me rÃ©soudre.

Â Â Rody Tolassy (PfE). â Madame la PrÃ©sidente, historiquement, HaÃ¯ti se trouve dans cette situation. Actuellement, les gangs pillent la sociÃ©tÃ©, la vie, lâÃ©conomie et tuent un pays tout entier. Mais il existe dâautres responsables de la situation catastrophique de ce pays, et cela ne date pas dâaujourdâhui.

Ã la suite du dÃ©clin Ã©conomique des annÃ©esÂ 50 aux annÃ©esÂ 80, dÃ» Ã  la corruption et Ã  la rÃ©pression par les dictateurs Duvalier, pÃ¨re et fils, certains, Ã  gauche, ont cru bon de rÃ©pondre Ã  la dÃ©tresse de ce pays en offrant comme unique solution lâimmigration. De la mÃªme faÃ§on quâils le font avec des pays dâAfrique, ils ont ainsi favorisÃ© une immigration qui, tout comme les gangs, pille la sociÃ©tÃ© et lâÃ©conomie.

Vous avez pillÃ© les hommes, les forces vives, tout ce qui fait HaÃ¯ti et ses habitants. Vous avez largement contribuÃ© Ã  la dÃ©stabilisation de ce pays. HaÃ¯ti a besoin dâaide pour retrouver la paix, de conserver ses forces vives et dâune vÃ©ritable politique de co-dÃ©veloppement oÃ¹ chaque HaÃ¯tien trouvera sa place dans son pays.

Â Â Diana Iovanovici ÅoÅoacÄ (NI). â DoamnÄ preÈedintÄ, da, este foarte interesant ceea ce se Ã®ntÃ¢mplÄ acolo. Colegul meu de la PatrioÈi are dreptate. ToatÄ lumea a contribuit la dezastrul din Haiti Èi nu i-a pÄsat nimÄnui.

Dar este foarte departe Èi nu vÄd cum vÄ veÈi putea implica, pentru cÄ orice sancÈiune, orice ameninÈare nu conteazÄ.

Ãn schimb, aÈi putea sÄ vÄ implicaÈi aici, Ã®n Uniunea EuropeanÄ, Ã®n RomÃ¢nia, unde avem, de asemenea, grupuri infracÈionale organizate care omoarÄ oameni Ã®n fiecare zi pe strÄzile RomÃ¢niei. Avem fetiÈe care sunt luate Èi prostituate. Avem nenumÄraÈi oameni bÄgaÈi Ã®n Ã®nchisoare pe nedrept.

Ãn loc ca poliÈia Èi jandarmeria sÄ ancheteze aceste cazuri, ei sunt puÈi de cÄtre ministrul de Interne Èi ministrul JustiÈiei sÄ Ã®nregistreze 24 din 24 de ore politicienii Èi parlamentarii Èi europarlamentarii Èi sÄ le facÄ dosare penale pentru orice opinie Èi pentru orice live pe care Ã®l fac.Â Mai Ã®ntÃ¢i, ocupaÈi-vÄ aici.

Èi apropo, douÄ secunde, azi e ziua Ursulei von der Leyen, sÄ-i spunem âLa mulÈi ani!â Èi lui Putin ieri, âLa mulÈi ani!â.

Â Â 
Â Â 

(Koniec zgÅoszeÅ z sali)

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Jessika Roswall, Member of the Commission. â MadamÂ President, honourable Members, the unrelenting violence perpetrated by armed gangs has led to a dire humanitarian crisis, with over 1.3Â million people internally displaced, half of whom are children.

The tragic toll of more than 3Â 000 lives lost since the beginning of the year underlines the urgent need for swift and decisive action. This tragic reality was most recently illustrated by the attack in the commune of Cabaret in September, which claimed at least 40Â lives â a horrible act that the EU delegation in Haiti and locally represented Member States were swift to condemn.

Equally devastating is the use of sexual violence by these gangs as a way to control and intimidate people. This makes the situation even worse, spreading fear and trauma among those affected.

Despite these challenges, there is a sign of hope.

In this context, we welcome the recent UN Security Council resolution reinforcing the Multinational Security Support Mission, whose numbers will be increased and whose financing will be facilitated. These resources will be strengthened through an assessed UN contribution, and a new UN logistics office will be established to ensure coordination and operational support. This new, stronger force will play a key role in fighting these armed gangs, hopefully bringing back peace and keeping people safe in Haiti.

We support the international effort and, with the intermediary of the Organization of American States, are financing the construction of forward operating bases that shall aid the security missions with a EURÂ 10Â million grant.

We have always supported Haiti and continue to do so, currently with more than EURÂ 300Â million in ongoing projects for development and humanitarian aid. For 2025 alone, EURÂ 39.5Â million has been set aside specifically for humanitarian needs in Haiti, including EURÂ 33Â million through our humanitarian partners on the ground and EURÂ 6.4Â million to ensure the functioning of the UNHAS helicopter service.

Humanitarian aid is essential, but not sufficient alone. A nexus approach, addressing all the components of this complex crisis â political, security, humanitarian and development, including the regional dimension with Dominican Republic â remains the only path forward for a durable solution. Massive investments in social protection are needed and access to food and basic services, health and water sanitation must be urgently re-established.

The crisis in Haiti does not just affect the country itself. It also has a significant effect across the entire Caribbean. Illegal arms and drug trafficking are fuelling gang activities, impacting not only the Caribbean, but also reaching Europe. This calls for a shared responsibility and it is something that we cannot ignore.

Â 
Â Â

Â Â PrzewodniczÄca. â Zamykam debatÄ.

GÅosowanie odbÄdzie siÄ w czwartek 9 paÅºdziernika 2025 r.

Â 

17.3. 10th anniversary of the detention of jailed Swedish publisher Gui Minhai in China
Video of the speeches
Â 

Â Â PrzewodniczÄca. â Kolejnym punktem porzÄdku dziennego jest debata nad szeÅcioma projektami rezolucji w sprawie 10. rocznicy zatrzymania i uwiÄzienia szwedzkiego wydawcy Gui Minhaia w Chinach (2025/2906(RSP)).

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Miriam Lexmann, author. â Madam President, ladies and gentlemen, we speak here today about Gui Minhai, a Swedish publisher abducted 10 years ago from his holiday home in Thailand by the Chinese intelligence forces. Through Gui's writing and publishing in Hong Kong, millions were able to reach books which were banned in mainland China.Â His case reminds us of the Chinese Government's long arm of repression against independent voices, against truth and against facts. Because of the Chinese communist regime, facilitating the free circulation of words and ideas is a crime.

Gui's case is not an isolated domestic matter. It is emblematic of a worrying practice of transnational repressions, disappearance of people for expressing freedom of thought and freedom of belief, and forced confessions, which are orchestrated during sham trials. This is how the Chinese regime suppresses its critics. And this is not only about the case of Gui Minhai â it's Ilham Tohti, Jimmy Lai, Panchen Lama, but also Ding Jiaxi, thousands of other political prisoners in China, millions of Uighurs who are held in concentration camps and labour camps, and many more suffer under the brutal regime of Beijing.

I grew up in the then communist regime in Czechoslovakia. These regimes do not play according to international rules. They are either exploiting them or violating them. We must finally understand this and act accordingly. Again and again, I call for a principled approach to the Chinese communist regime, for an approach in which human rights and fundamental freedoms are the central pillar of our relations, because respect for fundamental freedoms is non-negotiable.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Evin Incir, fÃ¶rfattare. â Fru talman! Tio Ã¥r sedan Gui Minhai levde i frihet. Tio Ã¥r sedan han trÃ¤ffade sina nÃ¤ra och kÃ¤ra. Tio Ã¥r sedan hans rÃ¶st â en rÃ¶st fÃ¶r yttrandefriheten â tystades av den kinesiska brutala regimen.Â Gui Minhais enda brott var att ge ut bÃ¶cker och vÃ¥ga berÃ¤tta saker som de Ã¤r. FÃ¶r det har han tillbringat ett decennium bakom galler utan en rÃ¤ttvis rÃ¤ttegÃ¥ng och utan konsulÃ¤rt tilltrÃ¤de.

Mina tankar gÃ¥r fÃ¶rst och frÃ¤mst till hans dotter Angela, som i tio lÃ¥nga Ã¥r har kÃ¤mpat fÃ¶r sin fars frihet. Hennes mod pÃ¥minner oss om att bakom varje politiskt fall finns en familj som vÃ¤ntar, hoppas och lider.

Vi upprepar vÃ¥rt krav pÃ¥ Gui Minhais omedelbara och villkorslÃ¶sa frigivning och pÃ¥ att Kina mÃ¥ste frige honom. Ingen mÃ¤nniska och ingen familj ska tvingas uthÃ¤rda en sÃ¥dan tystnad. Gui Minhai mÃ¥ste friges nu.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Beatrice Timgren, fÃ¶rfattare. â Fru talman! Gui Minhai har blivit en symbol fÃ¶r kampen fÃ¶r yttrandefrihet. Han vÃ¥gade kritisera Kommunistkina. FÃ¶r detta kidnappades han och fÃ¤ngslades â en attack mot Sveriges suverÃ¤nitet och vÃ¥ra grundlÃ¤ggande vÃ¤rderingar. VÃ¤nsterns utopi â Kommunistkina â visar Ã¥terigen sitt rÃ¤tta ansikte. DÃ¤r krossas frihet och tystas oliktÃ¤nkande.

ÃndÃ¥ fortsÃ¤tter EU med sin naiva globalism och sitt bugande fÃ¶r Peking. Vi blir allt mer beroende av denna blodrÃ¶da diktatur. VÃ¥ra industrier, vÃ¥ra jobb och vÃ¥ra vÃ¤rderingar offras pÃ¥ altaret fÃ¶r billigare handel. Detta Ã¤r inte bara ekonomiskt vansinne, det Ã¤r ett svek mot allt vi sÃ¤ger oss stÃ¥ fÃ¶r.

EU mÃ¥ste sluta krypa fÃ¶r Kina. Handelspolitiken mÃ¥ste sÃ¤tta Sveriges och Europas intressen fÃ¶rst. Inte stÃ¤rka en regim som fÃ¶rtrycker sitt folk och hotar vÃ¥r frihet. Det Ã¤r dags att tala klarsprÃ¥k och agera med ryggrad.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Helmut BrandstÃ¤tter, author. â Madam President, Commissioner, dear colleagues, October 17 marks ten years since the Swedish author and publisher Gui Minhai was kidnapped by Chinese agents while visiting Thailand. What is he accused of? Writing and selling books, which I think is very important, and especially when you write and sell books about the dictatorship.

And China is a dictatorship, and they prove to be a dictatorship, because they abducted him from another country. On this 10th anniversary, we demand his immediate and unconditional release, consular access and independent medical care.Â When I talk to Chinese diplomats these days, they tell me they want to have more trade, better relations with Europe. They tell us, you know, 'America is not your friend anymore. Why don't we work together?' Yes, but we do not want to work together with a brutal dictatorship. We do not want to work together with a country that just puts any critical author into jail.

So I am proud that this House, united, can stand behind this message. I don't see anyone from the right-wing extremists. We'll see. They are not here for this, which is also interesting. No one has to be imprisoned for exercising their freedom and their expression.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Alice Kuhnke, fÃ¶rfattare. â Fru talman! I en samtid dÃ¤r sanningen blir relativ och dÃ¤r fakta fÃ¶rvandlas till Ã¥sikter Ã¤r att stÃ¥ upp fÃ¶r yttrande- och pressfriheten â och fÃ¶r dess fÃ¶rsvarare â det viktigaste vi kan gÃ¶ra.Â Gui Minhai Ã¤r en fÃ¶rsvarare av frihet, en sanningssÃ¤gare, en pionjÃ¤r och en symbol fÃ¶r kampen mot auktoritÃ¤rt fÃ¶rtryck. Han Ã¤r ocksÃ¥ svensk medborgare, och i Ã¥r Ã¤r det tio Ã¥r sedan han kidnappades och fÃ¤ngslades av den kinesiska staten.

FÃ¶r varje rÃ¶st som diktaturen fÃ¶rsÃ¶ker tysta mÃ¥ste vi hÃ¶ja vÃ¥ra Ã¤nnu mer. Och fÃ¶r varje bok som diktaturen fÃ¶rbjuder mÃ¥ste det fria ordet fÃ¶rsvaras med Ã¤nnu stÃ¶rre kraft.Â VÃ¥rt gemensamma budskap Ã¤r tydligt. Gui Minhai mÃ¥ste omedelbart och ovillkorligt friges.

Â 

Â Â Jonas SjÃ¶stedt, fÃ¶rfattare. â Fru talman! Den 17 oktober Ã¤r det tio Ã¥r sedan den svenska medborgaren och fÃ¶rlÃ¤ggaren Gui Minhai kidnappades och fÃ¶rdes till Kina. Sedan dess Ã¤r han politisk fÃ¥nge. Han fÃ¥r inte trÃ¤ffa svenska diplomater. Han nekas kontakt med sitt eget barn och sin egen familj. Vi vet inte ens var i Kina Gui Minhai sitter fÃ¤ngslad.

IÂ morgon kommer vi i stor enighet i det hÃ¤r parlamentet att krÃ¤va att han omedelbart friges. Kina vill nu ha bÃ¤ttre kontakter med Europeiska unionen och nÃ¤rmare samarbete med Europaparlamentet. Frige dÃ¥ Gui Minhai. Visa att ni menar allvar. Visa att ni vill ha bÃ¤ttre fÃ¶rbindelser med Europeiska unionen och med Sverige.

Tio Ã¥r i fÃ¥ngenskap. Det Ã¤r dags, dags att Gui Minhai fÃ¥r resa hem â hem till Sverige, hem till sin dotter Angela.

(Koniec zgÅoszeÅ z sali)

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Jessika Roswall, Member of the Commission. â Madam President, honourable Members, ten years ago, the Swedish citizen Gui Minhai disappeared in Thailand. Several months later, he resurfaced in China. In a statement on China's state television, he claimed that he had returned voluntarily to China.

Gui Minhai was sentenced to two years and released in October 2017. He was re-arrested in 2018 and, two years later, in February 2020, sentenced to ten years' imprisonment, accused of providing intelligence for foreign powers.

Until today, the exact whereabouts of Gui Minhai are not known. His family has been unable to contact him in prison. There is no reliable information concerning his health condition. Moreover, in a clear contravention of the Vienna Convention on Consular Relations, Gui Minhai's rights to consular assistance were not respected. The Chinese authorities have denied all requests for consular access, claiming that he renounced his Swedish citizenship, whereas Sweden maintains that he is still a Swedish citizen, and thus also an EU citizen.

Gui Minhai is a strong proponent of freedom of speech and media freedom. He has authored numerous books expressing his views on Chinese politics, and founded a publishing house in Hong Kong.

We now know that his work has come at a high cost for him and his family, as Evin Incir and also Jonas SjÃ¶stedt talked about. His detention is symbolic of the Chinese Government's systematic suppression of fundamental freedoms and its concerning crackdown on human rights defenders, lawyers, journalists and academics.

We have consistently voiced our grave concern about the human rights situation in China and resolutely called for the unconditional release of all those detained for peacefully exercising their fundamental freedoms and human rights. We reiterate our calls on China to release Gui Minhai, as repeatedly put forward at the EU-China Human Rights Dialogue, in high-level meetings with Chinese interlocutors, as well as in public statements, including at the UN Human Rights Council. We further urge China to respect Gui Minhai's right to consular assistance and ensure contact with his family.

Allow me to assure Parliament that we will continue to express its concerns about the serious human rights situation in China, and to urge China to abide by its obligations under international and national law.
